rubrics_scientific.d2: Cell proliferation and infiltration dynamics align with pathophysiological principles: the sequence and timing of mitotic phases are appropriate; daughter cell adhesion and clonal expansion are authentic; the pathways of basement membrane disruption, stromal infiltration, and interactions with the extracellular matrix and vascular walls are scientifically substantiated.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Cell membrane and subcellular structure representation: The bilayer lipid membrane exhibits a semi-transparent texture with reasonable thickness and curvature; membrane receptors and adhesion molecules display heterogeneous distribution; the scale and spatial relationships of the nucleus, cytoskeleton, mitochondria, and other organelles are realistic.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Accuracy of Tumor Microenvironment and Intercellular Interactions: The orientation and density of fibrillar extracellular matrix accurately reflect their scientifically established effects on migration; the vascular network exhibits appropriate branching patterns, endothelial continuity, and alignment with blood flow direction; chemotaxis, recognition, and effector functions of immune cells, as well as tumor immune evasion mechanisms, are consistent with current literature.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Consistency between Microscopic Imaging and Biophysical Principles: The imaging resolution, depth of field, and contrast mechanisms correspond appropriately to the employed microscopy technique; molecular and cellular movements adhere to diffusion, osmosis, and fluid dynamics without exhibiting causeless instantaneous displacement, physically implausible optical artifacts, or super-resolution details in low-magnification images.

rubrics_visual.d1: High image resolution with finely detailed depiction of microscopic features; clear delineation of cell membrane textures, cellular morphology edges, fibrous matrix, and vascular structures with distinct layering; local magnifications maintain readability.
rubrics_visual.d2: The material and subsurface translucency are realistically rendered; the subsurface scattering, micro-refraction, and thickness variations of the double lipid bilayer are appropriately represented, with clear and consistent contrast between the materials of healthy tissue and immune cells.
rubrics_visual.d3: Lighting and depth of field are handled naturally; the directionality and softness of microscopic illumination are appropriate; translucent overlay layers exhibit clear stratification; volumetric scattering and shadow transitions are smooth; color saturation and contrast are balanced, with an overall unified tonal harmony.
rubrics_visual.d4: Animation smoothness and biomechanical coherence; stable rhythm in cell division, migration, adhesion, and interactions; continuous deformation without abrupt changes; camera movement appropriately matched to the microscopic scale, with no stuttering or unintended shaking.
rubrics_visual.d5: Artifacts and noise are well controlled; defects such as aliasing, flickering, banding, compression blockiness, excessive sharpening, or smear-like motion blur are not apparent, and translucent regions exhibit no ghosting or edge halos.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Under microscopic observation, the core scenario is the complete presentation of squamous cell carcinoma formation, proliferation, and invasion as the main trajectory within a single head and neck tissue environment.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Accurately represent key cellular components and interactions: the semi-transparent bilayer lipid membrane texture with irregular morphology, dynamics of cellular mutation and division, and antagonistic interactions with healthy cells and various immune cell types.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Restoration of microenvironmental details and tissue contrast: fibrous extracellular matrix texture, complex vascular network architecture, and clear spatial relationships and contrasts between healthy tissue and tumor regions.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Compliance with Visual and Technical Specifications: Vivid colors, realistic lighting and depth of field, semi-transparent overlays illustrating cellular processes, high-magnification microscopic perspectives with clear and stable imagery, and conformity to the specified duration and pacing of shots.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Avoid violating contextual constraints and thematic consistency: introducing irrelevant macro elements, switching across multiple organizational settings, diluting the primary theme, or omitting core dynamic factors.

任务: Dynamic lines of fluorescently labeled molecules flickering inside cancer cells, interweaving and interacting within the cellular microenvironment, displaying vivid colors and smooth motion, highlighting the fine textures of cellular structures, showcasing the spatial distribution and dynamic changes of molecular populations, with clear imagery and rich colors, suitable for a 5-second short video.
具体要求: Create a stunning microscopic world video capturing a surreal journey through the complex landscape of cancer cells, focusing on the forest of flickering lines representing different molecules within these cells. Observe fluorescent molecular labels illuminated by advanced confocal microscopy as they map out a vivid molecular "landscape" over tumor microregions, providing unique visual insights into the spatial heterogeneity of cancer tissues. This dynamic scene showcases the captivating dance and interactions of these molecules, with vivid colors and motion within the cellular environment emphasizing the significance of spatial omics in cancer research. From the iridescent glow of molecular clusters to the intricate textures of cellular structures, every detail should be captured with exceptional clarity and resolution, immersing the audience in this microscopic marvel. Smooth motion and continuously shifting hues must reflect the changes and adaptations of intrinsic patterns, driven by the symphony of molecular activity within the tumor. The video should highlight the importance of innovative cancer molecular mapping approaches, with each frame bearing witness to pioneering efforts in leveraging these visualization techniques to develop more effective diagnostic and therapeutic strategies.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Accurate representation of cellular substructures and scale relationships: the morphology and relative sizes of the nucleus, cell membrane, cytoplasm, and cytoskeleton conform to biological norms, and the spatial extent of fluorescent signals corresponds to the resolution of the optical imaging technique used.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Molecular kinetics and interaction dynamics are consistent and plausible: the proportions, velocities, and trajectory continuity of diffusion, restricted diffusion, and directional transport along the cytoskeletal framework align with the intracellular physical environment; displacements within a 5-second time window correspond appropriately to the acquisition frame rate.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Authenticity of fluorescence imaging and photophysical characteristics: fluorescence intermittency, photobleaching, point spread function blurring, and spatiotemporal features introduced by sampling and z-stack scanning are consistent with the employed imaging principles (e.g., confocal microscopy), with accurate spectral channel separation and registration.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Spatial distribution and spatial omics expression reliability: molecular clusters, gradients, and heterogeneity patterns are consistent with established biological principles; colocalization and mutual exclusivity patterns exhibit statistical concordance; 3D/2D localization and layer registration demonstrate coherent alignment.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Penalty for exaggerated or misleading visual effects: treating super-resolution or electron microscopy–level details as results of current optical imaging, excessive sharpening and halo enhancement, erroneous color mapping imparting nonexistent biological significance, or extensive synchronous flickering and other supraphysical phenomena.

rubrics_visual.d1: High image clarity and fine detail resolution, with sharply distinguishable cellular structural textures and fluorescent molecular lines, and clearly defined hierarchical relationships.
rubrics_visual.d2: Excellent color performance and dynamic range; fluorescent color saturation and contrast are natural; smooth tonal transitions; color layering effectively highlights spatial differences.
rubrics_visual.d3: The animation demonstrates high smoothness and temporal consistency; molecular line flickering is minimal, and motion trajectories are coherent and stable. Rhythm control is well-balanced, with no stuttering or unnecessary jitter.
rubrics_visual.d4: The 3D modeling and material texture are realistic, with precise morphology of cells and molecular clusters, clearly defined scale hierarchies, and natural rendering of translucency and microstructural details.
rubrics_visual.d5: Proper organization of lighting and depth of field, harmonious coordination of volumetric light and fluorescent glow, clear guidance through shadows and focal points, enhancing spatial distribution and sense of depth.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Comprehensively presents the core scenarios of the cancer cell microenvironment and the narrative of a transcendent journey, focusing on the flickering dynamic lines formed by fluorescently labeled molecules, and reflecting their intertwined interactions and spatial distributions.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Accurate restoration of key biological elements and details: fine textures of cellular structures, iridescent glow of molecular clusters, population dynamics, and spatial heterogeneity of tissue organization
rubrics_instruction.d3: Compliance with technical specifications and presentation requirements: high definition and high resolution, rich colors with tonal variations according to intrinsic patterns, smooth motion, duration approximately 5 seconds.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Unified microscopic perspective and microscopic imaging style: utilizing confocal visualization and lighting effects, with camera movement that conveys continuity of exploratory traversal while maintaining consistent cellular scale.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Major Deductions for Critical Violations: Absence of dynamic scintillating lines or fluorescent molecules, scenes deviating from the cancer cell microenvironment, blurred images or monotonous coloration, significant discrepancy in duration, inconsistent viewing angles and styles.

任务: Dynamic clusters of dopamine-producing neurons in the brain's microscopic environment emit electrical impulses and release dopamine molecules, highlighting the details of the neuronal membrane, synaptic connections, axons, and dendrites. The movement of dopamine molecules within the synaptic cleft is depicted, vividly presenting the microscopic process of neural communication with vivid colors and fine structures, suitable for a 5-second microscopic world video.
具体要求: Create a stunning microscopic world video that vividly portrays a dynamic cluster of dopamine-producing neurons as the main subject, intricately embedded within a finely detailed brain tissue environment. In this scene, demonstrate neurons actively firing electrical impulses and releasing dopamine, symbolizing the key biological processes affected in Parkinson's disease. Emphasize the captivating complex textures and vivid colors of the neuronal membrane, synaptic connections, and neurotransmitters, showcasing the complexity of neural communication. Highlight the intricate structures of axons and dendrites, detailing each tiny vesicle and synapse, while capturing the flickering movement of dopamine molecules within the synaptic cleft. This vivid depiction should underscore the urgency of medical research and call for the necessity of finding a cure.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Accurate morphology and topological relationships of neurons and synaptic ultrastructure: the relative positions and scales of the soma, axon, dendrites, presynaptic terminal, synaptic cleft, and postsynaptic membrane are appropriate, with synaptic vesicles localized in the presynaptic region.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Neural signal conduction mechanism is accurate: the action potential propagates unidirectionally along the axon, terminal depolarization triggers Ca2+-dependent exocytosis of synaptic vesicles, dopamine diffuses across the synaptic cleft and binds to receptors, followed by reuptake or degradation, with the process demonstrating logical coherence.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Molecular and ionic motions adhere to physico-chemical principles: dopamine molecules predominantly exhibit Brownian diffusion, with movement velocities and trajectories that are random and influenced by viscous environments and concentration gradients, without exhibiting directional ejection or synchronized drift that violate diffusion and conservation of mass.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Scale and Temporal Sequence Distortion Penalty: Enlarging dopamine molecules to a scale approaching that of synaptic vesicles or the synaptic cleft thickness, or representing millisecond-level synaptic transmission as slow structural deformations, resulting in confusion of temporal order and causal relationships.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Deduction for errors in key structural and signaling representation: visualizing electrical signals as beams/currents propagating directly across the synapse; localizing receptors on the presynaptic membrane or distributing synaptic vesicles on the postsynaptic side; or confusing the boundaries between presynaptic and postsynaptic structures.

rubrics_visual.d1: High image clarity with clearly distinguishable microscopic structural details; clean edges and stable texture without blurring.
rubrics_visual.d2: Strong animation smoothness and physical coherence; seamless and jitter-free integration of action potential propagation, synaptic release, and molecular motion rhythms.
rubrics_visual.d3: High accuracy in 3D modeling and realistic material rendering; consistent geometric scale; credible response of translucent and liquid-phase materials; stable surface details.
rubrics_visual.d4: Natural handling of lighting and depth of field; soft shadows, volumetric scattering, and bokeh exhibit smoothness; illumination direction and intensity are consistent and flicker-free.
rubrics_visual.d5: Color management and contrast control are properly executed; colors are vivid yet true to the original, with clear hierarchical differentiation, while avoiding oversaturation and color cast.

rubrics_instruction.d11: Comprehensively present the core scenario and theme: focus on dynamic clusters of dopamine-producing neurons, embedded within the intricate brain microenvironment, emphasizing the microscopic processes of neural communication.
rubrics_instruction.d22: Coverage of Key Structures and Elements: Clearly and distinctly present core structures such as the neural membrane, synaptic connections, axons, and dendrites, including the movement of vesicles and specified neurotransmitter molecules within the synaptic cleft.
rubrics_instruction.d33: Dynamic Mechanisms and Temporal Sequence Representation: Continuously depicting processes such as action potential firing, neurotransmitter release, and molecular diffusion/reuptake, thereby reflecting a causally linked and rhythmically coherent dynamic change.
rubrics_instruction.d44: Consistency between Perspective and Technical Specifications: Employ a stable microscopic viewpoint with focused subject emphasis, vivid coloration, and fine structural detail; ensure image clarity aligns with shot pacing and comply with the specified duration requirement (e.g., 5 seconds).
rubrics_instruction.d55: Alignment with Scientific Context and Communication Objectives: The content expression is consistent with the medical research context, emphasizing the urgency of the study and the science communication intent, while avoiding visual symbols or narratives that deviate from the central theme.

任务: In the microscopic world, vividly colored single human bone marrow cells are prominently displayed, with complex molecular structures inside the cells dynamically flickering, symbolizing active imaging of over 5,000 genes. The background features an ethereal bone marrow microenvironment, where cutting-edge spatial technology presents the intricate interactions between the cells and surrounding molecules. The visuals are richly detailed, emphasizing dynamic changes in cellular textures and molecular connections, suitable for a 5-second short video.
具体要求: Create a stunning microscopic world video showcasing the complex molecular landscape of human bone marrow, with the central focus on diverse individual cells, each uniquely highlighted in vivid colors to represent imaging of over 5,000 genes. The scene is set in an ethereal domain, where advanced spatial technology reveals astonishing insights brought by these molecular visuals, uncovering intricate internal operations and connections that could redefine our understanding of myeloma development and dissemination. As viewers immerse themselves in this extraordinary visual journey, they will witness a dazzling display of the most complex textures and details at the microscopic level, from flickering structural patterns within cells to the coordinated dynamic interactions of genes within the bone marrow environment. This detailed portrayal underscores the transformative potential of WEHI research, paving new avenues for myeloma treatment tailored to each patient's unique genetic profile, thereby laying the groundwork for groundbreaking advances in medical science.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Accuracy of the structural and scale relationships between bone marrow cells and their microenvironment: the cell morphology, nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio, cell diameter, and the relative size and spatial organization of capillary sinusoids, stromal cells, and extracellular matrix conform to human bone marrow histology.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Biological localization and mechanisms of gene activity visualization are correctly identified: transcription occurs within the nucleus or chromatin environment, mRNA is transported through the nuclear pore complex, translation takes place on cytoplasmic ribosomes, and the concepts of gene, protein, and cellular markers are not confused.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Molecular and intercellular interactions conform to physicochemical and cell biological principles: Brownian motion and constrained diffusion, binding-dissociation kinetics, molecular crowding effects, and the spatial proximity of receptor-ligand/signaling pathways are represented appropriately.
rubrics_scientific.d4: The dynamic time scales and event frequencies are appropriate and consistent with a 5-second observation window: the rates and durations of fluorescence flickering, expression bursts, transport, and interactions fall within known biological ranges, avoiding extensive synchronization or supra-physiological speeds.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Scientific Consistency of Imaging and Color Coding: Spectral channels and resolution fall within the realistic imaging capabilities; multi-color mappings clearly correspond to gene/molecular categories or expression levels, with no gene signals localized extracellularly or contradicting known localization patterns.

rubrics_visual.d1: Image clarity and precise presentation of microscopic details, with a prominent central subject; clear cell and molecular boundaries; well-defined internal texture layers; discernible fine structures without excessive sharpening.
rubrics_visual.d2: Color representation is scientifically accurate and distinctive, vivid without color bleeding; hue separation between channels and categories is clear, with natural saturation and contrast, avoiding color banding and color cast.
rubrics_visual.d3: Animation exhibits smoothness and excellent temporal coherence; micro-movements and flicker rhythms remain stable and interpretable; trajectories are smooth without jitter, stuttering, or extraneous motion blur; rhythm is clear within short-duration segments.
rubrics_visual.d4: High accuracy in 3D modeling and realistic material rendering; geometrically accurate representation of the cell membrane, cytoplasm, and molecular connections; credible depiction of translucent, anisotropic, and luminescent materials; no interpenetration or Z-fighting artifacts.
rubrics_visual.d5: Light and spatial layers are naturally unified; volumetric lighting is harmonized with ambient lighting; subsurface scattering and specular highlights are appropriately controlled; depth of field and atmospheric effects enhance three-dimensionality without obscuring critical information.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Accurate depiction of the core subject and scene setting: using the microscopic world of human bone marrow as the stage, with a clear central focus on one or multiple single cells; vivid coloration is employed to represent gene activity information and to highlight the subject.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Comprehensive demonstration of dynamic molecular processes and interactions: the flickering variations of complex intracellular structures, the dynamic evolution of cellular textures and molecular connections, as well as the detailed and coherent presentation of interactions with the surrounding microenvironment.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Key elements are thoroughly covered: the ethereal bone marrow microenvironment ambiance, the application of advanced spatial visualization language, and the symbolic representation of vast active gene populations (without the need for individual display).
rubrics_instruction.d4: Technical and shot specifications compliance: total duration approximately 5 seconds; shot composition and framing remain stable with focus on the subject; details are clearly discernible; information density and pacing are balanced to avoid overload.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Instruction Deviation Penalty: Ambiguous subject or deviation from bone marrow cells, lack of dynamic molecular interactions, background style inconsistent with the setting, significant deviation in duration, and color coding disconnected from gene active expression.

任务: In the microscopic world of colorectal cancer, cancer cells and immune cells dynamically interact within the cytoplasmic environment. Cancer cells rapidly proliferate while immune cells strive to suppress their spread. The background features fibrous tissue structures, with cell surfaces covered by glossy membrane layers, highlighting the morphology and intense combat state of both cell types, showcasing the tense confrontation and microscopic texture between them.
具体要求: Produce a captivating and emotionally resonant video exploring the enigmatic microscopic world of cancer cells, with a focus on the complexity of colorectal cancer. Depict the active and dynamic interactions among malignant cells as they rapidly divide and invade their surroundings, contrasted sharply by the diligent efforts of immune cells to inhibit their dissemination. This scientific ballet is set against the backdrop of human internal structures, presenting intricate and delicate textures: fibrous, reticular tissue fibers, glossy membrane-bound cellular layers, and flowing, almost ethereal cytoplasm. Emphasize the profound contrast between the destructive power of cancer cells and the resilient defense mechanisms of the immune response, capturing their details with precision. The video should convey the severity yet hopeful nature of cellular combat, inspiring awe and underscoring the critical importance of cancer research in overcoming these microscopic adversaries and transforming countless lives.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Accuracy of organizational hierarchy and cellular morphology: The hierarchical relationship among intestinal epithelium, basement membrane, and stroma is clearly defined, with the fibrous network architecture well-demarcated. Cancer cells exhibit loss of polarity, increased nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio, and characteristic disruption of glandular structures. The glossiness and texture of the cell membrane/glycocalyx are represented with scientific accuracy.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Accuracy of cancer cell growth and invasion dynamics: mitotic phase sequence and relative timing are appropriate; clonal expansion is coherent; invasion manifests as basement membrane breach followed by remodeling/degradation of the extracellular matrix pathways.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Correct immune-tumor interaction mechanisms: effector T/NK cells form immunological synapses and mediate cytotoxicity; phagocytes perform phagocytosis and antigen presentation; cytokine gradients drive chemotaxis; expression of immunosuppressive and immune evasion signals is biologically consistent.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Scale and temporal mapping rationality: size proportions and densities of cells, nuclei, organelles, and fibrous matrix are consistent; motility speeds, diffusion distances, and cytoplasmic/interstitial viscosities correspond appropriately; temporal compression or dilation preserves event sequencing and causality.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Microscopic physical and fluidic environmental consistency: Cellular motility follows low Reynolds number, viscosity-dominated dynamics, with credible attachment-traction-deformation cycles; molecular/particle behavior exhibits Brownian and confined diffusion; mechanical responses of interstitial fluid flow and fibrous networks are plausible.

rubrics_visual.d1: Image clarity and microscopic detail presentation are finely rendered, with fibrous tissue textures, the glossiness of cell membranes, and the granular layers of the cytoplasm distinctly distinguishable.
rubrics_visual.d2: Color reproduction and contrast control are natural, with warm and cool tonal gradations and saturation levels that support the emotional expression of tense confrontation without excessive distortion.
rubrics_visual.d3: The animation is smooth with biomechanically plausible movements, and the trajectories and rhythms of cell division, invasion, and immune interception are coherent and free of stuttering.
rubrics_visual.d4: Realistic modeling and texturing, with accurate cell morphology proportions, and consistent surface membrane, cytoplasmic fluidity, and tissue fiber material properties.
rubrics_visual.d5: Light and volume rendering appear natural, with balanced soft shadows, volumetric scattering, and depth of field transitions, enhancing micro-spatial layering and subject prominence.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Comprehensively present the dynamic interplay between cancer cells and immune cells within the colorectal cancer microenvironment, encompassing the core processes of rapid cancer cell proliferation and the expansion of immunosuppression.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Accurate reproduction of the microenvironment and texture: flowing cytoplasm, fibrous/reticular tissue background, glossy membrane layer on the cell surface, and clear morphological presentation.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Highlight the distinct contrast and continuous dynamics between destruction and defense, emphasizing the rhythm and tension of invasion, propagation, and immune containment.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Utilize a consistent microscopic/microstructural perspective aligned with internal human anatomical characteristics, ensuring that framing and composition avoid macroscopic or irrelevant elements, thereby complying with the technical specifications stipulated by the task.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Convey a solemn yet hopeful emotional tone, balancing scientific accuracy with popular science narration, to enhance immersion and resonance.

任务: Under the microscope, the malignant Plasmodium falciparum attempts to escape from the red blood cell but is firmly immobilized by a novel antimalarial compound. The footage highlights the cellular details and surface textures of both the red blood cell and the parasite, demonstrating the structural changes induced by the compound and capturing the critical dynamics of the parasite being halted, reflecting its potential in treating drug-resistant malaria.
具体要求: Create an engaging microscopic world video showcasing the profound effects of a novel antimalarial compound developed by researchers from Australia and the United States. The central scene depicts the capture process: Plasmodium falciparum, the most lethal malaria parasite species, immobilized within the red blood cell by the innovative compound. Illustrate the parasite’s dynamic attempt to escape its host red blood cell—a critical stage in the malaria infection cycle—being powerfully inhibited by the compound, ultimately leading to parasite death. Throughout the video, emphasize the intricate cellular details and surface textures of the malaria parasite and red blood cells, presenting vivid imagery of the structural alterations induced by the compound. Highlight the stark contrast between healthy red blood cells and the visually distinctive, destructive parasite trapped within, capturing the compound’s breakthrough capability in targeting previously unexplored parasitic pathways and demonstrating its potential to overcome challenges in drug-resistant malaria treatment.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The morphology, relative size, and scale proportions of both cells and parasites conform to microbiological standards; erythrocytes exhibit a biconcave disc shape without a nucleus, and the volume ratios of parasites at various developmental stages to the diameter of erythrocytes are appropriate.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Accuracy of Key Physiological Processes and Temporal Sequence: The membrane rupture sequence during the parasite egress phase (parasitophorous vacuole membrane rupture preceding erythrocyte membrane rupture) and the duration on the order of seconds to minutes, with dynamics consistent with energetic and diffusion constraints.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Hierarchical organization of membrane structures and surface ultrastructure are accurately presented: clear delineation of parasitophorous vacuole membrane, erythrocyte membrane, and parasite membrane boundaries; surface texture differences between healthy and infected erythrocytes (e.g., roughness/protrusions) correspond with known pathological alterations; compound-induced deformation and stiffening effects are appropriately manifested.
rubrics_scientific.d4: The compound’s mechanism of action and localization are scientifically rational: molecular transmembrane entry and subcellular distribution conform to diffusion/transport principles; inhibition of efflux-related proteases or signaling pathways is demonstrated through observable structural and kinetic changes; unknown mechanisms are described using neutral terminology.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Points will be deducted for visual elements that contradict fundamental biological facts: such as adding nuclei or mitochondria to red blood cells, incorrectly depicting unrelated microorganisms or organelle structures, or representing drugs as macroscopic energy beams or causing instantaneous whole-cell solidification, among other unreasonable effects.

rubrics_visual.d1: Image clarity and high levels of microstructural detail, with clear edges of key structures and distinguishable surface textures and functional interfaces.
rubrics_visual.d2: Key dynamic performance is coherent and credible; the pacing of obstructed and deformed movements is natural, with smooth and seamless motion interpolation.
rubrics_visual.d3: The rendering of light and shadow as well as volumetric effects is natural; microscopic illumination, translucency and subsurface scattering, depth of field, and focal shift are controlled stably with clear layering.
rubrics_visual.d4: High fidelity in 3D modeling and material realism, with well-defined geometry and topology details; material parameters consistently correspond to structural variations; absence of mesh distortion or interpenetration.
rubrics_visual.d5: Color and contrast are scientifically controlled, with clear chromatic distinction between the subject and background; saturation and contrast appear natural, enhancing the readability of structural differences.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Under microscopic observation, the complete core process of the parasite attempting to egress from the host erythrocyte but being firmly immobilized and ultimately killed by the novel compound is accurately demonstrated.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Highlight the cellular structures and surface textures of erythrocytes and parasites, clearly demonstrating the morphological and structural changes as well as visual contrasts induced by the compound’s action.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Accurately capture critical dynamics and time series, presenting the continuous progression and key moments from escape attempts and impeded stalls to resolution.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Meets the requirements of microscopic perspective and technical specifications, with appropriate magnification, stable focus and depth of field, and lighting and composition that emphasize the contrast between healthy red blood cells and trapped parasites.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Convey the scientific and applied significance, reflecting the compound’s role in underexplored parasitic pathways and its potential to combat drug-resistant malaria, with a clear and engaging narrative.

任务: Breast cancer cells penetrating the fibrous matrix within the bone microenvironment, with clearly visible cell membrane structures, dynamically showcasing the complex interactions and invasive behaviors between the cells and bone tissue. Emphasizing cellular morphology and microstructural changes, utilizing vivid colors and high-resolution enhanced imaging techniques. The footage focuses on the cell invasion process, suitable for a 5-second microscopic world video.
具体要求: Produce a captivating microscopic world video that depicts the invasive journey of breast cancer cells penetrating and expanding within the bone microenvironment, illustrating their adaptive evolution to sustain tumor growth. Within the scene, visualize the complex dynamic interplay between breast cancer cells and bone tissue through enhanced imaging technology developed by WEHI researchers, demonstrating the transition of cellular remodeling and invasion. Highlight intricate textures visible at the cellular level, emphasizing cell membrane structures, interactions with the fibrous matrix, and the complex signaling networks guiding invasive spread. Accentuate the rich visual contrast between cancer cells and the bone environment using vivid colors and high-resolution imaging, underscoring the morphological and microstructural changes occurring as tumor cells evolve and promote their growth. Ensure a smooth visual narrative that engages the audience with detailed microscopic depictions, reflecting the pioneering research led by Dr. Raymond Yip, Associate Professor Edwin Hawkins, Professor Geoff Lindeman, and Professor Jane Visvader as published in Nature Communications. The video should provide an inspiring experience showcasing WEHI Dynamic Imaging Centre's revolutionary imaging technologies to educate and engage viewers on the cellular mechanisms underlying breast cancer metastasis.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Cell morphology and cell membrane microstructure are clearly defined and consistent with micrometer-scale proportions; the fiber orientation within the fibrous matrix reasonably corresponds to the pore scale.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Accuracy of cell invasion biological processes: The temporal and mechanical coupling of cell–matrix adhesion, matrix degradation, and migration aligns with physiological principles, emphasizing the coordination between pseudopodia/invadopodia and matrix remodeling.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Correct representation of key components and spatial relationships within the bone microenvironment: the distribution of mineralized bone matrix, bone-related cells, and vasculature/bone marrow stroma aligns with their functional rationale, with intercellular signaling and bone remodeling processes described without contradictions.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Imaging and color mapping conform to the principles of biological imaging: resolution, depth of field, and noise levels are consistent with the claimed enhanced imaging techniques; pseudocolor mapping does not confound structures or pathways and does not exceed the resolution attainable by existing technologies.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Narrative and causal expressions contain scientific misrepresentations: describing transient cellular responses as evolutionary changes, implying cellular intent through anthropomorphic language, overstating mechanistic certainty, or indicating incorrect causal relationships.

rubrics_visual.d1: High image clarity and microscopic detail resolution, with clear texture edges of structures such as cell membranes and fibrous matrix; fine detail reproduction is delicate without excessive sharpening or smudging.
rubrics_visual.d2: The animation demonstrates coherence and smooth temporal narrative flow, with natural deformation, penetration, and interactive motion trajectories. Camera movements and depth of field transitions are seamless, exhibiting no stuttering or frame drops.
rubrics_visual.d3: Precise control of color and lighting, vivid yet undistorted; stable white balance and contrast; clear separation between subject and background layers; natural rendering of volume and depth of field effects.
rubrics_visual.d4: The 3D modeling and material appearance are realistic, with high geometric and topological accuracy; microscale material properties such as roughness, subsurface scattering, and refraction are appropriately configured; contact and deformation feedback are believable.
rubrics_visual.d5: Visual Defect Control (Negative): The presence of prominent defects such as noise, flickering, banding, aliasing, inter-frame jitter, and compression artifacts significantly degrades the viewing experience and detail recognition.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Core Scenario and Main Narrative Implementation: Target cells penetrate fibers/matrix within the specified microenvironment and exhibit invasive expansion; cell membranes and microstructures are clearly visible, with the visualization continuously focused on the invasion process and dynamic interactions.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Presentation of Key Elements and Relationships: Encompasses core components such as cell-tissue dynamic interactions, fibrous matrix texture, and signaling pathway networks, highlighting morphological and microstructural changes as well as environmental contrasts.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Imaging style and technical specifications compliance: utilizes vibrant colors and high-resolution enhanced imaging, with sharp details, distinct texture layering, and consistent yet prominent visual contrast.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Duration and Perspective Control: Total duration approximately 5 seconds, with coherent and concise narrative; stable camera maintaining a microscopic close-up view, subject clearly centered; editing rhythm facilitates process comprehension.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Educational and Science Communication: Clearly conveys the highlights of imaging technologies and the associated biological mechanisms; the narrative is insightful and holds educational value.

任务: In the microscopic world, blue natural killer cells actively recognize and attack fetal red blood cells coated with red anti-Rh D antibodies within the bloodstream. Utilizing a high-definition fluorescence microscopy style, emphasize cellular textures and structures, showcasing the dynamic interaction between immune cells and red blood cells, highlighting the critical role of the immune system in the prevention of hemolytic disease of the newborn.
具体要求: Create a stunning video depicting the microscopic world of the human immune system, specifically illustrating the complex and dramatic interactions between natural killer cells and fetal red blood cells in the prevention of hemolytic disease of the newborn (HDN). Capture the dynamic processes within the bloodstream, portraying natural killer cells in vivid blue as they actively seek, recognize, and destroy fetal red blood cells, which are dramatically highlighted in red after being coated with maternally derived anti-Rh D antibodies. Emphasize fine high-definition details of the scene, revealing the intricate textures and structures of immune cells and red blood cells as seen through fluorescence microscopy. Focus on the exquisite interplay of light and color, reflecting the advanced scientific techniques employed in this research, conveying the urgency and significance of this immune response. Ensure the video embodies the profound research efforts of Behnaz Heydarchi and colleagues, providing viewers with an engaging visual narrative that underscores the importance of discoveries aimed at improving HDN prevention methods.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Immunological mechanisms and effector cell attribution consistent with hemolytic disease of the newborn: fetal red blood cells coated with IgG anti-RhD are primarily cleared via the mononuclear-macrophage system mediated by Fcγ receptors, while complement involvement and NK cell-mediated ADCC are considered possible secondary pathways and not part of the central mechanism.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Accuracy of Cell Morphology and Scale Proportions: Erythrocytes exhibit anucleate biconcave disc shape with appropriate size-to-thickness ratio; natural killer cells possess slightly larger volume with prominent nucleus and cytoplasmic granules; fluorescent signal localization corresponds accurately to the cellular structures.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Reasonable spatial and hemodynamic environment: interactions occur within microenvironments consistent with clearance mechanisms (e.g., low shear regions in the splenic cords/sinusoids or hepatic sinusoids); if contact is demonstrated in the bloodstream, it should reflect leukocyte margination, shear-dependent arrest, and receptor binding timescales.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Correct sequence of molecular and cellular interactions: antibody Fab specifically binds RhD antigen while Fc is oriented outward to engage Fcγ receptors; formation of immune synapse/phagocytic cup proceeds coherently in both process and timing; erythrocyte clearance is primarily via phagocytosis, avoiding mechanistically unsupported lytic bursting.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Fluorescence microscopy presentation is scientifically credible: blurring caused by point spread function and depth of focus is appropriately accounted for; levels of photobleaching and background noise are reasonable; color coding and colocalization criteria are consistent, avoiding the use of colors that imply nonexistent functions or structures.

rubrics_visual.d1: The image exhibits high clarity with finely rendered microscopic details; cell membrane folds, granular structures, and edges are distinctly identifiable, while textures remain faithful and free from apparent noise and compression artifacts.
rubrics_visual.d2: Accurate fluorescence color separation and contrast control, with clearly defined layers of blue immunocytes and red-labeled targets; saturation, white balance, and black level are natural and stable, avoiding color contamination and excessive fluorescence bleed-through.
rubrics_visual.d3: The animation exhibits smoothness and good consistency with biophysical fluid dynamics, with drift/rolling motions driven by blood flow and a coherent, seamless sequence of recognition—contact—attack—clearance, free of stuttering, tearing, or motion blur.
rubrics_visual.d4: 3D modeling and material texture are realistic, with biologically plausible cell morphology and size proportions; translucency/fluorescent materials, surface roughness, and subsurface scattering are accurately rendered; deformations and adhesion responses during interactions appear natural.
rubrics_visual.d5: Consistent lighting and microscopy imaging style; volumetric rendering exhibiting plausible emission, depth of field, and point spread function defocus effects; appropriately controlled exposure and noise levels to prevent glare flicker and abrupt luminance changes.

rubrics_instruction.d11: Comprehensively depict the core interactions between natural killer cells and antibody-coated fetal red blood cells highlighted in red within a microcirculatory blood flow environment, focusing on the prevention of hemolytic disease of the newborn.
rubrics_instruction.d22: Accurately represents key elements and attributes: immune cells are depicted in blue, target red blood cells are highlighted in red with evidence of coating; cellular texture and structure are clearly distinguishable.
rubrics_instruction.d33: Conformance to high-definition fluorescence microscopy style and perspective standards: high resolution, appropriate fluorescence contrast and spectral layering, stable microscale presentation, and lens focus capturing cell-level interactions.
rubrics_instruction.d44: Dynamic process and duration control are well-executed: presenting a continuous sequence of searching—identification—attack—elimination, with smooth pacing, duration appropriately aligned with narrative requirements, natural transitions, and an emphasis on urgency and dramatic tension.
rubrics_instruction.d55: Conveying Scientific and Popular Science Objectives: Emphasizes the critical role of immune responses in disease prevention, with clear information presentation and a narrative that aligns with the research context without extraneous elements.

任务: Dynamically present the fluorescently stained human pulmonary vasculature from a high-resolution light sheet microscopy perspective, highlighting the luminescent vascular network and its interactions with migrating cancer cells. The details should be clear and richly textured, showcasing the microscopic process of cancer cells navigating through the vascular maze. The imagery should be vivid and focused, suitable for a 5-second microscopic world video.
具体要求: Create a stunning microscopic world video that immerses the audience into the vibrant hidden universe of human pulmonary vasculature, dynamically depicting the complex process of cancer metastasis. The video should center on vividly presenting a captivating “red forest,” where blood vessels are illuminated by fluorescent dyes, revealing the dynamic interactions between these vessels and migrating cancer cells. Utilize advanced light sheet microscopy to visually capture the surreal landscape inside the lung, allowing viewers to witness how cancer cells traverse this vascular maze in search of new tissues to colonize. Present the vasculature with striking and vivid detail to enhance its complexity and texture, emphasizing its critical role in cancer metastasis. Highlight the glowing reticular vascular structures, showcasing contrasting textures in high-resolution detail aimed at engaging viewers and inspiring their imagination toward novel cancer therapies that could disrupt these interactions and halt metastatic spread. Subtly indicate scientific progress at the conclusion, encouraging viewers to explore more in the virtual gallery at [URL], thereby motivating further intellectual engagement with this breathtaking depiction of microscopic biology.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The vascular network’s reticular structure and scale relationships correspond to the characteristics of human pulmonary microcirculation, with appropriate branching hierarchy, vessel diameter distribution, curvature, and connectivity, as well as an accurate ratio between cancer cell size and vessel diameter.
rubrics_scientific.d2: The mechanism of interaction between cancer cells and vascular endothelial cells is accurately described, reflecting the sequential processes of adhesion, rolling, flow-driven deformation, and transmigration. The transmigration sites and cellular deformation are consistent with biomechanical principles.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Hemodynamic and cellular motility dynamics conform to microcirculatory principles, characterized by laminar flow dominance, physiologically consistent near-wall velocity gradients, and constrained deformation patterns; under a 5-second time compression, event sequencing and motion continuity are scientifically accurate.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Fluorescent labeling and imaging are physically consistent, with clear channel separation and precise signal localization (distinct differentiation between endothelial and cancer cell regions). The optical sectioning, out-of-focus attenuation, and anisotropic resolution characteristics of light sheet microscopy are appropriately represented, without unreasonable hotspots or autofluorescence artifacts.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Accurate histological identification and structural texture, clear differentiation between blood vessels, lymphatic vessels, and bronchial regions, vessel wall layers and texture not excessively exaggerated, with network orientation compatible with pulmonary tissue architecture.

rubrics_visual.d1: High image clarity with detailed representation of microstructures; structural edges are sharp and stable; texture information is rich with no evident blurring.
rubrics_visual.d2: The fluorescence color performance is pure with natural contrast; saturation and brightness levels are balanced, exhibiting clear color stratification without color bleeding or false colors.
rubrics_visual.d3: Excellent animation smoothness and motion continuity; smooth camera and object movements; stable inter-frame transitions without jitter or stutter; rhythm consistent with micro-dynamic characteristics.
rubrics_visual.d4: High accuracy in 3D modeling with realistic material textures; credible structural complexity and scale relationships; clear mesh topology; delicate representation of translucency and surface roughness.
rubrics_visual.d5: The lighting and shadow effects are naturally and harmoniously integrated, with directional illumination and volumetric scattering exhibiting softness. Highlights are well-controlled without overexposure, and details are preserved in shadowed areas. The focus and depth of field transitions are smooth and seamless.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Comprehensive presentation of core biological structures and specified dynamic processes: Fluorescently labeled human pulmonary vascular network interacting with migrating cancer cells, featuring vivid focus and clear details.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Adhering to the high-resolution perspective and imaging style of light sheet microscopy: layered illumination, spatial tomographic sensitivity, rich textural detail, and high-contrast textures.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Prominent luminescent reticular vasculature serves as the visual focal point and creates a "red forest" ambiance, depicting the pathways and interaction sites of cancer cells navigating through the vascular labyrinth.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Technical Specifications for Duration and Rhythm Compliance: Total duration approximately 5 seconds; stable shots with clear composition; completion of the dynamic sequence’s initiation, development, transition, and conclusion within the allotted time. The ending should include scientific guidance, encouraging viewers to continue exploring the virtual gallery.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Avoidance of Instruction Deviations and Low-Quality Performance: Deductions will be applied for the presence of non-designated biological structures or incorrect scenes, mismatched perspectives, substantially insufficient duration, blurred details, or interference from extraneous elements.

任务: Ultra-high-definition microscopic perspective showcasing the thymic lobule structure, focusing on the vibrant cortex and the complex medulla. The medulla features a large, beautifully branched magenta central structure surrounded by colorful islands, suggesting the T cell selection process. The imagery is rich in detail and color, displaying the intricate textures and unique microstructures of the thymic lobule, highlighting its critical role in the immune system.
具体要求: Create an engaging and scientifically accurate microscopic world video emphasizing the reconstructed thymic lobule, a key organ of the immune system, presented with astonishing detail. Capture dynamic scenes of the thymic 'coral reef,' highlighting its distinctive structure composed of two regions: the vibrant cortex and the complex medulla. The medulla should exhibit two captivating morphologies—a large, aesthetically branched central structure in striking magenta, and smaller, vividly colored peripheral isolated islands—demonstrating how T cells are selected and 'trained' to recognize foreign antigens. Emphasize the extraordinary textures and colors of this microscopic landscape, showcasing diverse hues and the remarkable complexity of thymic architecture. Depict the vitality of this organ, most active in early childhood but reduced in size and function after puberty, with attention to how chemotherapy and radiotherapy may impair the thymus, thereby affecting patient immune function. Through this video, explore the potential to enhance thymic function and initiate regeneration post-cancer treatment to improve patient survival. Present the complex patterns and vivid colors in ultra-high definition, revealing an unprecedented artistic beauty of the microscopic world.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Accurate thymic cortex-medulla anatomical compartmentalization and topological continuity, clearly demonstrating the outer cortex, inner medulla, and corticomedullary junction, with the medulla presenting a realistic morphology of branching networks and scattered island-like structures.
rubrics_scientific.d2: The spatiotemporal mechanisms of T cell development and selection are accurately described, reflecting maturation migration from the cortex to the medulla, with positive selection occurring in the cortex and negative selection in the medulla, both mediated by antigen-presenting cells.
rubrics_scientific.d3: The scale relationship and density gradients between cells and microstructures are appropriate; the cortex contains a high density of immature thymocytes while the medulla is relatively sparse; the sizes and spacing of blood vessels and stromal networks correspond to microscopic scales; cellular motility and molecular diffusion rates are physiologically consistent.
rubrics_scientific.d4: The dynamic processes and temporal sequences conform to physiological principles, incorporating events such as cell migration, receptor rearrangement and selection, apoptosis, and clearance, with their relative order and duration being appropriate and consistent with spatial localization.
rubrics_scientific.d5: The visual encoding and life history narration do not mislead biological interpretations; colors and textures are used to distinguish regions or processes consistently, accurately representing heightened activity during childhood, functional decline after puberty, as well as the effects of chemotherapy and radiotherapy on structure and function and their gradual recovery.

rubrics_visual.d1: Ultra-high-definition image clarity and micro-detail fidelity: sharp microstructural edges, rich textural layering, consistent scale, without excessive sharpening or smearing.
rubrics_visual.d2: Color Rendering and Layer Separation: Saturation and contrast are naturally stable; key structural tonal areas are distinctly differentiated; overall tonality is uniform without noticeable color cast.
rubrics_visual.d3: Animation Smoothness and Temporal Consistency: Smooth camera movement and focal plane shift, coherent particle/cell motion, stable inter-frame details without flickering, motion blur matched to perceived velocity.
rubrics_visual.d4: Realistic 3D modeling and material optical properties: clear geometric hierarchy and rational topology; materials exhibiting translucency and subsurface scattering, microsurface roughness, and volumetric texture, with texture resolution and scale appropriately matched.
rubrics_visual.d5: Visual artifacts and noise are prominent, including jagged edges, flickering, color banding, compression blocks, moiré patterns, edge jitter, shadow noise, and texture stretching, all of which degrade the image quality.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Comprehensive presentation of the thymic lobule core region: clearly distinguish the outer cortex from the inner medulla, with the entire area consistently displayed through a microscopic close-up perspective.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Key structures and elements in place: The central branches of the medullary region and the distinct peripheral islets are clearly distinguishable, exhibiting prominent morphological and color contrast, and visually narrating the process of T cell selection and maturation.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Scientific Accuracy and Educational Content Expression: The structure-function relationship is presented reliably, encompassing core background information such as activity changes with age, therapeutic effects, and regenerative potential, while avoiding misleading statements.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Visual and Technical Specifications Compliance: Ultra-high-definition final footage; rich details and textures; diverse yet consistent color palette; stable camera work and clear composition; thorough depiction of dynamic processes and structural interactions; duration and pacing aligned with instructions.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Major Deviation Penalty: Missing two major regions or specified key structures, lacking a microscopic immersive perspective, containing significant scientific inaccuracies, or exhibiting image quality notably below ultra-high-definition standards, resulting in the failure to achieve the instructional objectives.

任务: In the microscopic world, WEHI researchers discovered an insulin-mimetic molecule that dynamically binds to cell surface receptors, activating insulin-like biochemical reactions. The high-definition cellular membrane background displays a mosaic of lipids and proteins on the receptor surface, with the molecular binding process to the receptor clearly visible. Delicate textures and fluid dynamics illustrate molecular interactions, reflecting a breakthrough in diabetes research.
具体要求: Create an engaging video set in the microscopic world, focusing on a revolutionary molecule discovered by WEHI researchers that mimics the effects of insulin, showcasing a remarkable breakthrough in diabetes research. The scene captures the dynamic interaction as this innovative molecule freely binds to cell surface receptors and initiates a series of insulin-like biochemical reactions. As the video progresses, complex molecular animations reveal atomic-level binding events, elegantly demonstrating how this alternative molecule activates cellular processes typically regulated by insulin. Fine textures and vivid colors depict subtle fluctuations of the cell membrane, with the receptor surface adorned by a mosaic of lipids and proteins. Close-up shots highlight intricate patterns on the molecular surface, with bond formation and dissociation rendered precisely and captivatingly. This scene not only illuminates the scientific marvel of a potential oral insulin substitute but also showcases the elegance and complexity of molecular biology in exquisite detail. High-definition visuals dramatize the microscopic world into reality, capturing the essence of biomedical innovation and presenting viewers with a vivid spectacle of particle interactions and fluid-like cellular environments, while emphasizing the potential future impact of this pioneering research on transforming diabetes management.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The fluid mosaic model of the cell membrane is accurately represented, with bilayer thickness and lipid/protein distribution appropriately rendered, membrane fluctuation amplitude and temporal scales realistic, and receptor transmembrane topology and membrane-anchoring positions authentically depicted.
rubrics_scientific.d2: The receptor-ligand binding mechanism is scientifically rigorous, with binding sites and conformational changes consistent with the principles of induced fit and/or conformational selection. Affinity and reversibility are appropriately characterized, and the activation state transitions and the potential sequence of receptor dimerization are logically coherent.
rubrics_scientific.d3: The temporal and spatial organization of signal transduction following receptor activation is accurate, with clear causality and appropriate latency in cascade events. The recruitment and diffusion range of signaling molecules correspond to cellular scale and localization characteristics.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Molecular motion and interactions conform to the principles of physical chemistry. The diffusion and Brownian motion trajectories, the influence of solvent and ionic environment, as well as the energy barriers and reversibility of bond formation and cleavage are presented plausibly, without any instantaneous long-range forces or frictionless motion that constitute pseudoscientific effects.
rubrics_scientific.d5: The consistency of cross-scale visualization is well-maintained, with atomic-level details and cellular-level backgrounds coordinated in terms of scale, speed, and time steps; the abstraction of molecular number density and crowding effects does not lead to misinterpretation.

rubrics_visual.d1: Image clarity and fine representation of microstructural details, with sharp structural edges and distinct layering.
rubrics_visual.d2: Excellent animation smoothness and interaction coherence, with particle motion and deformation trajectories exhibiting smoothness, free of stuttering and motion blur.
rubrics_visual.d3: The 3D modeling exhibits high precision and realistic material response, with standardized geometric structures, and appropriate representation of micro-surface roughness, anisotropy, and subsurface scattering.
rubrics_visual.d4: Lighting and shadow effects and depth of field control are natural; volumetric scattering, refraction, and soft shadows are well-coordinated, highlighting spatial hierarchy.
rubrics_visual.d5: Color management and contrast control are effectively implemented, exhibiting good hue consistency, moderate saturation, and balanced luminance distribution.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Comprehensively present the core process in which insulin mimetic molecules discovered by WEHI dynamically bind to cell surface receptors and trigger insulin-like biochemical responses, forming a closed loop of recognition–binding–activation.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Scene restoration of the microscopic world and cell membrane characteristics: High-definition cell membrane background with clearly defined receptor surface lipid–protein mosaic texture; atomic-level details of molecule–receptor interactions and clear visualization of bond formation and breakage.
rubrics_instruction.d3: The camera angles and perspectives align with the microscopic theme: employing close-up or zoom-in shots to focus on relevant events, with stable framing and a prominent subject; the tempo of the shots supports a coherent presentation of key stages.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Image quality and dynamic performance meet standards: high-definition resolution, detailed textures with fluid motion, vivid and scientifically accurate colors, continuous animation without noticeable stuttering or compression artifacts.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Accuracy in Scientific Narrative and Educational Expression: Emphasizes breakthroughs and potential impacts in diabetes research, with precise use of terminology and clear expression of causal relationships, focusing content on the mechanistic presentation of insulin replacement molecules.

任务: Under a high-definition microscope perspective, display a top-down cross-section of a neuron with a significant genetic mutation in a petri dish. The neuron is connected through fine synaptic junctions, with electrical currents dynamically flowing along the fibers to transmit information. The image is richly detailed, highlighting the synaptic textures and the microscopic interactions of electrical flow, showcasing the complex beauty of the neuronal structure.
具体要求: Produce a visually stunning and awe-inspiring microscopic world video focusing on the intricate and beautiful structure of neurons, which are the fundamental components of our nervous system. Capture the essence of neurons in a petri dish as researcher Melody Leong investigates how specific genetic mutations affect neuronal interactions, leading to neurodevelopmental disorders. This high-definition video should begin with a breathtaking top-down cross-section of a neuron bearing a significant mutation. Throughout the video, demonstrate neurons connected via small junctions called synapses, transmitting information through electrical impulses flowing along fine fibers, resembling electricity passing through delicate wires. By emphasizing the vivid textures and details of the synapses, illustrate the dynamic interactions of neurons, highlighting the subtle electrical currents and the structural impact of genetic mutations. The video should be a compilation of high-resolution microscopic images captured by expert Yuqing Yang, providing in-depth insights into how these mutations may alter normal neurodevelopmental processes, thereby revealing potential new therapeutic targets. Finally, ensure the visual journey is detailed and richly textured, offering the audience an unparalleled experience of the neuronal microscopic world and its boundless possibilities.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The microscopic structure of neurons and synapses is accurately presented: the morphology and connectivity of the soma, axon, dendrites, and dendritic spines are appropriate; the hierarchical arrangement and spatial relationships among the presynaptic terminal, synaptic cleft, and postsynaptic density are correct.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Scientific performance of neural signal conduction mechanisms: action potentials propagate directionally along excitable membranes; synaptic transmission is mediated via neurotransmitter/receptor interactions with ion channels; visualization of electrical activity accurately reflects the nature of ionic currents, avoiding representations of intracellular electrical signals as transmembrane leakage beams or vacuum electric sparks.
rubrics_scientific.d3: The manifestation of the effects of genetic mutations on morphology and function is based on a plausible biological mechanism: the impact is focused at the cellular or molecular level (e.g., dendritic spine density, synaptic vesicle cycling, ion channel kinetics, or protein localization), with a logically consistent causal chain and congruent findings relative to control groups.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Multi-scale spatiotemporal consistency: structural dimensions and relative proportions are within reasonable magnitudes; the sequence and duration of dynamic processes correspond to physiological patterns (e.g., rapid electrical signals and slower calcium signals, vesicle cycling, and more gradual hierarchical structural remodeling).
rubrics_scientific.d5: Physical Consistency of Microscopic Imaging: The texture details, contrast mechanisms, resolution, and noise characteristics are consistent with the claimed imaging modality (optical, super-resolution, electron microscopy, etc.), without exhibiting clarity beyond the resolution limit or unreasonable false-color encoding.

rubrics_visual.d1: High image clarity and detailed resolution, with well-defined hierarchical microstructural textures (synapses, fibers, cell membranes), clean edges, and natural, non-excessive sharpening.
rubrics_visual.d2: Smooth animation with high fidelity in microscopic dynamics; coherent rhythm in electrical signals or particle flow; velocity variations consistent with scale; free of jitter and motion blur.
rubrics_visual.d3: The rendition of light, color, and depth of field is natural and harmonious; details are preserved in both highlights and shadows; color saturation and contrast are properly controlled; and the bokeh transition is smooth.
rubrics_visual.d4: The 3D modeling demonstrates accurate geometry and authentic material rendering, with clear and non-intersecting structural topology. Translucency, subsurface scattering, and micro-surface roughness are appropriately represented.
rubrics_visual.d5: Effective control of image artifacts and noise to prevent issues affecting visual quality such as flickering, compression blocks, aliasing, moiré patterns, and color banding.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Fully realize a petri dish scene under a high-definition microscope perspective, opening with a top-down cross-sectional view of neurons, clearly presenting prominent genetic mutations.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Demonstrates neuronal interactions connected via synaptic boutons, with electrical impulses dynamically propagating along fine fibers, highlighting synaptic textures and microscopic details.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Accurately reproduces the intricate aesthetics of neuronal structures and scene elements, including synapses, fibers, the culture dish background, and the textural qualities of the microscopic environment, with comprehensive element coverage.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Meets high-resolution and lens technical specifications; the microscope field of view encompasses the entire specimen; the image is stable, clear, and detailed; the top-view cross-section is used as the initial frame.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Demonstrates research-oriented and public science interpretation of the impact of genetic mutations, conveying information and insights through a coherent compilation of microscopic images, with clear content organization.

任务: Demonstrate the molecular dynamics of a novel molecule simulating insulin effects, focusing on the binding process between this molecule and cellular receptors, highlighting molecular surface details and unique binding patterns, with a dynamic cell membrane background, subtle cytoplasmic flow movements, vibrant color contrasts, clear and concise, suitable for a 5-second microscopic world video.
具体要求: Create a stunning video showcasing a groundbreaking discovery in diabetes research within the microscopic world. The central theme of this captivating scene is a novel molecule identified by researchers that can mimic insulin effects. Within a complex cellular environment, the video reveals the molecular dynamics between this insulin-like molecule and cellular receptors, demonstrating its potent impact on glucose uptake. Emphasize exquisite molecular surface details and textures, highlight its unique binding patterns, complex structural configurations, and convey the subtle motions orchestrating metabolic reactions. The video should capture vibrant colors representing distinct molecular domains, dynamic changes of the cell membrane, and the fluidity of the cytoplasm, providing viewers with an astonishing perspective on this key discovery, which holds promise for advances in oral diabetes therapy.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The receptor–ligand binding interface and conformational changes conform to molecular recognition principles, with the contact region exhibiting hydrophobic, hydrogen bonding, and electrostatic complementarity, while avoiding non-physical penetration and severe atomic clashes.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Reasonable scale relationships and hierarchical structure: the relative sizes, spacing, and crowding of ligands, receptors, lipid bilayers, and the intracellular background conform to cellular-scale principles.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Accuracy of Membrane Environment and Receptor Topology: The bilayer nature and fluidity of the lipid bilayer are realistically represented, with correct embedding of the receptor transmembrane regions and proper orientation of the extracellular and intracellular domains.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Molecular dynamics and time series are scientifically coherent: the ligand approaches driven by Brownian motion/diffusion, inducing either induced fit or conformational selection; the binding event clearly triggers receptor activation followed by glucose uptake with a well-defined causal sequence, without exaggerating cross-scale instantaneous effects.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Consistency between visualization encoding and scientific semantics: Colors, brightness, and special effects establish stable mappings to different molecules or states, avoiding the misinterpretation of decorative effects as genuine physicochemical processes.

rubrics_visual.d1: Image clarity and fine delineation of microscopic details; key binding sites and surface textures are distinctly identifiable; edges are clean and free of jagged artifacts.
rubrics_visual.d2: 3D modeling and material realism are achieved with consistent geometric proportions and scale. Molecular and receptor surface normals, roughness, transparency, and refraction accurately depict microscopic texture, with natural transitions at contact interfaces.
rubrics_visual.d3: The animation and dynamic performance are smooth and coherent, with molecular motion trajectories and binding transitions occurring naturally without frame skipping. Camera movements are stable, the 5-second pacing is clear with moderate information density, and background dynamics do not interfere with the main subject.
rubrics_visual.d4: Lighting and color are naturally and harmoniously managed, with consistent illumination direction and shadow layering; depth of field moderately enhances spatial layering. Hue, saturation, and contrast are vivid yet not glaring, and the subject is well separated from the background in terms of color.
rubrics_visual.d5: Technical defects such as noise, compression artifacts, color banding, moiré patterns, excessive sharpening or noise reduction traces, edge halos, and inter-frame flickering are prominent, compromising image stability and detail.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Comprehensively present the molecular dynamics process centered on the binding of novel insulin-like molecules with cellular receptors, emphasizing the binding sites and interaction sequences.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Accurately reflects key elements and details: molecular surface texture, unique binding patterns, and structural conformations, while conveying insights into their impact on glucose uptake.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Consistency between scene environment and microcontext: dynamic changes of the cell membrane and subtle cytoplasmic flows are continuously presented, with the overall scale corresponding to the biological microscopic world.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Compliance with visual and technical specifications: vivid color contrast with a clean and clear image, duration approximately 5 seconds, focused viewpoint combined with the relevant area, coherent camera movement and rhythm.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Major Deduction Criteria: Omission or weakening of the integration process; inclusion of macroscopic content irrelevant to the microscopic topic; significant errors in key interactions or structures; substantial deviation in duration; disorganized perspective resulting in unintelligible information.

任务: Under the microscope, cancer cells rapidly proliferate while immune cells (macrophages and T cells) actively interact within the extracellular matrix. Immune cells extend pseudopodia to attack cancer cells. The cell membranes exhibit fine texture, and fluorescent labeling reveals molecular signals, highlighting the contrast between the smooth surface of cancer cells and the rough texture of immune cells. The extracellular matrix displays fibrous and fluid-like structures with vivid colors, dynamically portraying the moment of cellular combat.
具体要求: Create a stunning microscopic world video showcasing the complex cellular interactions occurring within cancerous tissue, focusing on the vivid encounters between cancer cells and immune cells in their microenvironment. Capture dynamic scenes of rapid cancer cell proliferation alongside active engagement of immune cells such as macrophages and T cells as they attempt to neutralize malignant threats through intricate maneuvers. Emphasize the remarkable details and textures visible at the microscopic level, illustrating diverse cell membrane structural features, fibrous and fluid-like characteristics of the extracellular matrix, and the dynamic presentation of colorful cellular components and proteins. Highlight the contrast between the smooth surfaces of cancer cells and the rough appearance of immune cells, as well as the subtle molecular interactions and signals revealed by fluorescence microscopy, vividly narrating the story of cellular combat, resilience, and breakthroughs in medical research.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Cell morphology and size ratios correspond to the characteristic features of tumor cells and immune cells; cancer cells exhibit smoother surfaces, whereas immune cells display folds and pseudopodia. The nucleus-to-cytoplasm ratio and overall scale fall within acceptable ranges.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Scientific credibility of cellular dynamics and interaction mechanisms: The sequence and timing of cancer cell proliferation and mitotic phases are reasonable; chemotaxis, adhesion, immunological synapse/phagocytosis, or cytotoxic processes of macrophages and T cells occur at points of contact.
rubrics_scientific.d3: The fibers and gel-like structures of the extracellular matrix are accurately represented, with pore size and fiber orientation influencing cell migration and deformation. Cells exert traction forces on the matrix rather than being irrationally driven by large-scale fluid flow.
rubrics_scientific.d4: The localization and imaging characteristics of fluorescent labeling and molecular signals are appropriate: the label distribution corresponds accurately to the respective cellular compartments, spectral bands and intensity settings prevent false colocalization, and signal diffusion/aggregation, as well as photobleaching and blinking phenomena, remain within acceptable limits.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Scale and temporal consistency conforming to physicobiological constraints: cell migration speed on the order of micrometers per minute; small molecules exhibiting thermal diffusion and random motion; membrane deformation and cytoplasmic motion amplitudes not exceeding the limits of biological materials; mass and volume changes adhering to conservation principles.

rubrics_visual.d1: High image clarity with finely detailed depiction of microscopic-scale features (membrane texture, fibrous structure, particulate layering); edges are sharp without significant blur or ghosting.
rubrics_visual.d2: Color and fluorescence labeling exhibit natural and consistent expression, with well-controlled saturation and contrast; signals demonstrate clear hierarchical distinction without noticeable crosstalk or spillover.
rubrics_visual.d3: The animation exhibits smoothness and physical coherence, with seamless transitions in cell interactions, pseudopod extension and retraction, and morphological deformations, free of stuttering or frame skipping.
rubrics_visual.d4: The 3D modeling demonstrates high accuracy and realistic material texture, with a distinct contrast between surface roughness and smoothness, and consistent representation of extracellular matrix fibers and fluid structures.
