任务: Microscopic visualization of cervical tissue, focusing on the rapid proliferation of early abnormal cervical cancer cells, dynamically illustrating the destruction of healthy cellular structures by cancer cells, accompanied by abnormal secretions and micro-hemorrhages, highlighting the contrast in color and texture between healthy cells and cancer cells, with a finely detailed cervical tissue background. The overall style is scientific and vivid.
具体要求: Create a stunning microscopic world video centered on the transformation of cervical cells into cancer cells, capturing the microscopic changes and growth patterns associated with the early stages of cervical cancer. This scene immerses the viewer in the complex progression of cervical cancer, demonstrating the dynamic interactions of abnormal cervical cell proliferation that lead to increased tissue density and disruption of normal cellular architecture. These cellular transformations are visually represented with abnormal secretions and micro-hemorrhages, emphasizing the manifestation of these physiological symptoms at the cellular level. The video should meticulously depict the texture of cervical tissue, accentuating the contrast between healthy and cancerous cells by showcasing differences in color and granular texture that distinguish malignant cells from normal cells. The animation aims to educate viewers by vividly portraying the biological processes underlying cervical cancer symptoms, revealing the intricate cellular anatomy and the destructive growth characteristics of cancer within the cervical region, while ensuring educational accuracy, vividness, and visual appeal, capturing both the microscopic beauty and the tragic nature of this medical phenomenon.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The cervical epithelium, basement membrane, stroma, and glandular layers are clearly delineated, with spatial orientation and proportions accurately maintained; in the early lesion stage, the integrity of the basement membrane or the presence of focal microinvasion is consistent with the indicated disease progression.
rubrics_scientific.d2: The scale relationships at the cellular and subcellular levels are appropriate, with the sizes and relative proportions of cells, nuclei and nucleoli, as well as red blood cells and capillaries consistent with microscopic scale ranges; imaging magnification and resolution are coherently represented.
rubrics_scientific.d3: The malignant cell morphology and proliferative kinetics are accurately expressed, demonstrating increased nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio, nuclear atypia with augmented chromatin content, and increased abnormal mitoses; the sequence of mitotic phases is correct, and abnormal proliferation leads to increased cell density, loss of polarity, and disruption of tissue architecture.
rubrics_scientific.d4: The manifestations of microvasculature and microbleeding correspond with physiological mechanisms; the distribution and caliber of capillaries are appropriate. Extravasation of erythrocytes, plasma diffusion, and local hemostasis/aggregation dynamics resulting from leakage or rupture align with fundamental principles of blood flow and hemostasis.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Characterization of abnormal secretions and the microenvironment is scientifically sound, with the viscosity, particulate nature, and color of secretions corresponding to mucus, inflammatory exudate, or necrotic debris components; interaction mechanisms such as reduced adhesion of cancer cells, matrix degradation, and migration are consistent with principles of cell biology and biochemistry.

rubrics_visual.d5: Lighting and depth of field are naturally controlled; subsurface scattering, volumetric lighting, as well as specular highlights and refraction in liquids are handled coherently; spatial hierarchy is clearly defined.

任务: Comparison of urothelial carcinoma cells and healthy cells within bladder tissue under high-definition microscopy, highlighting cancer cell pleomorphism, irregular nuclei, and damaged structures, dynamically demonstrating localized proliferation and dense clusters of cancer cells accompanied by a microvascular network, set against a background of healthy bladder epithelium, with high-contrast coloration focusing on a single microscopic field.
具体要求: Create a captivating high-definition microscopic world video to explore the complexity of urothelial carcinoma, or transitional cell carcinoma, within the inner layers of bladder tissue. This scene should depict the dynamic formation and proliferation of cancer cells against the backdrop of healthy bladder tissue, illustrating the contrasting life cycles and interactions between malignant and non-malignant cells. The video must focus on ultra-fine cellular details and textures, revealing structural differences between healthy and cancerous cells, emphasizing cancer cell pleomorphism, irregular nuclei, and compromised cellular architecture. Highlight the angiogenic process nourishing tumor growth, as well as dense, entangled clusters composed of rapidly dividing cells, all observed through a virtual cystoscopic perspective. Use vivid colors and sharp contrast to convey the alarming yet educational reality of bladder cancer under the microscope, enabling viewers to appreciate the microscopic battle occurring within the human body and providing an engaging and informative visual tool to enhance understanding for patients facing bladder cancer and their medical teams.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The morphology and scale ratio of cells and nuclei are accurately represented, demonstrating the differences between epithelial cells and cancer cells in polymorphism, nuclear irregularities, nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio, and subcellular structural impairment.
rubrics_scientific.d2: The organizational hierarchy and spatial relationships are accurate, with clear delineation of the bladder epithelial layers, basement membrane, and stromal boundary. The interface between tumor masses and healthy tissue, as well as the direction of invasion, are appropriate.
rubrics_scientific.d3: The temporal sequence and rate of cell proliferation and aggregate formation align with physiological ranges, mitotic phases proceed continuously, and the comparison of life cycles alongside intercellular interaction mechanisms is consistent and plausible.
rubrics_scientific.d4: The microvascular network and hemodynamic characteristics are physiologically representative, with capillary branching and density, as well as angiogenic targeting toward tumor regions; erythrocyte scale, flow velocity, and leakage features conform to physiological norms.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Microscopic imaging is consistent with visual mapping; magnification and scale calibration, depth of field, and focal points are maintained uniformly; high contrast or pseudocolor mapping corresponds accurately to the target structural features without causing misinterpretation.

任务: Under the microscope, bright white needle-shaped urate crystals slowly form within the vivid articular cartilage tissue. The crystals are translucent and shiny, with sharp edges that contrast distinctly against the soft cartilage. Leukocytes rapidly accumulate, triggering localized redness and swelling inflammation. The video dynamically depicts the crystals embedding into the soft tissue and the ensuing immune response, highlighting the texture of the crystals and the coloration of the inflammation.
具体要求: Create a stunning microscopic world video that deeply explores the fascinating and complex process of a gout attack, focusing on the central theme: the formation of urate crystals within the joint. Portray this captivating scene with high detail, showing bright white urate crystals forming against the vibrant background of healthy cells, vividly illustrating the drama unfolding within the human body. Capture the dynamic process of sharp needle-like crystals slowly crystallizing and embedding into the articular soft tissue within the microscopic environment, triggering a cascade of immune responses. Emphasize intricate textures such as the shiny, translucent quality of the urate crystals and the contrast between their sharp edges and the surrounding soft, cushion-like cartilage tissue. The video should bring to life the moments when these crystals provoke reactions, using visually striking inflammatory colors to engage viewers, highlighting the characteristic redness and swelling of this medical condition as leukocytes swarm in a desperate attack. Use close-up shots to emphasize tactile details on the crystal surfaces and the corresponding textures of the affected tissue, providing a scientifically accurate yet artistically presented exploration of this arthritic process, ensuring viewers gain a profound understanding of the pain and inflammation caused by these acute chemically induced formations.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Accurate microanatomical structures and spatial relationships of the joint: avascular cartilage matrix and chondrocyte lacunae, collagen network, synovial cavity and synovial vascular distribution and their adjacency are correctly represented; leukocyte origin and migration pathways conform to anatomical standards.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Scientific correlation between urate crystal morphology and scale: needle-shaped, highly refractive, semi-transparent appearance; length-to-diameter ratio proportional to neutrophil/chondrocyte dimensions; sharp edges and surface textures consistent with crystalline material characteristics.
rubrics_scientific.d3: The dynamics of immunity and inflammation conform to physiological principles: the chronological sequence of crystal-induced innate immune activation, chemotactic gradient formation, directed leukocyte migration and phagocytosis, cellular injury and release of inflammatory mediators, as well as changes in vascular permeability and perfusion, is coherent and appropriate.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Crystallization kinetics consistent with physicochemical principles: nucleation driven by supersaturation and anisotropic growth, dissolution-recrystallization equilibrium, particle and cellular motion under Brownian motion and viscous fluid mechanics, and crystal-soft tissue mechanical interactions are presented reasonably.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Imaging and visualization processing do not produce scientific misrepresentation: microscopy imaging modes correspond appropriately with optical effects (e.g., bright-field/polarization/phase contrast), color enhancements and temporal/velocity scaling are clearly conveyed, and scale bars and time scales are represented appropriately.

rubrics_visual.d1: Image clarity and precise rendering of microscopic details, with clear edges and textures, and effective control of noise and compression artifacts.
rubrics_visual.d2: High accuracy in 3D modeling and realistic material rendering, with clear differentiation between semi-transparent/crystalline and soft tissue textures, alongside consistent edge sharpness and volumetric perception.
rubrics_visual.d3: Lighting and depth of field are controlled naturally; the sense of scale in macro perspective is well-defined; transitions between highlights, refraction/scattering, and shadows are smooth; the subject is prominently emphasized.
rubrics_visual.d4: The animation exhibits smoothness and temporal consistency, with coherent microscopic processes (growth, embedding, aggregation) free of stuttering and jitter.
rubrics_visual.d5: Color and contrast are natural and stable, with appropriate emphasis on key reactive colors, avoiding oversaturation or distortion.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Complete presentation of the core microscopic scene: using articular cartilage as the primary background, focusing on depicting the formation of urate crystals within the joint and their embedding into soft tissues, thereby highlighting the central theme.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Accurately reflect the key elements and dynamic sequence: the texture of needle-like crystals and their sharp edges contrasted with the surrounding soft cartilage, accompanied by rapid leukocyte aggregation and localized erythema and swelling as part of the inflammatory response, forming a continuous progression of → embedding → immune reaction.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Meets the requirements for a microscopic perspective and close-up expression: the shot focuses on the interface between crystals and soft tissue, with clear and distinguishable detailed textures, consistent microscopic scale perception, scientifically accurate while incorporating artistic presentation.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Compliance with technical and temporal specifications: Duration and shot rhythm support the temporal relationship of "slow crystallization and rapid aggregation," with image clarity and contrast adapted to ensure the readability of microscopic details.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Deviation and Misleading Penalties: Presence of visual elements inconsistent with the microenvironment or joint context, incorrect biological structures, or exaggerated coloration and narrative that cause scientific misinterpretation, or shot composition that diminishes the central theme.

任务: Microscopic view of breast tissue with the breast compressed between two compression plates; low-dose X-rays penetrate the compressed tissue, revealing details of cellular structures, glands, calcification areas, and vascular networks. Vivid contrasting colors are used to differentiate tissue types, while soft lighting and shadows enhance the 3D texture. The focus is on a single dynamic scene, scientifically accurate and suitable for a 5-second microscopic world video.
具体要求: Create a captivating microscopic world video that delves into the complex cellular architecture within breast tissue, capturing intricate anatomical structures critical for medical diagnosis. Depict the fascinating interaction between X-rays and breast tissue during mammography, highlighting how low-dose X-rays traverse layered tissues to reveal changes within glandular tissue, ducts, adipose regions, connective tissue, and the intricate vascular network. The video should emphasize the moment the breast is positioned between two professional compression plates; as the plates compress the tissue, detailed arrangements and subtle textures of these biological components are displayed, capturing transient changes at the cellular level. Smooth transitions should demonstrate how different angles alter the perspective, showcasing the beautiful microscopic details of tissue composition and potential abnormalities that only this scientific technology can reveal. High-resolution visuals must include vivid contrasting colors to distinguish different tissue types and structures, focusing on textures and patterns representing calcification areas or imperfections, reflecting the precision and importance of mammography in detecting abnormalities in women over 40 or those exhibiting symptoms such as lumps or nipple discharge. Realistic shadowing and soft lighting effects should enhance the 3D visualization of these microscopic features, making this educational sequence both engaging and scientifically accurate, ultimately demonstrating innovative technology and its critical role in breast cancer screening and diagnosis.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Accuracy of breast microanatomical structures and spatial relationships: The morphology, hierarchical organization, and relative positioning of lobules, mammary ducts, adipose tissue, connective/stromal tissue, and the vascular network correspond to physiological anatomy; morphology and distribution of calcifications are appropriate.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Correct physics of X-ray imaging and projection geometry: accurate representation of contrast influenced by attenuation differences among tissues and by thickness/overlapping; compression reduces thickness and tissue overlap, enhancing structural visibility; high attenuation of calcifications results in increased brightness; changes in projection shape, magnification, and occlusion caused by varying viewing angles conform to geometric principles.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Scale and resolution are presented appropriately: the relative sizes and hierarchical relationships of cells, glands, small blood vessels, and calcifications are consistent; details beyond the resolving power of X-ray imaging are represented through abstraction or pseudocolor mapping rather than realistic rendering, avoiding implication of true single-cell-level visibility.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Biomechanical responses under compression consistent with physiological conditions: interlaminar compression induces uniform tissue thinning, mild lateral displacement, and flattening; vessels and conduits exhibit compliant, continuous deformation; calcifications show minimal displacement without deformation; temporal sequences are smooth without exaggerated rebound.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Avoid non-physical or misleading visual cues: the depiction of X-rays as visible light emission, colored scatter streaks, specular reflections, or the use of lighting effects and colors unrelated to attenuation that misrepresent the true appearance and contrast relationships of tissues.

rubrics_visual.d1: High image clarity with well-defined microscopic textures and detailed layering, avoiding blurriness and ghosting.
rubrics_visual.d2: The 3D modeling demonstrates high precision and realistic material texture, with clear and consistent representation of scale hierarchy and structural relationships.
rubrics_visual.d3: Color separation and contrast are naturally controlled, vivid but not glaring; structural types are distinctly differentiated, avoiding color banding and oversaturation.
rubrics_visual.d4: The animation and camera movements are smooth, with seamless transitions and coherent timing, effectively avoiding stutter, motion blur, and jitter.
rubrics_visual.d5: The lighting and depth of field are handled naturally, with soft lighting enhancing volumetric perception and spatial layering; shadows are stable without harsh edges or artifacts.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Completely demonstrate the core scenario of mammography: the breast is compressed between two compression plates, low-dose X-rays penetrate the compressed breast tissue, and the process is exhibited from a microscopic perspective.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Key tissue components and diagnostic elements are complete and distinctly differentiated: cellular structures, glands/ducts, adipose tissue, connective tissue, vascular networks, and calcified regions, each labeled with vivid contrasting colors to delineate tissue types.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Narrative and Camera Angle Consistency in a Single Continuous Dynamic Scene: Employ smooth transitions to present different shooting perspectives centered on the compression process and transient changes at the cellular level.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Compliance with Technical Specifications and Visual Style: Approximately 5 seconds duration, high-resolution output, soft lighting, and realistic shadows enhancing three-dimensional texture.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Scientific Accuracy and Alignment with Educational Objectives: The organizational structure and X-ray interactions correspond to authentic mechanisms, emphasizing the significance of screening and diagnosis as well as potential abnormal features.

任务: High-definition animation showcasing the dynamic mutations of a single breast cancer cell, meticulously depicting the granular euchromatin within the nucleus and the fluidity of the cell membrane. The cell is situated within an active cellular ecosystem, vividly presenting the organic changes of intracellular structures. The depiction is scientifically accurate and suitable for a 5-second microscopic world video.
具体要求: Create a breathtaking microscopic world video focusing on the dynamically evolving breast cancer cell as the central subject. This video employs high-definition animation designed to educate viewers about the progression and staging classification based on disease dissemination. The scene unfolds like a captivating journey, with the cancer cell elegantly mutating and proliferating within a vibrant cellular ecosystem. The imagery vividly illustrates the complex processes of mutation and metastasis of the cell within surrounding tissues. Intricate intracellular structures are finely detailed, showcasing the vivid, organic textures of the nucleus and cytoplasm, and capturing the cancer development and transformation stages through the complex details of molecular interactions. The textures reveal contrasts between healthy and cancerous cells, the granular appearance of nuclear chromatin, and the shimmering fluidity of the cell membrane, all collectively portraying the profound complexity of cancer evolution. Advanced rendering techniques are employed to amplify realism, creating a visually stunning yet scientifically accurate representation that engages and informs viewers about the microscopic intricacies of breast cancer pathogenesis and the critical importance of precise staging and diagnosis in medical contexts.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The morphology and size proportions of cells, nuclei, cytoplasm, and cell membranes correspond to biological scales, reflecting the commonly observed increased nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio and atypia in breast carcinoma cells without exaggerated deformation.
rubrics_scientific.d2: The granular texture and compartmentalized distribution of intranuclear chromatin, as well as the spatial relationship between the nucleolus and the nuclear envelope, are scientifically credible; the fluidity and viscoelasticity of the cell membrane, along with lateral diffusion and membrane undulations, conform to the physical principles of fluid membranes and the coupling mechanisms with the cytoskeleton.
rubrics_scientific.d3: The sequential order and interdependence of events such as mutation, proliferation, invasion, and early metastasis are correctly portrayed, with temporal compression that does not confound physiological rates. Stage-wise evolution is presented through coherent transitions within 5 seconds without claiming instantaneous completion or exaggerating phase changes.
rubrics_scientific.d4: The tumor microenvironment elements (stroma, fibroblasts, immune cells, and microvasculature) and their spatial relationships and interaction mechanisms with cancer cells (adhesion, matrix remodeling, chemotactic migration) are accurately represented.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Avoid visual exaggerations or anthropomorphic metaphors that violate the principles of physical chemistry and biology (e.g., molecules or cells exhibiting uniform directional movement over long distances, super-scaled DNA/protein decorations, energy halos, or environments without thermal noise); the presence of such misleading elements will result in point deductions.

rubrics_visual.d1: High image clarity with strong discernibility of microscopic details; cellular structural hierarchy is distinct, and texture boundaries are sharp and free of blurring.
rubrics_visual.d2: Cell-level modeling and material representation are realistic, with consistent and natural depiction of soft tissue volumetric presence, membrane fluidity, and granular texture.
rubrics_visual.d3: The animation exhibits smooth motion and coherent dynamics, with deformation, fragmentation, and molecular interaction trajectories that are stable and fluid, free from frame skipping and excessive motion blur.
rubrics_visual.d4: Lighting, volumetric scattering, and depth of field are handled naturally; subsurface scattering and translucency effects are accurately rendered; color saturation and contrast are well balanced.
rubrics_visual.d5: Visible artifacts (such as noise, flickering, banding, aliasing, and compression block effects) significantly degrade the visual perception.

rubrics_instruction.d1: The core scene centered on a single target cancer cell is comprehensively presented within an active cellular ecosystem, coherently demonstrating dynamic processes such as mutation and proliferation.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Accurate representation of key microstructural and textural features, highlighting the granular chromatin texture and membrane fluidity, while reflecting the dynamic interplay between the nucleus, cytoplasm, and molecular interactions.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Scientific accuracy and educational clarity are achieved, effectively conveying clues regarding disease progression or staging, with appropriate depiction of the contrast between healthy and cancerous cells to facilitate understanding.

rubrics_instruction.d5: Severe Deviations: The central subject is not prominent or multiple main subjects appear; the perspective shifts away from a microscopic view; image quality falls below high definition; duration deviates noticeably by more than 5 seconds; dynamic processes are missing or unrelated macroscopic elements are introduced.

任务: Precise excision of colonic polyps using soft surgical instruments under a microscope, showcasing the complex texture of soft tissue and the translucent characteristics of the colonic mucosa, highlighting the stark contrast between healthy and cancerous cells, dynamically capturing the subtle interactions between instruments and tissue, with delicate lighting enhancing the three-dimensional effect, presenting an authentic and scientific microscopic perspective.
具体要求: Create a visually stunning and highly detailed microscopic world video that deeply explores the complexities of colorectal cancer treatment. The focus is on the delicate process of early cancer or polyp removal during colonoscopy, where a flexible tube is carefully inserted through the anus. The scene is set to capture the captivating and intricate motions of polyp excision, revealing the precision of microsurgical techniques. Emphasize the complex texture of soft tissue, the translucent properties of the colonic mucosa, and the subtle glimmer of surgical instruments. Include dynamic interactions between instruments and tissue, demonstrating the fine techniques employed in detecting and removing polyps. Highlight the sharp contrast between healthy and cancerous cells, providing a visual representation of accurate cancerous tissue excision. Utilize lighting that reveals subtle tones and shadows to enhance the dimension and depth of the microscopic view, presenting the polyp removal in a realistic yet educational manner. This video aims to offer a fascinating and comprehensive microscopic perspective on the critical early intervention stage in colorectal cancer treatment, enhancing understanding through immersive visualization within the cellular environment.

任务: Microscopic world video showcasing luminescent vaccine particles elegantly binding to a single receptor on the surface of human cells, with clear cell membrane texture and detailed receptor structure. Dynamically capturing the precise interaction process between the vaccine and the cell surface, featuring natural lighting and color gradients to highlight the microscopic biological interactions and dynamic responses on the cell surface.
具体要求: Create an engaging microscopic world video illustrating the complex biological phenomena and subtle interactions occurring during maternal vaccination. The central focus of the scene is a human cell undergoing changes upon encountering vaccine components, exhibiting vitality and intricate detail. This immersive video should depict the dynamic process of vaccine particles elegantly binding to receptor sites on the cell membrane, triggering a cascade of cellular signals critical for immune response development. Emphasize the texture details of the cell membrane, revealing multiple receptors and protein structures to reflect the complexity of cellular interactions. Highlight the luminescent vaccine particles and their detailed coating, demonstrating their precise interaction with cellular structures and capturing the essence of maternal vaccination at the microscopic level. Incorporate realistic color gradients and lighting effects to attract viewer attention, emphasizing cellular dynamic responses and internal adjustments, including the synthesis of new proteins following vaccination.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Receptor-ligand specificity and binding kinetics conform to molecular recognition principles, reflecting diffusion encounter, correct orientation, and reversible binding, with binding/dissociation timescales and affinity ranges being reasonable.

rubrics_scientific.d3: Scientific validity of the causal signaling cascade following receptor activation: receptor conformational change or aggregation → adaptor protein recruitment → kinase cascade/second messenger generation → transcriptional regulation → cellular functional response, with consistent spatial localization and sequential order.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Accuracy of intracellular compartmentalization and temporal sequencing in de novo protein synthesis triggered by vaccination: transcription occurs within the nucleus; translation is executed by ribosomes; secretory and membrane proteins undergo processing and trafficking through the endoplasmic reticulum–Golgi apparatus pathway; temporal sequence and relative durations are appropriate.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Optical and motional physical behaviors adhere to established laws: fluorescence signals correspond consistently with excitation/emission, scattering, and refraction, exhibiting photobleaching and intensity decay; particle and receptor movements display Brownian diffusion and lateral membrane flow, without anomalous acceleration or rigid-body motion.

任务: Microscopic world video focusing on the active protozoa and bacterial communities within a drop of river water, showcasing their shimmering cilia and transparent cell walls as they slowly swim and interact within the clear aquatic environment. The details are sharp and colors vivid, highlighting the dynamics and textures of microorganisms, reflecting the vitality and complexity of the microbial ecosystem, and illustrating the microscopic processes involved in disease tracking and antibiotic resistance transmission. High-definition microscopic perspective, 5-second short video.
具体要求: Create a stunning microscopic world video centered on a drop of river water, revealing its intricate details with an emphasis on how its microbial ecosystem is analyzed to track disease and the spread of antibiotic resistance. The scene depicts a vibrant microbial world, dynamically busy and full of life, moving and interacting in a complex aquatic dance that reflects the unsung heroes of disease surveillance in public health. The video meticulously presents the minute details and textures of various microorganisms, such as the sparkling cilia of protozoa and the transparent cell walls of bacteria, capturing subtle movements and interactions within this miniature ecosystem through vivid colors and high-definition clarity. By immersing the audience in this microscopic world, the video serves not only as an educational tool but also as an inspiring visual experience, enhancing viewers' understanding of the critical role genomic research plays in public health initiatives, as advocated by the Wellcome Sanger Institute and the Genomic Light Project.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Accuracy of Microbial Morphological Features and Scale Relationships: The morphology and size of protozoa—including body shape and cellular structures (cilia/flagella/pseudopodia)—as well as bacterial forms (rod/coccus/spirillum) and their relative size proportions, must fall within authentic micrometer-scale ranges; size relationships relative to background components such as suspended particles, algae/diatoms, and gas bubbles should be appropriately represented.
rubrics_scientific.d2: The locomotion mechanisms and trajectories conform to biological and physical principles: rhythmic ciliary beating drives smooth swimming or rotation, flagellated bacteria exhibit tumbling or combined linear–turning movements; Brownian motion is clearly distinguished from active motility, and deceleration, steering, and absence of inertia under low Reynolds number conditions are appropriately manifested.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Subcellular Structures and Coating Presentation Accuracy: Protozoa predominantly exhibit cell membranes/plasmalemmas rather than thick cell walls; the thickness and transparency of bacterial cell walls and capsules are consistent with expectations; the visibility of nuclei, contractile vacuoles/phagosomes, vacuoles, and cytoplasmic granules corresponds appropriately with the resolution of the microscopy employed.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Reasonable microecological interactions and community dynamics: mechanisms such as predation and grazing flows, chemotactic aggregation, resource competition, and initial biofilm attachment are presented plausibly; antibiotic resistance dissemination occurs via conjugation (contact and plasmid transfer), transduction, or transformation pathways, with time scales and event frequencies not excessively exaggerated.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Visual exaggeration inconsistent with principles of microscopy: treating pseudocoloring as intrinsic color, displaying submolecular details invisible in bright-field imaging, neglecting depth of field and scattering effects leading to excessive sharpness, and employing color mapping that does not correspond to the imaging modality used.

rubrics_visual.d3: Smooth animation and coherent motion, with microbial swimming trajectories and velocity variations exhibiting continuity; collective interactions occur at a natural rhythm without stuttering or frame skipping.

任务: Under the microscope, healthy white blood cells and abnormally distorted cancer cells engage in intense interactions within the blood environment. The white blood cells exhibit smooth, vibrant contours, while the cancer cells display irregular and dark surfaces with dynamic changes in the cell membrane, revealing the microscopic details of their mutual attack.
具体要求: Create a stunning microscopic world video showcasing the exploration of blood cells combating cancer cells, revealing layers invisible to the naked eye. In this captivating scene, observe the dynamic interactions between healthy blood cells and malignant cancer cells, portraying a dramatic cellular battle. Imagine immersing in a vivid world where healthy white blood cells are depicted fighting rapidly dividing cancer cells, highlighting the complex cellular processes of leukemia, lymphoma, and myeloma. Ensure the video is rich in microscopic detail, with special emphasis on cellular texture, illustrating the intricate morphology of both healthy and cancerous cells. Healthy blood cells should appear vibrant and robust, their smooth contours gliding prominently through the membrane environment under low light. In stark contrast, cancer cells should appear abnormally distorted, their surfaces covered with irregular dark tones, creating a visual narrative of the ongoing research and therapeutic advancements presented by Professor Andrew Roberts and discussed by the expert panel at the Walter and Eliza Hall Institute. Through this engaging microscopic journey, elucidate the groundbreaking research led by pioneering scientists at the institute, as they delve into innovative therapies aimed at transforming outcomes for patients with hematologic malignancies.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The scale relationship between cells and the hematological microenvironment is consistent with temporal scaling, with cell diameter, spacing, and motility velocity within physiologically plausible ranges.
rubrics_scientific.d2: The morphology and surface texture of leukocytes, erythrocytes, and tumor cells are accurately represented, with cellular membrane dynamics and characteristics such as pseudopodia and membrane ruffles consistent with biological features.
rubrics_scientific.d3: The immune-related interaction mechanisms are correctly presented, encompassing a rational sequence of processes including chemotaxis, rolling and adhesion, recognition, and phagocytosis/cytotoxicity, while excluding anthropomorphization or non-biological attack effects.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Hemodynamic phenomena and microscale physical principles are accurately represented, with laminar flow and shear stress, as well as Brownian motion and confined diffusion, conforming to the laws of physical chemistry.
rubrics_scientific.d5: The temporal sequence of cell proliferation and death processes is coherent, and the division frequency of cancer cells and the activation/effect timeline of leukocytes maintain their physiological order under time compression.

rubrics_visual.d4: High fidelity in 3D modeling and material realism, with distinct morphological differences among various cell types; accurate representation of surface roughness, elasticity, and membrane adhesion characteristics; consistent subsurface and normal map details.

任务: High-definition visualization of the DNA double helix structure within the breast cell nucleus, focusing on the dynamic interaction between BRCA1 protein and the defective strand. Concisely depict specific drug molecules approaching and interfering with BRCA1 binding. The imagery should be rich in detail, scientifically accurate, and feature smooth animation, suitable for a 5-second microscopic world video.
具体要求: Create a stunning microscopic world video presenting a cellular scene with an emphasis on the interactions of the BRCA1 gene, highlighting its defective strand. The scene unfolds within the breast cell nucleus at a molecular scale visible to the naked eye, illustrating the complex and dynamic behavior of gene transcription as well as the potential interference by specific drug molecules. Engage viewers with remarkable detail and texture, showcasing the DNA’s helical double helix, high-resolution depiction of intricate nucleotide connections, and vivid interactions between proteins and molecular compounds. Capture the elegant orchestration of gene expression through animation that realistically simulates cellular processes, while also demonstrating the invasion of potential preventive drugs, elucidating their mechanisms targeting cancer development. This video should provide an awe-inspiring visual experience that combines scientific accuracy with high artistic value, immersing the audience in a quest that appears to combat breast cancer through preventive science.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The morphology and scale proportions of the DNA double helix and chromatin hierarchical structure conform to established biological standards.
rubrics_scientific.d2: The localization, recruitment, and binding patterns of BRCA1 at sites of DNA damage or defective strands are consistent with established repair mechanisms and molecular interaction principles.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Small molecule drugs approach the target through diffusion, exhibiting rational binding site interactions and affinity trends that reflect competitive or allosteric modulation rather than non-physical driving forces or exaggerated effects.
rubrics_scientific.d4: The temporal sequence of molecular motions and processes is scientifically coherent, with the relative timing and rate compression of Brownian motion, binding–dissociation events, and gene transcription dynamics consistent with intranuclear diffusion and reaction principles.

任务: A 3D animation of healthy bronchial cilia under the microscope, demonstrating the synchronized wave-like motion of the cilia propelling the mucus layer upward, with detailed presentation of ciliary structure and dynamics, set against the bronchial wall background, highlighting the critical role of cilia in airway protection.
具体要求: Create an engaging and scientifically detailed video showcasing the complex world of bronchial cilia and emphasizing their vital function in maintaining respiratory health. The animation immerses viewers in a microscopic scene centered on the dynamic, tiny hair-like structures called cilia that cover the bronchial walls. Observe these intricate structures performing their primary action: a synchronized wave-like motion that propels a protective mucus layer upward, effectively capturing and expelling dust, allergens, and harmful particles to protect the lungs from infection. Emphasize the highly detailed and textured morphology of the cilia, focusing on their structure, motility mechanisms, and vivid representation of their role in the mucociliary clearance system. Highlight contrasting scenes that demonstrate optimal ciliary function in a healthy respiratory environment versus the detrimental effects of pollutants such as smoke or particulate matter on impaired cilia. Provide a seamless educational journey explaining the importance of cilia, the consequences of their damage due to factors like smoking or pollution, and their essential contribution to respiratory health. This comprehensive video will serve as a valuable resource for biology students, medical enthusiasts, and anyone interested in the complexity of the human respiratory system, offering profound insight into the microscopic choreography vital for maintaining lung function. Utilize a series of vivid, educational visuals and animations to effectively convey the protective role of cilia through a high-quality and captivating 3D animation experience, captivating those seeking knowledge of respiratory anatomy, ciliary motion, and lung health by bringing to life the unseen critical functions within the airways.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The hierarchical arrangement and ultrastructural presentation of bronchial epithelium and cilia are accurate (including the axonemal and basal body characteristics of motile cilia, stratification of the ciliary layer and mucus layer, and consistency of tissue orientation with the background).
rubrics_scientific.d2: The rhythmicity and phase coordination of the ciliary population form a metachronal wave propagating in the effective stroke direction; the kinematics of the effective and recovery strokes are appropriate, resulting in net mucus transport toward the oral side.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Scientific validity of mucus-cilia coupling and fluid rheological properties; viscoelastic characteristics of mucus, interlayer relative motion, particle capture, and flow-mediated clearance mechanisms conform to physiological principles; flow direction and velocity are continuous and consistent.
rubrics_scientific.d4: The scale is consistent with the temporal dimension; cilium length and density, beat frequency, mucus thickness, and flow velocity are spatially proportioned to match the microscale. Visual presentation does not cause misleading impressions of size or velocity.
rubrics_scientific.d5: The mechanistic expression contrasting health and damage is scientifically accurate, with precise articulation of the causal chain linking pollution or tobacco exposure to rhythm disruption, frequency alterations, decreased ciliary coverage, or changes in mucus properties, ultimately resulting in reduced clearance efficiency.

rubrics_visual.d1: High image clarity with well-resolved microstructural details; distinct separation between subject and background layers; stable detail rendition without blurring.
rubrics_visual.d2: Excellent ciliary rhythm and fluency, with cilia exhibiting coordinated, wave-like synchronous movement that is continuous and natural; mucus propulsion is steady and uninterrupted; smooth transitions in both lens focus and editing.
rubrics_visual.d3: Realistic 3D modeling and texturing; accurate geometric proportions and sense of scale; credible representation of surface textures and material properties such as viscosity and translucency.

任务: Microscopic world video focusing on the dynamic binding process of immune checkpoint membrane proteins TIGIT and PD-1 on the surface of cancer cells with monoclonal antibodies Ociperlimab and Tislelizumab, delicately showcasing the fine structure of membrane proteins, the synergistic interactions between antibodies, and molecular movements, highlighting intermolecular interactions and microscopic texture changes, centered on a single scene suitable for a 5-second short video.
具体要求: Create a stunning microscopic world video that focuses on the complex interactions between cancer cells, their membrane proteins, and monoclonal antibodies. The central theme of this microscopic scene is the surface of cancer cells, adorned with key immune checkpoints such as PD-1 and TIGIT, which play crucial roles in immune evasion. The scene unfolds to depict the dynamic interplay between Ociperlimab, an experimental monoclonal antibody specifically designed to bind TIGIT, and Tislelizumab, an anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody, emphasizing their synergistic effects on the activation of T cells, natural killer cells, and myeloid cells, ultimately enhancing antitumor activity. Highlight minute anatomical details and textures, illustrating the exquisite structure of membrane proteins and the antibody binding process, portraying the molecular choreography that leads to cellular activation. The video should capture the intricate balance of molecular interactions, microscopic textures, and biological motions, providing viewers with an awe-inspiring insight into cellular mechanisms, complex protein networks, and the potential of innovative cancer therapies, while celebrating scientific breakthroughs in the field of immunotherapy research.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The cellular origin and membrane topology of immune checkpoint molecules (such as PD-1 and TIGIT) are scientifically accurate: they are localized on the outer membrane surface of immune cells including T and NK cells, with correctly oriented extracellular domains, transmembrane segments, and intracellular tails; antibodies are positioned extracellularly and bind exclusively to lateral epitopes.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Antibody-target specificity and synergistic mechanism correctness: Anti-PD-1 antibodies bind exclusively to PD-1, and anti-TIGIT antibodies bind exclusively to TIGIT; the causal chain of blocking inhibitory signals and generating synergistic facilitative effects is clearly delineated without introducing off-target interactions or cross-reactivity.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Reasonable relationships at molecular and cellular scales: membrane thickness, the size ratio between receptors and IgG antibodies, intermolecular distances, and surface densities fall within physiologically appropriate ranges; depth of field and focus in a single scenario do not exaggerate or diminish key structures.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Molecular motion and binding kinetics comply with physicochemical principles: Brownian motion and lateral diffusion within the membrane are natural; the binding-dissociation timescales and diffusion-limited capture processes are plausible, without occurrences of rigid-body linear interpenetration or instantaneous “magnetic adsorption” type binding.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Structural biology details are credible: the folding of the receptor extracellular domain and transmembrane hydrophobic characteristics, glycosylation orientation and flexible loop dynamics, bivalent antibody binding geometry and Fc orientation, as well as the handling of epitope accessibility and spatial exclusion relationships are all appropriately addressed.

rubrics_visual.d1: Image clarity and microscopic details are finely rendered, with sharp boundaries of key structures and textures—such as membrane surface folds, binding sites, and molecular patterns—free from aliasing and jagged edges.
rubrics_visual.d2: The molecular motion and binding process animation is coherent and smooth, with natural kinetic pacing and seamless transitions free of stuttering; the trajectory aligns consistently with interaction feedback, and camera micro-movements remain stable.
rubrics_visual.d3: The 3D modeling and material representation are realistic, with consistent proportions and scale; surface normals and micro-bump details are clearly defined, and parameters such as roughness and scattering correspond accurately to the microscopic texture, with no clipping or overlapping.
rubrics_visual.d4: Illumination, shadows, and depth of field are controlled naturally; microscale subsurface scattering and volumetric light exhibit clear stratification; occlusion lighting and shadow edges remain stable without flickering, overexposure, or black clipping.
rubrics_visual.d5: Color and contrast are consistent and scientifically credible, with high chromatic separation of key objects; saturation and luminance are appropriately controlled to avoid banding, color casts, and excessive noise.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Complete implementation of the core task scenario: Focus on the microenvironment of the cancer cell surface, centered on the interactions between specified immune checkpoint membrane proteins and their corresponding monoclonal antibodies, maintaining a single coherent narrative scenario.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Accurately reflect key elements and relationships: Clearly demonstrate the correct pairing and binding process between the specified membrane proteins (e.g., PD-1, TIGIT) and their corresponding antibodies (e.g., Tislelizumab, Ociperlimab), avoiding mismatches and conceptual confusion.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Demonstration of dynamic processes and cooperative interactions: reflecting molecular motion, temporal binding sequences, and synergistic effects directing immune cell activation, emphasizing the continuity and rhythm of intermolecular interactions.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Enhancement of Microscopic Structures and Textural Details: Delicately rendering the fine structures of membrane proteins, microscopic textural variations, and local interaction details, ensuring both scientific accuracy and aesthetic representation.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Compliance with technical specifications and visual constraints: duration controlled at 5 seconds, stable close-up/macro perspectives, a single focal point with coherent imagery, avoiding multi-scene transitions and irrelevant element insertion.

任务: The dynamic process of dengue hemorrhagic fever virus escaping from the captured vesicle within the host cell, meticulously depicting the interactions among the viral protein envelope, RNA genome, and vesicle membrane, showcasing the critical moments of vesicle membrane rupture and viral release, set against a detailed host cell lipid bilayer environment, using realistic colors and lighting effects to highlight the viral escape action, in a 3D microscopic world style, approximately 5 seconds in duration.
具体要求: Produce a highly engaging and visually striking microscopic world video that illustrates the complex interactions of dengue hemorrhagic fever virus within the host cell. The primary focus is on the dengue virus, with detailed depiction of its unique structural features, including the protein envelope and RNA genome. The scene unfolds as an educational exploration of the virus escaping from the captured vesicle, a critical step for evading cellular defenses and initiating infection. Emphasize the dynamic actions of the virus breaching the host cell, highlighting the dramatic process of releasing and replicating genetic material within the cellular environment. The animation should reveal captivating details such as the intricate textures of viral surface proteins, the host cell’s lipid bilayer membrane, and the complex membrane changes during viral entry. Utilize realistic color schemes and lighting to accentuate the diverse interactions between the virus and cellular components, reinforcing scientific accuracy and visual appeal. Incorporate smooth transitions and streamlined movements to provide an immersive and educational viewing experience, effectively conveying the viral infection mechanism to a broad audience through impactful 3D visualization, thereby enhancing their understanding.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Scientific Validity of the Escape Mechanism: Enveloped viruses primarily escape from acidified vesicles through membrane fusion, exhibiting a coherent sequence of hemifusion intermediates, fusion pore formation, and pore expansion.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Structural and scale accuracy: The size and symmetry of viral particles, surface protein topology, and the ratio of lipid bilayer thickness to vesicle diameter conform to nanoscale measurements.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Rationality of genome release and localization: The genome of positive-sense RNA enveloped viruses is released into the cytoplasm without erroneous entry into the nucleus; this process temporally follows the fusion event closely and does not exhibit exaggerated immediate replication.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Biophysical Consistency: Membrane continuity and curvature variations, protein-membrane interactions, and molecular movements adhere to principles of energy minimization and diffusion/Brownian motion, with motion velocity and damping consistent with the intracellular viscous environment.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Penalty for Major Scientific Deviations: Describing extensive vesicle membrane rupture or bursting as the primary viral envelope escape mechanism, or designating the positive-sense RNA genome nuclear targeting as a critical post-release localization.

rubrics_visual.d2: Accurate rendering of lighting and materials, with natural microscale scattering, reflection, and refraction; consistent depth of field and volumetric lighting transitions; no overexposure or black clipping.
rubrics_visual.d3: Animation smoothness and physical coherence; dynamic rhythm is reasonable in membrane tension variation, structural rupture, and release; keyframe interpolation is smooth without jitter or motion blur.
rubrics_visual.d4: 3D modeling accuracy and material texture quality; structural proportions and topology consistency; well-executed normal and displacement map details; absence of mesh penetration, geometry artifacts, and texture stretching.
rubrics_visual.d5: Color rendering and contrast appear natural, with clear separation between the subject and background layers; saturation and color temperature are consistent; visual focus is effectively directed to critical moments, avoiding color bleeding and contamination.

rubrics_instruction.d2: Accurately depicts the specified microscopic entity and its key structures and interactions, emphasizing the relationships among the protein capsid, RNA/nucleic acid genome, vesicle membrane, and lipid bilayer membrane, reflecting scientific accuracy and educational value.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Conforms to the stylistic requirements of a 3D microscopic world with realistic color, lighting, and shading; features prominently dynamic subject movement; exhibits smooth and natural camera work and transitions; delivers an overall visually impactful experience.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Meets approximately 5 seconds duration and perspective specifications, with the primary subject stable and clearly visible, framing focused on the core action, and information conveyed concisely.

任务: From a microscopic perspective, pneumonia bacteria inside the alveoli move along and penetrate the alveolar wall surface, with clear texture on the bacterial surface, delicate alveolar mucosal texture, slight ciliary movement, realistic fluid flow in the surrounding environment, high resolution, scientifically accurate, 5-second short video.

The scene should depict how these microscopic pathogens interact and proliferate within the alveoli. Capture the dynamic process of bacteria penetrating the alveolar wall, illustrating their role in causing respiratory complications post-discharge. Emphasize the complex details and textures of the respiratory environment, highlighting the intricate alveolar structure, ciliary motion, and delicate mucosa. Ensure viewers can observe the vivid texture of the bacterial surface, diffusion of surrounding fluids, and subtle changes in adjacent tissues, all presented in clear high resolution to provide both educational value and scientific insight into the pathophysiology of pneumonia after hospital discharge.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Scale and Proportion Accuracy: The relative dimensions and spacing of the pathogen, alveolar epithelial cells, capillaries, and alveolar wall thickness conform to established micrometer-scale ranges.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Correct hierarchical microanatomical and spatial relationships of the alveolus: the arrangement and continuity from the alveolar lumen—surfactant and liquid film—type I/type II epithelium—fused basement membrane—to the capillary endothelium demonstrate scientific validity and credibility.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Pathogen adhesion, migration, and penetration mechanisms: accurately represent authentic pathways such as receptor-mediated adhesion, paracellular routes, or transcellular transport, without depicting active motility or instantaneous membrane disruption inconsistent with the bacterial type.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Physiological process time series validity: within a 5-second duration, only stages such as adhesion, micro-displacement, and early invasion progression are observed, without the occurrence of a complete bacterial division cycle or rapid large-scale proliferation.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Microscopic fluid dynamics and molecular motion conform to physicochemical principles: accurate representation of liquid film wetting and surface tension effects, particle trajectories dominated by Brownian motion and diffusion, and fluid behavior primarily characterized by slow laminar flow.

任务: Extreme close-up of the surface of a copper intrauterine device (IUD) releasing copper ions, surrounded by active endometrial cells. The intricate metallic texture contrasts sharply with the cellular structures, while simultaneously showcasing the smooth and flexible edges of the hormonal IUD. The video delicately presents the microscopic effects of hormones on endometrial cells, illustrating the chemical and biological interactions between the IUD and cells within the microenvironment.
具体要求: Create a stunning microscopic world video that reveals the detailed interactions between hormonal and copper intrauterine devices (IUDs) within the female reproductive system. The scene should vividly depict the uterine environment where these devices operate, highlighting their complex mechanisms in contraception. Emphasize the delicate tissue interface of the endometrial lining as the IUD releases hormones or copper ions, generating a sperm-hostile environment and disrupting fertilization. Capture the subtle effects of estrogen and progesterone on the uterine wall, simulating their roles throughout the menstrual cycle, reflecting the fluidity of cellular processes and distinct molecular interactions. Prioritize extreme close-up shots to reveal surface textures and contrast the smooth, flexible edges of the hormonal IUD with the complex, coiled structure of the copper IUD, ensuring an exceptionally clear contrast between the metallic device and the vibrant cellular background. This video should possess profound educational value, encompassing the synergy of biological and mechanical elements in modern contraceptive technology, tailored as a comprehensive visual experience for medical education and digital content marketing.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Accurate expression of contraceptive physiological mechanisms: Copper ions create a localized inflammatory and toxic microenvironment within the uterine cavity that is detrimental to sperm, reducing sperm motility and fertilization capacity; progestogens predominantly thicken cervical mucus and decrease endometrial receptivity, with the causal chain and relative importance consistent with medical consensus.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Scale proportions and temporal sequences are appropriate: the surface texture of the IUD, cells and organelles, as well as molecular/ionic sizes and spatial relationships are accurate; the temporal progression of diffusion, cellular responses, and menstrual cycle-related changes is presented in a scientifically coherent manner.
rubrics_scientific.d3: The release and diffusion of ions and hormones comply with physicochemical principles: oxidation/corrosion of the copper surface leads to sustained copper ion release, with concentration gradients and diffusion kinetics consistent and plausible; the rate of hormone sustained release from the reservoir/coating and its local distribution are reasonable, exhibiting no non-physical behavior.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Accurate representation of tissue and cellular structures: clear delineation of endometrial epithelium, glands, and stromal layers; cellular morphology and polarity, intercellular junctions, and vascular distribution consistent with histological characteristics; interface between instruments and endometrium as well as positioning within the uterine cavity conforming to anatomical relationships.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Visualization of molecular and cellular interactions: the intensity and spatiotemporal scale of hormone receptor binding, signal transduction, and changes in gene expression are appropriate; cellular behaviors (secretion, proliferation, apoptosis) and dose-response relationships are credible.

rubrics_visual.d1: High image clarity with superior resolution of microscopic details; key textures and cellular structure edges are well-defined, and noise is effectively controlled.
rubrics_visual.d2: The animation demonstrates smoothness and natural microdynamic behavior, with continuous and stable diffusion of molecules and ions, as well as uninterrupted cellular activity and deformation, free from jittering or stuttering.
rubrics_visual.d3: High accuracy in 3D modeling and realistic material rendering, with clearly distinguishable surface properties (roughness, reflectivity, elasticity) between metallic and biological tissues.
rubrics_visual.d4: Lighting and shadow effects as well as depth of field are handled naturally; subtle light scattering and subsurface scattering are well-coordinated; out-of-focus blur is clean, with a strong sense of spatial layering.
rubrics_visual.d5: The color and contrast performance are scientifically consistent and stable, with moderate saturation and accurate white balance; the contrast between metallic and cytoplasmic tones is realistic and harmonious without appearing abrupt.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Complete implementation of the core scenario and main subject: Present an extreme close-up of the intrauterine microenvironment and the endometrial interface, clearly demonstrating the key surface features of two specified types of intrauterine devices (copper and hormonal) along with the surrounding active cells.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Accurately represent the contraceptive mechanisms and interactions: demonstrate the release and diffusion of specified active agents such as copper ions and hormones, the formation of an inhospitable environment for the endometrium and sperm, the dynamics of cellular processes, and molecular-level interactions, with clear causal relationships to enhance educational value.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Compliance with Perspective and Technical Specifications: Prioritize extreme close-ups that emphasize the clear contrast between metallic textures and cellular structures; ensure camera stability, consistent focus, and adherence to prescribed guidelines regarding scale, resolution, and duration.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Completeness of elements and appropriate contrast: simultaneously includes the core structural and material characteristics of both device categories, highlights the contrast between smooth flexibility and complex texture, and ensures the presence of relevant cellular, tissue interface, and microenvironmental components.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Scientific and Instructional Deviations: Presence of physiological mechanism errors, omission of specified subjects or key processes, perspective deviation from the microscopic context, failure to reflect interactions and contrasts between devices and cells/molecules.

任务: From a microscopic perspective, radioactive seeds release radiation during brachytherapy, with the radiation acting on cancer cells, depicting the morphological changes of cancer cells at the moment of irradiation and the detailed cellular environment. The visuals are vivid, highlighting the microscopic dynamics of energy transfer, with a duration of approximately 5 seconds.
具体要求: Create a stunning and highly detailed video showcasing the microscopic world of brachytherapy, focusing on the complex mechanisms of this advanced cancer treatment at the cellular level. In this captivating scene, observe the tiny radioactive seeds carefully placed near the tumor within the body, emphasizing their potent effect on malignant cells. As the microscopic drama unfolds, viewers will be drawn to the visual spectacle of cancer cells being bombarded by radiation, illustrating the destruction and eventual death of these cells. Highlight the rich details and textures of the cellular environment, capturing the morphological changes of cancer cells before and after treatment. The interaction of radiation creates a vivid display of energy transfer, with a colorful palette illustrating the process of cellular breakdown. By visiting http://www.nucleushealth.com/?utm_source=youtube&utm_medium=video-description&utm_campaign=brachy-050114, this video can be used as an educational tool for patient education or content marketing, exploring the fascinating intersection of science and art, and showcasing the life-saving procedures of #brachytherapy, #cancertreatment, and #radiotherapy. ANH13116.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Physical Consistency Between Radioactive Seeds and Dose Distribution: The seed geometry and implantation position are appropriate; radiation emission is approximately isotropic and attenuates with distance and tissue, resulting in steep local dose gradients.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Accurate depiction of the mechanisms underlying radiation interactions with biological media: ionization and excitation, free radical generation, secondary electrons/δ-rays, and the presentation and subcellular localization of DNA single- and double-strand breaks.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Scale and temporal self-consistency: the dimensional ratios of structures such as cells and nuclei are accurate; particle trajectories and ionization cluster spacings are within the micro- to nanoscale range; energy deposition occurs on the femtosecond to nanosecond timescale, whereas morphological changes happen on the minute to hour timescale, presenting a logically coherent temporal compression.
rubrics_scientific.d4: The visualization of energy and dose is physically consistent: energy deposition is represented by discrete tracks and ionization clusters, with dose rapidly attenuating over distance; color and brightness mappings correspond accurately to energy or dose intensity, avoiding representations of radiation as macroscopic flames, shock waves, or slowly drifting beams.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Scientific Performance at the Tumor Microenvironment and Cellular Population Level: Cell density and heterogeneity, vasculature and hypoxic regions, extracellular matrix and stromal architecture are appropriate and consistent with the spatial relationship to radiosensitivity or dose distribution; the observable changes in cell death pathways correspond to mechanisms such as apoptosis and mitotic catastrophe.

rubrics_visual.d1: Image clarity and richness of microscopic detail levels, with sharp edges free from excessive sharpening, and fine structures remain stable and discernible during motion.
rubrics_visual.d2: Color rendering and contrast are naturally unified, with color encoding consistent and hierarchical for energy transfer and radiation effects, avoiding oversaturation and color bias.
rubrics_visual.d3: Animation exhibits smoothness and temporal coherence, with seamless transitions in deformation, fracture, and particle/volume effects, free from jittering, motion blur artifacts, or frame skipping.
rubrics_visual.d4: 3D modeling and material rendering are credible, with consistent scale and geometric details; translucent and microsurface properties are presented appropriately.
rubrics_visual.d5: Natural lighting and volumetric effects, precise control of depth of field and exposure, prominent key focal points, and the synergistic enhancement of spatial layering through dynamic lighting and shadowing.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Complete implementation of the core mechanisms and scenario: close-range radiotherapy process from a microscopic perspective, where radioactive seeds adjacent to the tumor emit radiation that acts upon cancer cells.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Coverage of Key Elements and Microscopic Details: Presentation of energy transfer dynamics, comparative analysis of morphological changes in cancer cells before and after treatment, and clear depiction of cellular microenvironment texture and structure.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Clear and coherent dynamic process: the temporal progression of radiation emission—interaction—cellular damage/death is explicitly defined, emphasizing microscopic dynamics with vivid coloration to enhance information expression.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Technical Specification Matching: Micro/close-up perspective with stable composition, approximately 5 seconds in duration, featuring clear and discernible imagery focused at the cellular level.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Severe deviation penalty: Instances involving subjects or mechanisms that contradict the instructions (e.g., non-microscopic manifestations, absence of interaction between radioactive seeds and cancer cells, significantly discrepant duration, introduction of irrelevant plot elements, or manifest scientific inaccuracies).

任务: From a microscopic perspective, depict the rapid and irregular contractions of atrial cardiomyocytes within the atrium, highlighting atrial fibrillation caused by chaotic electrical signals. Present the myocardial fiber structure and charge flow in detail, with soft light waves in the environment symbolizing medical intervention. The video should feature high-definition close-ups and vivid colors, reflecting the microscopic pathological characteristics of atrial fibrillation and the hope for treatment.
具体要求: Create an immersive microscopic world video that deeply explores the complexity of atrial fibrillation, illustrating its impact and treatment within the cardiac environment. The main content should meticulously portray the atrium—the upper chamber of the heart—capturing the rapid, chaotic contractions characteristic of fibrillation, resulting in intermittent and irregular heartbeats. The scene should vividly depict the disordered electrical signals causing ineffective atrial fibrillation, while contrasting this with the heart's normal rhythm represented by stable, synchronized pulses. The actions in the scene should include a clear contrast between normal contractions and fibrillatory contractions, emphasizing the critical disruption fibrillation causes to cardiac function. Details should highlight the intricate texture of myocardial fibers and cellular structures, showcasing the charge-rich environment within the atrium. Common medical interventions, such as catheter ablation or pharmacological treatment, should be illustrated as calming waves or constructive forces restoring rhythmic harmony. This video should convey the urgency and severity of treating atrial fibrillation, using bright colors, precise details, and high-definition close-ups to bring the microscopic world to life and enhance viewers' understanding of the risks and solutions associated with this condition.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Accurate correlation between electrical activity patterns and mechanical performance: Sinus rhythm is characterized by stable, synchronous propagation wavefronts and effective contraction; atrial fibrillation is characterized by rapid, disorganized depolarization caused by multiple focal or reentrant circuits, resulting in asynchronous and inefficient contraction.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Accurate representation of the atrial and myocardial microstructure and scale relationship: cardiomyocyte morphology with striations, intercalated discs, and gap junctions is appropriately depicted; fiber orientation reflects anisotropic conduction; the atrial wall and tissue layers, as well as their proportions relative to surrounding structures, are reasonable.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Excitation–contraction coupling time series are physiologically coherent: action potentials and ion channel activities precede calcium transients and subsequent cross-bridge cycling leading to contraction; during fibrillation, disruption of electro-calcium coupling correlates with variability in contraction amplitude.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Biophysical Expression of Charge and Ion Flux: Primarily characterized by transmembrane ionic currents and cell-to-cell coupled propagation, without free diffusion in the form of extracellular sparks or beams; visualization color coding complies with fundamental bioelectrical principles.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Accurate representation of medical intervention mechanisms: catheter ablation precisely targets and isolates trigger foci or abnormal conduction pathways to terminate disorganized electrical activity; pharmacological agents modulate ion channels or atrioventricular nodal conduction to restore or control cardiac rhythm, resulting in improved electromechanical synchrony post-intervention.

rubrics_visual.d1: High image clarity with well-resolved microstructural details; sharp edges of key structures; clear hierarchical delineation of fibers and cellular substructures; precise focus on critical regions; minimal noise and compression artifacts.
rubrics_visual.d2: Excellent animation timing and motion continuity, with sufficient rapid and irregular dynamic sampling; stable frames without stuttering or ghosting. Clear contrast between normal and abnormal rhythm patterns, with smooth camera transitions.
rubrics_visual.d3: The 3D modeling and material rendering are realistic, with high geometric accuracy of the structure, clean topology without interpenetrations, fiber orientation consistent with anisotropy, and natural representation of translucency and microsurface roughness.
rubrics_visual.d4: Color mapping and contrast are natural and accurate, with moderate saturation, no highlight clipping, and well-defined shadow details; color coding enhances information readability, clearly distinguishing different states and energy/charge flow.
rubrics_visual.d5: Lighting and volumetric effects are realistic, with consistent light direction and energy; volumetric scattering and subsurface scattering are naturally represented, and shadow contact edges are accurate; depth of field and macro scale are matched without excessive blurring of key details.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Fully accomplish the core scenarios and thematic intents required by the task.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Accurately depict key dynamic processes and comparative relationships, emphasizing the disruptive effects of abnormal mechanisms on function and the restorative impact of interventions.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Clearly demonstrate the specified microstructures and key elements (structural texture, signal/energy flow, environmental symbolism)
rubrics_instruction.d4: Meets the requirements for duration, perspective, shot composition, and image technical specifications (micro perspective, high-definition close-ups, color expression).
rubrics_instruction.d5: Conveying educational and informational intent through narrative expression that reflects risk, urgency, and therapeutic hope.

任务: Red blood cells, white blood cells, and platelets flowing within the bloodstream from a microscopic perspective, meticulously showcasing the biconcave morphology of red blood cells, the surface texture of white blood cells, and the discoid structure of platelets. Cells dynamically float within blood vessels, interacting with the vascular endothelium and other cells. The imagery features vivid colors and clear textures, with the background depicting the inner walls of blood vessels, emphasizing the natural motion of cells and the active state of blood components during transfusion.
具体要求: Create a visually stunning video that deeply explores the microscopic world of blood components and the transfusion process, capturing the intricate beauty of this vital phenomenon. The primary focus is on various cellular elements in blood, including vibrant red blood cells, dynamic white blood cells, and delicate platelets, all presented within their natural habitat in the circulatory system. The scene should illustrate the transformative impact of transfusion, demonstrating how these blood components interact with and integrate into the recipient’s system, combating diseases such as leukemia, hemophilia, anemia, sickle cell disease, and cancer. Intimately focus on the detailed textures and structures of each cell type, highlighting the flexible biconcave surface of red blood cells, the rugged exterior of white blood cells, and the small discoid shape of platelets, vividly portraying the physiological marvels of their replication and function. Enrich the visual narrative through the inherent vivid colors and subtle movements of these microscopic entities, ensuring viewers gain a profound appreciation of blood anatomy and physiology, as well as the critical role of transfusion in sustaining life.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Accurate reproduction of cellular morphology and ultrastructure: erythrocytes exhibit biconcave shape and flexible deformability; leukocytes possess multilobed nuclei and rough surface texture; platelets are small, anucleate, disk-shaped or fragmented.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Consistency of scale and quantitative relationships: relative size proportions of cells, hematocrit-to-plasma ratio, vessel diameter in relation to cell density, and the relative sparsity of leukocytes and platelets.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Accuracy of hemodynamics and cell kinetics: under laminar flow conditions, erythrocyte axial migration and deformation, leukocyte and platelet margination accompanied by rolling and transient adhesion, with collisions and adhesion governed by physicochemical principles.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Correct cellular functions and interaction processes: the leukocyte rolling–adhesion–transmigration sequence; the platelet adhesion–activation–aggregation sequence and fibrin formation timeline; erythrocytes perform gas transport without proliferation within blood vessels, avoiding depiction of cell division/proliferation within the vascular lumen.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Exaggeration or misrepresentation of transfusion effects: depicting transfusion as directly eradicating cancer cells or curing genetic disorders, or neglecting ABO/Rh blood typing, crossmatching, immunological compatibility, and the risk of hemolysis.

rubrics_visual.d1: High image clarity and distinguishable microscopic details, with clear cell boundaries and surface textures; plasma microstructure appears clean, without noticeable noise or excessive sharpening.
rubrics_visual.d2: Color saturation and contrast are natural and realistic; the hues of red blood cells, white blood cells, and platelets are distinctly differentiated without exaggeration; the overall color temperature is harmonious with the vascular wall background.
