rubrics_visual.d3: Animation exhibits smooth fluidity and coherent microscopic dynamics; cells float, roll, interact, and undergo slight deformations with seamless rhythm; trajectories and velocity changes are natural, without frame skipping or jittering.
rubrics_visual.d4: The 3D modeling exhibits high accuracy and realistic material rendering, with appropriate cell morphology proportions and normal map details. Subsurface scattering and translucency effectively represent the optical properties of blood media, while the texture of the vascular endothelium remains consistent.
rubrics_visual.d5: Lighting and shadow effects, as well as depth of field processing, are natural; volumetric scattering, soft shadows, and focal layering enhance spatial perception; highlights are free from overexposure, and details are preserved in shadowed areas.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Fully implement the microscopic vascular scene in accordance with the thematic requirements, focusing on blood components and the transfusion process, with the background depicting the inner vascular wall; maintain consistent microscopic scale and perspective.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Accurately presenting the three key elements—erythrocytes, leukocytes, and platelets—along with their characteristic morphological and textural features, with vivid coloration and clear detailing.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Reflects the natural dynamics and interactions within the blood flow environment, with cells floating along the flow and engaging in appropriate interactions with the vascular endothelium and other cells, maintaining narrative continuity.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Clearly articulate the visual narrative of transfusion intervention and integration, depicting the fusion and functional role of blood components within the recipient’s system, while balancing scientific accessibility and aesthetic impact.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Conformance with the specified technical specifications and performance requirements of the task, including duration, camera angle/depth of field, resolution and texture clarity, as well as color consistency.

任务: Microscopically display healthy epidermal cells dynamically covering the skin injury area, with cells intertwining and proliferating to initiate epidermal repair. The background clearly presents the dermal collagen fiber network and subcutaneous adipose tissue, highlighting the cellular and tissue hierarchical structural details and color contrasts, while a simple background emphasizes the repair process of the skin layers.
具体要求: Create a stunning and intricate microscopic world video focusing on the medical marvel of skin transplantation surgery. The primary theme of the video should be the detailed stratification of human skin, magnifying the epidermis, dermis, and subcutaneous tissue, showcasing the rich tapestry formed by these layers along with spectacular close-ups of their structural complexity. The scene unfolds at the cellular level, illustrating the meticulous process of skin transplantation, with an emphasis on healthy skin precisely covering the injured skin area. The sequence should artistically capture the surgical precision, depicting vivid microscopic cells intertwining, proliferating, and initiating the repair process, demonstrating the transition of new life integrating into the existing network. Emphasize contrasting textures and colors: the epidermis presenting its protective volcanic-like topography; the dermis revealing its collagen fibers and vascular network; and the subcutaneous tissue displaying its soft adipose layer and complex neural network. This video should reflect the subtle nuances of the skin restoration journey, celebrating the natural healing process through dynamic colors and movements. This detailed portrayal should be both educational and visually captivating, rendered with the aesthetics of biological art.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The anatomical layer boundaries and relative thicknesses of the epidermis, dermis, and subcutaneous tissue are accurate; the reticular structure of collagen fibers and the pattern of fat lobules and fibrous septa are clearly defined; the layer-specific distribution of microvasculature and nerves conforms to histological characteristics.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Epidermal cells exhibit correct directional migration toward the wound edge, adhesion, and proliferation, continuously covering the wound surface during the re-epithelialization process, in coordination with dermal matrix remodeling and angiogenesis consistent with physiological healing.
rubrics_scientific.d3: The relative sizes and layer thickness proportions of cells, collagen bundles, capillaries, and adipocytes are appropriate, with cell motility and blood flow velocity consistent with microphysiological scales; coloration or labeling corresponds accurately to structure and function, avoiding misleading codification.
rubrics_scientific.d4: The integration process between the transplanted tissue and the recipient site is presented scientifically, demonstrating reasonable temporal sequence and directionality in interface adhesion, basement membrane and extracellular matrix reconstruction, as well as vascular anastomosis and reperfusion restoration.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Penalty for Evident Scientific Errors: Demonstrations exhibiting intradermal vascular presence, inversion of dermis and subcutaneous tissue layers, transient occurrence of supra-physiological proliferation rates, or active peristaltic movement of collagen bundles that are inconsistent with biological facts.

rubrics_visual.d1: High image clarity and microscopic detail resolution with sharply defined cellular morphology and layer boundaries, exhibiting no blurring.
rubrics_visual.d2: High fidelity in 3D modeling and material realism, with appropriate organization hierarchy and fiber network proportions, and consistent surface texture.
rubrics_visual.d3: The animation motion and interactions are coherent, with stable rhythms of cell migration and proliferation; transitions are smooth without jittering or stretching artifacts.
rubrics_visual.d4: Color and contrast are naturally unified with clear hierarchical differentiation; texture and tonal contrasts emphasize structural differences without distortion.
rubrics_visual.d5: Proper control of lighting and depth of field, natural transitions between volumetric light and shadows, with the focus emphasizing the subject while maintaining a clean background.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Accurately depicts the key process of healthy epidermal cells covering the wound area under the microscope, demonstrating the continuous dynamics of cell interconnection, proliferation, and initial repair.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Accurately reproduces the three-layer structure and hierarchical relationships of the skin, clearly distinguishing the key features of the epidermis, dermis (collagen fibers and vascular network), and subcutaneous tissue (adipose tissue and neural network).
rubrics_instruction.d3: The camera angle and framing are consistent with the microscopic scale and medical surgical context, maintaining stable focus on key areas. The sequence demonstrates the progression of transplant precision and the stages of integration between donor and recipient tissues.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Background and composition serve the hierarchical contrast and restoration process, with the dermal collagen network and subcutaneous fat presented in a clear and concise manner to avoid interference with the subject.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Texture and color contrast enhance educational value and aesthetic expression, ensuring clear information delivery and a rhythm and duration that align with the requirements of process presentation.

任务: Microscopic visualization of the close connection between sutures and synthetic mesh structures in the vaginal tissue near the female bladder neck, highlighting the details of fascial fibers, blood vessels, and connective tissue. Clearly demonstrate the microscopic repositioning of the urethra and its connection with the inguinal ligament, set against a biological tissue microscopic background. Dynamically portray structural stability and cellular interactions under surgical intervention.
具体要求: Create an engaging microscopic world video showcasing the complex anatomy and physiological functions of the female urinary system, with a focus on the cellular architecture of the bladder and urethra. Provide an in-depth depiction of stress urinary incontinence surgical treatment, meticulously illustrating the precise placement of sutures in the vaginal tissue near the bladder neck and their strategic connection to the inguinal ligament, offering vivid insights into the surgical intervention. Capture the transformative movement of urethral repositioning to reduce urine flow under pressure, clearly displaying interactions with synthetic sling materials and their anchoring beneath the urethra and bladder neck. Emphasize prominent textures and details such as the fibrous nature of fascial tissue, the mesh composition of the synthetic sling, and cellular behavior during urethral support. Highlight the clear visibility of tissue sutures, demonstrating microscopic-level interactions, with comprehensive close-ups of vascular and connective tissue connections, as well as the dynamic and complex interplay between biological materials and synthetic elements. Provide detailed depiction of the cellular layers and illustrate the maintenance of structural integrity during and after surgery, reflecting a profound understanding of anatomical precision and medical innovation.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Accuracy of spatial topology and course of key pelvic floor anatomical structures: the relative positions, layers, and continuity of the bladder neck, urethra, anterior vaginal wall, pelvic fascia, and supporting ligaments conform to anatomical standards; the urethral repositioning and its connections with surrounding structures are realistically presented.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Scientific Consistency of Surgical Technique and Implant Materials: The suture trajectory and fixation points conform to clinically common pathways and safety margins; the synthetic sling’s route and anchoring method align with procedural paradigms such as retropubic or transobturator approaches, functioning to provide support under intra-abdominal pressure transmission rather than exerting continuous compression.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Accuracy of Histological and Cellular-Level Details: Clear differentiation between urothelial and vaginal epithelial types and layers; realistic texture and fiber orientation of the basement membrane, lamina propria, smooth muscle, and connective tissue; capillary diameters, erythrocyte sizes, and blood flow patterns consistent with microcirculatory characteristics.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Multiscale proportionality and spatiotemporal dynamics are appropriate: the size ratios among cells, collagen fibers, blood vessels, suture fibers, and pores fall within histological ranges; the rates and sequences of cell adhesion, fibroblast migration, and collagen deposition during surgery and the early postoperative period align with physiological timescales; the responses of urinary flow dynamics and intra-abdominal pressure variations conform to physiological principles.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Penalty for Scientific Inaccuracies: Points will be deducted for evident anatomical or physiological deviations (e.g., anchoring the suspensory ligament to unrelated ligaments, distortion of mesh-to-cell or mesh-to-vessel proportions, urine flow traversing parenchymal tissue or contradicting pressure gradients, materials or tissues exhibiting non-physiological luminescence or acoustic effects).

rubrics_visual.d1: High image clarity and resolution of microscopic details, with distinct tissue texture, fiber orientation, reticular structures, and cellular layer boundaries, exhibiting no defocus or blurring.
rubrics_visual.d2: High accuracy in 3D modeling and realistic material fidelity; well-structured organizational hierarchy and coherent topology of composite mesh materials; appropriate surface roughness and detailed normal mapping; harmonious scale relationships without noticeable low-poly appearance.
rubrics_visual.d3: Lighting and volumetric rendering are natural; subsurface scattering and soft shadows are accurate; depth of field control corresponds to the microscopic scale; reflections and specular highlights are free from overexposure and do not obscure details.
rubrics_visual.d4: The animation is smooth and consistent with microscale dynamics, with seamless deformation and contact transitions. Camera movement is stable, exhibiting no jitter, flicker, or motion blur caused by insufficient temporal sampling.
rubrics_visual.d5: Colorimetry and contrast are naturally balanced; white balance is accurate; saturation is moderate; tonal gradation is clearly defined, enhancing the recognition of tissues and materials; no evident color cast is present.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Fully achieve the core objectives of the microsurgical scenario, accurately presenting the specified anatomical region, surgical intervention pathway, and the primary trajectory of repositioning.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Key elements and details are clearly identifiable and interrelated: fascial fibers, blood vessels, connective tissue, cellular layers, suture lines, synthetic mesh materials, and their anchorage relationships with surrounding tissues.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Comprehensive demonstration of dynamic processes and functional changes: evolution of structural stability before, during, and after surgical intervention; microscopic interactions among materials, tissues, and cells; and pressure-related functional performance.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Alignment of Perspective and Technical Specifications with Instructions: consistent microscopic perspective, stable camera framing and focus, duration and pacing conforming to instructions, background depicting biological tissue microenvironment, scale and annotations accurately matched.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Points will be deducted for content that significantly violates instructions, including anatomical and connective relationship errors, deviations in surgical logic, omission of key elements, irrelevance to the subject matter, or scale misplacement causing misunderstanding.

任务: At the cellular level, an insulin pump precisely releases translucent insulin molecules through pulsatile microcatheters. The insulin molecules diffuse within the bloodstream and bind to glucose particles. The scene showcases the intricate textures of cellular tissues and dynamic bioluminescent effects, highlighting the molecular interactions between insulin and glucose molecules, as well as the microcatheter’s pulsations simulating a heartbeat rhythm, revealing detailed microscopic structures and molecular motion.
具体要求: Create a breathtaking microscopic world video focusing on the complex operation of the insulin pump at the cellular level. The main content should be a highly detailed 3D depiction illustrating how insulin molecules are precisely released into the bloodstream via microcatheters. The scene unfolds within the dynamic arterial system of a diabetic patient, demonstrating a battery-powered mechanism that simulates the heartbeat rhythm to drive insulin delivery. Emphasize capturing the molecular interactions between insulin molecules and glucose particles, highlighting their contrasting and smooth motions as they bind to cellular receptors. Accentuate the exquisite textures of biological tissues, the translucent characteristics of insulin during diffusion, and the fine structure of the microcatheters. Lighting should reflect through the fluid medium to create a dazzling bioluminescent display, allowing viewers to witness the seamless integration of technology within the human body and its critical role in maintaining blood glucose levels. This video is intended as an educational tool for Nucleus Medical Media, providing compelling visual content to enhance patient understanding of insulin pump functionality and its vital contribution to diabetes management.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The molecular mechanism of insulin and glucose regulation is accurately presented: insulin binds to insulin receptors on the target cell membrane, initiating signaling pathways that promote glucose entry into the cell via transporters, without depicting a direct binding between insulin and glucose.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Scale and Order of Magnitude Appropriateness: The relative sizes, densities, and spatial intervals among insulin, glucose, plasma proteins, erythrocytes, and vascular endothelium conform to biological orders of magnitude.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Molecular motion and transport processes conform to physico-chemical laws: exhibiting Brownian motion under pulsatile blood flow convection conditions, as well as the effects of diffusion gradients and shear fields on molecular cloud morphology.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Accuracy of anatomical and device correlation: Precise depiction of arterial wall layering and endothelial continuity; appropriate microcatheter structure and outlet positioning; insulin pump infusion pathway and operational principles conform to clinical standards (subcutaneous tissue diffusion and absorption into the bloodstream rather than direct intravascular arterial release).
rubrics_scientific.d5: Optical and fluorescence visualization adhere to scientific constraints: the fluorescence origin is based on labeling or indication without altering biochemical properties, and the scattering, refraction, and attenuation of light within fluids and tissues are represented realistically.

rubrics_visual.d1: Image clarity and detailed representation of microstructural features are excellent, with clear and unblurred visualization of cellular tissue patterns, microtubule boundaries, and molecular morphology.
rubrics_visual.d2: The animation is smooth and rhythmically consistent, with coherent and stable pulsation release, molecular diffusion, and receptor-binding movements, free from unnatural acceleration, deceleration, or stuttering.
rubrics_visual.d3: The lighting and volumetric effects are natural; bioluminescence, refraction, scattering, and depth of field transitions are well-coordinated; specular highlights are not overexposed, and shadow areas exhibit clear tonal gradation.
rubrics_visual.d4: High realism in 3D modeling and material rendering, with accurate representation of the textures of the cell membrane, blood medium, and microcatheter materials; the insulin molecule exhibits appropriate translucency and proportional scaling.
rubrics_visual.d5: Color and contrast are well controlled; fluorescent hues harmonize with blood tones; primary and secondary elements exhibit clear contrast while avoiding oversaturation.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Comprehensively present the core processes and scenarios of insulin release at the cellular scale via a microcatheter pulsatile insulin pump, including diffusion in the bloodstream and interaction with glucose.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Accurately includes key elements with clear discernibility: microcatheter and pump structure, translucent insulin molecules, glucose particles and cellular receptors, arterial blood flow and tissue texture, bioluminescence and light reflection.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Reflects rhythmic-driven pulsatile release synchronized with the heartbeat rhythm; molecular diffusion, encounter, and binding processes proceed continuously and smoothly; the focal point of the imaging remains stable and focused at the microscale.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Compliance with production specifications and visual parameters: high-detail 3D rendering, microscopic perspective with appropriate magnification, realistic optical effects (transmission/reflection/fluorescence), and duration and pacing aligned with instructed requirements
rubrics_instruction.d5: Ensure alignment with medical and scientific education objectives: accurate articulation of mechanisms, clear and learnable information, a style consistent with medical educational purposes, and enhancement of understanding regarding the role of insulin pumps in diabetes management.

任务: Microscopic visualization of the hip joint cartilage surface, dynamically presenting the propagation of cartilage fissures and the flow of synovial fluid, highlighting cartilage degradation and the granular texture of the bone surface caused by arthritis. The environment is within the joint cavity, rendered with fine realism, illustrating the microscopic contact between the femoral head and acetabulum as well as tissue damage under joint movement pressure. Suitable for a 5-second medical educational short video.
具体要求: Create a stunning and insightful microscopic world video showcasing the complex interactions inside the hip joint, with particular focus on areas damaged by severe arthritis or requiring total hip arthroplasty. This scene should explore detailed joint motion between the femoral head and the pelvic acetabulum, simulating the movements and stresses that may occur in arthritic or injured hips. Capture the remarkable texture and degradation process of cartilage wear, emphasizing microscopic fissures and erosion that ultimately necessitate surgical intervention. The animation should reveal the granular texture of the bone surface, the dynamics of synovial fluid, and subtle deterioration of joint tissues, providing an immersive and educational exploration that underscores the necessity and impact of medical intervention, crucial for patient education and medical content marketing.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The anatomical hierarchy and scale proportions accurately represent the spatial relationships and stratification of the femoral head, acetabulum, articular cartilage, subchondral bone, and synovial fluid space within the joint cavity. The geometric proportions and local curvature of the contact area conform to the macro- and micro-scale anatomical standards of the hip joint.
rubrics_scientific.d2: The biomechanical behavior of joint loading and crack propagation is scientifically valid; cartilage cracks propagate along structurally weak interfaces aligned with collagen fiber orientation. Shear and peeling deformations, the migration of contact pressure zones, and the rolling-sliding interactions conform to the principles of viscoelastic materials and contact mechanics.
rubrics_scientific.d3: The synovial fluid hydrodynamics and lubrication mode are accurately represented, with a rational transition between viscoelasticity and boundary/mixed/fluid film lubrication. The infiltration and reflux of the fluid within cracks and surface textures conform to fluid mechanics and lubrication theories while maintaining mass conservation.
rubrics_scientific.d4: The temporal and spatial scale consistency is reasonable; the time compression of degradation progression, crack propagation, and fluid response within the 5-second time history does not violate causality. The particle diffusion, flow velocity, and tissue elastic recovery magnitudes correspond appropriately to the microscopic spatial scale.
rubrics_scientific.d5: The degenerative phenotype and tissue texture are accurately characterized; cartilage thinning and fibrosis, surface roughness and granular detachment, as well as subchondral bone sclerosis or exposure, are precisely described in terms of texture. The avascular nature of cartilage is clearly distinguished from the distribution of synovial tissue.

rubrics_visual.d1: Image clarity and fine depiction of microstructural details; textures, fissure edges, and grain structures are clearly visible without excessive sharpening.
rubrics_visual.d2: 3D modeling accuracy and material realism, precise representation of soft and hard tissues as well as surface roughness, consistent refraction and viscosity characteristics of fluid materials.
rubrics_visual.d3: Animation transitions and physical logic are credible, with coherent and stable processes in microcrack propagation, fluid dynamics, contact friction, and pressure response.
rubrics_visual.d4: Lighting and depth of field are handled naturally; subtle light scattering and shadow transitions are smooth, with clear layering that does not obscure critical details.
rubrics_visual.d5: Color and contrast are controlled scientifically, with consistent saturation and hue, emphasizing key areas without compromising medical discernibility.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Complete implementation of the core scenarios and pathological themes described in the task, focusing on cartilage degeneration, crack propagation, and synovial fluid dynamics at the microscopic level of the hip joint, demonstrating tissue damage caused by mechanical loading.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Accurately present key elements and organizational relationships, reflecting the microscopic contact between the femoral head and the acetabulum, the granular texture of the bone surface, as well as cartilage wear patterns and erosion.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Compliance with technical specifications and duration constraints, approximately 5 seconds in length, featuring a micro close-up perspective, realistic and detailed imagery, stable camera movement, and coherent pacing.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Reflecting dynamic processes and causal logic, the continuous progression of crack exacerbation, fluid dynamic responses, and tissue deterioration in response to joint motion and pressure variations.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Strengthen educational and medical communication objectives by clearly conveying disease progression and the necessity of medical interventions, with content tailored for use in medical education and health science marketing.

任务: From a microscopic perspective, realistically depict the rupture of a semi-transparent blood vessel in the brain, with blood flowing into the brain tissue. Blood cells struggle as their flow is obstructed, the aneurysm slightly expands, and neurons begin to degenerate due to ischemia. The environment presents a contrast in texture between healthy and damaged tissues. Biochemical reactions are displayed at the cellular level, with dramatic lighting emphasizing the severity of the lesion, focusing on a single core stroke scene.
具体要求: Create a captivating microscopic world video that vividly illustrates the complex process of stroke occurring within the brain, focusing on the central theme: the intricate cerebral vascular network. Capture the dynamic scene of blood flow interruption with detailed realism, showing the rupture of blood vessels and blood infiltrating brain tissue, while highlighting the impact on neurons caused by nutrient deprivation. Depict the vivid textures of arteries and veins along with semi-transparent vessel walls, emphasizing the stark contrast between healthy and compressed vessels. Include subtle animations of blood cells struggling under flow obstruction, demonstrate detailed morphological changes in the affected area, and capture the premonitory expansion of the aneurysm exerting pressure on surrounding brain structures. Highlight the transition from flexible healthy tissue to textures approaching hemorrhagic tension zones. Closely monitor biochemical interactions at the cellular level unfolding in real time, enhancing the visual presentation by focusing on tissue degeneration under high-pressure hemorrhage, deepening viewers' understanding of ischemic and hemorrhagic injury. Ensure the inclusion of dramatic lighting to convey the severity of the condition, while providing a detailed portrayal of brain physiology and critical affected processes, laying the foundation for potential interventions such as surgical procedures or pharmacological restoration of normal blood flow.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The anatomical structures and scale relationships between cerebral blood vessels and surrounding tissues are accurate; arteries and veins are clearly differentiated, with reasonable branching and trajectories; the layering, thickness, and translucency of the vessel walls are physiologically consistent; the size ratios and densities of erythrocytes, neurons, glial cells, and capillary diameters conform to standard brain tissue parameters.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Vascular rupture and hemodynamic processes conform to physiological and fluid dynamic principles; the flow direction at the rupture site aligns with the pressure gradient, and the jetting/exudation patterns correspond to tissue resistance; erythrocyte deformation, aggregation, and viscosity behaviors in obstructed flow are appropriately represented; early hemostasis and coagulation trends, plasma permeation and diffusion, as well as the transition between turbulent and laminar flow and shear stress, are accurately reflected at the relevant scales.
rubrics_scientific.d3: The spatiotemporal sequence of ischemic and hemorrhagic injury; the progression order, relative timing, and spatial gradients consistent with perfusion boundaries, ranging from perfusion decline, impaired energy metabolism, ionic pump failure, cellular swelling/dendritic beading, to excitotoxicity and calcium overload, free radical generation, increased blood-brain barrier permeability, and edema expansion.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Accurate representation of the mechanical and morphological characteristics of the aneurysm; located at arterial bifurcations or curvatures, with expansion and pulsation amplitude constrained by wall stress and blood pressure; documented variations in thickness and translucency of the weakened region; compression-induced deformation and rebound of surrounding tissues consistent with the biomechanics of biological materials.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Severe scientific inaccuracies will be penalized; deductions will be applied for each occurrence of elements that contradict medical and physical principles, such as depicting veins as pulsatile blood supply structures, retrograde blood flow due to pressure, immediate complete reperfusion after rupture, instantaneous neuronal regeneration, and the presence of free gas bubbles within hematomas.

rubrics_visual.d1: Image clarity and fine depiction of microscopic details, with well-defined organizational texture and distinct layering of edges in minute structures, free from noise and compression artifacts.
rubrics_visual.d2: High fidelity in 3D modeling and material realism, with the translucency of vascular walls and cells exhibiting credible volumetric thickness and subsurface scattering effects.
rubrics_visual.d3: The animation is smooth and physically consistent, with continuous and natural dynamic behaviors such as interruption of blood flow, obstruction and deformation of blood cells, and slight expansion of aneurysms, without jittering or frame skipping.
rubrics_visual.d4: Lighting and depth of field are controlled naturally and dramatically, with volumetric light and shadow transitions smoothly rendered; exposure and contrast emphasize the pathological core while maintaining information legibility.
rubrics_visual.d5: Color and contrast are scientifically consistent; the hue and luminance contrast between healthy and damaged tissues are clear without being exaggerated; color grading and white balance remain stable.

rubrics_instruction.d11: Focus on a single core stroke scenario from a microscopic perspective, narrating primarily the interruption of blood flow and vascular rupture within a complex cerebrovascular network.
rubrics_instruction.d22: Comprehensively present key pathological features: vascular rupture with blood extravasation, erythrocyte stasis in obstructed blood flow, mild aneurysmal dilation, neuronal ischemic degeneration, contrast in texture between healthy and damaged tissues, and the translucent vessel wall with arterio-venous texture.
rubrics_instruction.d33: Accurately depicting biochemical interactions and microscopic details of tissue degeneration at the cellular scale, with realistic materials and dramatic lighting collaboratively emphasizing the severity of lesions while maintaining scientific rigor.
rubrics_instruction.d44: Demonstrate a continuous dynamic process with clear temporal sequencing: from rupture, hemorrhage, and blood flow obstruction to the progression of tissue response; adhere to a microscopic close-up perspective, stable framing, and depth of field control, while meeting technical specifications regarding duration and pacing.
rubrics_instruction.d55: Avoidance of Topic Deviation and Inappropriate Scientific Representation: Do not introduce unrelated scenarios or excessive artistic effects that lead to information deviation; do not confuse the relationship between ischemic and hemorrhagic injuries; maintain a coherent and focused narrative flow.

任务: Under a high-definition microscopic perspective, coexistence of healthy colonic mucosa and localized mild ulcers is depicted, showcasing the smooth mucosal surface, fine vascular networks, and subtle movement of tiny villi, with a background of gently flowing intestinal fluid, highlighting the detailed mucosal structures and pathological features.
具体要求: Produce an engaging and educational video focusing on the complex microscopic world of the lower gastrointestinal tract during colonoscopy or sigmoidoscopy procedures. The primary theme of the video is the mucosal architecture of the lower digestive tract, a complex and delicate tissue structure that plays a critical role in human digestive and immune systems. In this scenario, exploration is conducted through a microscopic camera mounted on a flexible endoscope, revealing the vivid and detailed environment of the colon and rectum. The camera carefully navigates the intestinal wall, capturing unique textures and dynamic processes such as rhythmic contraction and relaxation of muscles, gentle flow of intestinal fluid, and subtle movements of tiny villi. The video should vividly emphasize various textural variations, including the smoothness of healthy mucosa, intricate vascular patterns supplying blood, presence of polyps with potentially uneven surfaces, and areas of inflammation or infection characterized by pronounced erythema and swelling. Special attention should be given to illustrating pathological changes such as ulcers or diverticula, enhancing their complexity and clinical significance with high-resolution imagery. The video must be both informative and visually captivating, combining authentic visual details with expert narration to deepen viewers' understanding of the diagnostic capabilities and clinical importance of colonoscopic examinations.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Accurate depiction of the microscopic structure and histological layers of the colonic mucosa: correct representation of the surface morphology dominated by crypts, the mucus layer, and the regular mucosal vascular pattern.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Expression of pathological changes: mild ulceration/erosion, morphological characteristics and anatomical localization of inflammation, congestion, edema, polyps, and diverticula consistent with established colonic pathology knowledge.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Scale and spatial proportions are appropriate: the relative sizes of the epithelium, crypt openings, microvessels, and intestinal fluid particles, as well as the scale of camera movement, are consistent with the actual magnification of the endoscope.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Physiological dynamic processes are accurate: the rhythm and amplitude of colonic segmentation/peristalsis, intestinal fluid viscosity and flow patterns, and changes in mucosal blood perfusion correspond to the physiology of the lower gastrointestinal tract.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Presence of non-colonic features or physically implausible elements (e.g., small intestinal villus-like structures, exaggerated peristalsis, jet-like blood flow, low-viscosity, high-velocity turbulent intestinal fluid)

rubrics_visual.d1: Image clarity and preservation of microscopic details, with clear organization texture, vascular networks, and edges of fine structures without excessive sharpening; rich detail hierarchy with consistent scale.
rubrics_visual.d2: High fidelity in modeling and material realism, with accurate soft tissue and fluid morphology; materials exhibit moist and translucent properties, appropriate subsurface scattering and specular highlight control; lighting and shadow layering are clear without obscuring critical details.
rubrics_visual.d3: The animation and camera motion are smooth and stable, with rhythmic contractions, microfluidic movements, and villi gentle swaying exhibiting natural coherence at the microscopic dynamic level, free from jitter, tearing, or stuttering.
rubrics_visual.d4: Color and contrast appear natural and credible, with stable white balance; saturation and contrast are moderate, and subtle color variations are clearly presented to facilitate differentiation between healthy and pathological features.
rubrics_visual.d5: Significant artifacts and noise (such as aliasing, compression blocks, flickering, banding, over-sharpening halos, overexposure, or black level crushing) that result in detail loss or diminished realism.

rubrics_instruction.d11: Comprehensive presentation of the core scene: normal colonic mucosa alongside minor ulceration, emphasizing mucosal structural details and pathological features.
rubrics_instruction.d22: Strict alignment of perspective and procedure: Close-up microscopic endoscopic views (colonoscope/sigmoidoscope) navigate smoothly within the intestinal wall; imaging is high-definition and stable, with natural illumination, appropriate focus, and depth of field; duration and pacing meet the specified task requirements.
rubrics_instruction.d33: Comprehensive coverage of key microscopic elements and dynamic processes: smooth mucosal texture, fine microvascular networks, slight motility of diminutive villi, gentle intestinal fluid flow, and rhythmic contraction and relaxation of the muscularis layer.
rubrics_instruction.d44: Lesion and texture diversity are comprehensively and educationally demonstrated: typical pathological features such as polyps, inflammation/infection-associated erythema, ulcers, or diverticula are clearly presented, accompanied by professional explanations or annotations to emphasize key diagnostic points and clinical significance.
rubrics_instruction.d55: Presence of content conflicting with the established criteria or misleading information: inclusion of images from the upper gastrointestinal tract, macroscopic external scenes, or third-person external perspectives; fictional or erroneous anatomical/pathological representations; omission of core elements; or excessive stylization that diminishes medical authenticity.

任务: At the microscopic level, depict the human colonic mucosal surface, showcasing the intricate interactions between surgical instruments and the cellular matrix, emphasizing the moist mucosal texture and fibrous structures. Display subtle dynamic movements of cells at the incision site, reflecting early-stage cellular aggregation during healing. Use vivid colors to differentiate healthy and pathological regions. The imagery should be clear, detailed, and scientifically accurate, suitable for a 5-second short video presentation.
具体要求: Create a compelling microscopic world video highlighting the complex cellular processes involved in colon resection surgery. The primary focus is on the fine structural layers of human colonic tissue and the disease-affected mucosal surface, illustrating the intricate cellular network associated with colorectal cancer or other conditions requiring resection. Capture the dramatic interactions between surgical instruments and the colonic cellular matrix as the surgeon performs precise incisions and excisions. Emphasize the moist texture of the mucosa, the fibrous details of the colonic wall, and the subtly glistening membranes involved in fluid and electrolyte absorption. Demonstrate the potential complexity and dynamic cellular movements in response to surgical intervention and the healing process. Consider using vivid color coding to distinguish healthy tissue from pathological areas, portraying a rich spectrum of biological activity invisible to the naked eye. Highlight the seamless reconnection of the colonic stump, vividly illustrating cellular cohesion and the regeneration of tissue integrity. This engaging visual journey should blend scientific accuracy with artistic aesthetics, providing an immersive perspective on the microscopic world of human anatomy and the refined craftsmanship of medical science.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The hierarchical organization of the target tissue corresponds to established biological standards in terms of cellular morphology and scale ratios, including the epithelium—basement membrane—stroma/lamina propria—muscularis layers, with appropriate relative thickness, density, and arrangement of microvasculature and fibrous networks.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Mechanical and physical responses of device–tissue microscale interactions are accurate (compression, tension, cutting, and coagulation/hemostasis induce cellular and matrix deformation, rupture, and recoil consistent with tissue mechanics; device dimensions correspond to cellular/fiber scales).
rubrics_scientific.d3: Accurate representation of the early healing process at the wound margin (epithelial cell migration and aggregation, initial inflammatory cell infiltration, and the temporal and spatial rhythm of provisional matrix and vascular responses are appropriate; the logic of tissue continuity reconstruction is coherent).
rubrics_scientific.d4: The distinction between healthy and pathological regions is based on reliable morphological and functional differences (such as glandular/tissue architectural disorganization, cellular polarity and atypia, exudation/necrosis), with scientifically consistent correspondence to staining or marker patterns.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Artistic and visual encoding must not introduce scientific misrepresentations (such as unsupported luminescence/color mapping, exaggerated structural dimensions, clarity/depth of field inconsistent with imaging principles, or unrealistic dynamic rates leading to misperception).

rubrics_visual.d1: Image clarity and fine presentation of microscopic details, with clearly distinguishable mucosal texture and fibrous layer stratification.
rubrics_visual.d2: High precision in 3D modeling with realistic material representation, rigorous organizational hierarchy and instrument structure, and credible scale relationships.
rubrics_visual.d3: The handling of lighting and depth of field is natural; moist textures, subsurface scattering, and micro-specular highlights are consistently rendered, with no overexposure or shadow artifacts.
rubrics_visual.d4: Color representation and encoding are clearly distinguished, with balanced saturation and contrast, ensuring high discriminability between healthy and pathological regions while maintaining realistic texture.
rubrics_visual.d5: Animation and interactions are smooth, with continuous dynamics among the device, matrix, and cells; the cutting and healing rhythm is stable without abrupt frame jumps.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Completely depict the target organ’s microscopic mucosal and cellular-level core scenarios, including surgical intervention points, interactions between instruments and cellular matrix, as well as critical moments of incision and resection processes.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Accurately represent key biological structures and textural characteristics, such as the moist mucosal texture, details of the fibrous network, and the subtle gloss of the absorptive membrane, while employing distinct color coding to differentiate between healthy and pathological regions.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Reflects dynamic processes and changes, demonstrating the continuity of mild cellular motility, aggregation, and tip reconnection during the early stages of intervention and healing, emphasizing the regeneration of tissue integrity.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Meets technical specifications and duration requirements; the image is clear and detailed, scientifically accurate, and vividly colored; the microscopic viewpoint is stable and focused on key areas; overall duration approximately 5 seconds.
rubrics_instruction.d5: The scene setting and background demonstrate good consistency, with scale and environment presented appropriately, avoiding interference from macroscopic elements. The narrative focuses on the cellular structural networks and surgical procedures related to the specified disease.

任务: Under a microscopic perspective, squamous cell carcinoma cells of the lung undergo division and morphological mutations within a dynamic microenvironment. The cell surface gradually changes from smooth to rough and irregular, accompanied by alterations in pigmentation. Surrounding healthy cells appear as smooth spherical structures for contrast. The details are clear and richly textured, highlighting the abnormal structures and dynamic changes of the cancer cells. Suitable for a 5-second short video.
具体要求: Create a stunning microscopic world video that meticulously captures the cellular-level presentation of early-stage lung squamous cell carcinoma as the central subject. Set within a dynamically active microenvironment, cells implicated in the development of this lung cancer subtype are undergoing division and mutation, vividly illustrating the chaotic and complex biological mechanisms driving disease progression. Ensure the details are prominent and textured, emphasizing unique cellular structures and abnormalities such as the irregular shapes, textures, and altered pigmentation of cancer cells compared to the surrounding healthy tissue. The video should highlight the contrast between the smooth, spherical texture of healthy cells and the uneven, rough surfaces of cancer cells, showcasing the microscopic drama of these processes. The overall theme should focus on the transition from normal to abnormal cellular morphology, providing an in-depth insight into how these precursors contribute to the formation of lung squamous cell carcinoma, thereby offering a glimpse into this critical aspect of cancer research.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The cell morphology and size ratios are scientifically rational; cancer cells exhibit anticipated morphological abnormalities (such as irregular margins and coarse texture), while healthy cells are smooth and near-spherical, providing clear contrast; the scale relationships between overall and subcellular structures are consistent.
rubrics_scientific.d2: The time series of cell division and morphological mutations conform to the principles of mitosis and pathological progression, exhibiting continuous stage transitions without biologically implausible instantaneous jumps, and demonstrating clear causal relationships between processes.
rubrics_scientific.d3: The histological type characteristics of the target lesion are consistently expressed, prominently exhibiting the corresponding phenotype without the inclusion of structural features exclusive to other histological types.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Cell–cell and cell–matrix interactions, as well as motility and diffusion behaviors in the microenvironment, adhere to microscale physical and cellular biological principles (e.g., adhesion, compression, Brownian motion, contact inhibition).
rubrics_scientific.d5: The mapping of temporal and spatial scales is appropriate, with event rates, magnification factors, and motion amplitudes maintained at magnitudes consistent with the actual microscopic processes over a duration of 5 seconds.

rubrics_visual.d1: High image resolution with finely detailed micro-scale features, clean edges, and distinguishable cell membrane textures and surface microstructures.
rubrics_visual.d2: Color and tonal qualities are natural and stable, with saturation and contrast appropriately controlled; pigment deposition differences and regional tonal gradations are distinct and free from color casts.
rubrics_visual.d3: Animation is smooth and temporally consistent; transitions between splitting and morphological mutations are continuous; motion speed and acceleration rhythms are reasonable; no jittering or motion blur artifacts are present.
rubrics_visual.d4: High realism in modeling and material quality, with accurate surface roughness and normal map details corresponding to transparent/translucent properties; clear morphological and textural contrast between the subject and the reference object.
rubrics_visual.d5: Lighting and depth of field are handled naturally; volumetric lighting and subsurface scattering are credible; specular highlights and shadow gradations are rich without overexposure or underexposure; depth of field effectively directs attention to the subject.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Fully depict the specified lesion cells as the central subject under microscopic observation, illustrating their dynamic processes of division and morphological mutation within the microenvironment.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Accurately reflect the key contrasting elements: the irregular morphology of lesional cells, the progression of surface texture from smooth to rough, and changes in pigmentation, which form a distinct contrast with the regular and smooth morphology of the surrounding healthy cells.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Reflects a continuous dynamic narrative from normal to abnormal states, with clear temporal sequencing of fragmentation and mutation; abnormal structures and dynamic changes are prominently highlighted and identifiable.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Technical and Presentation Specifications Met: Microscopic lens and viewing angle remain stable; details are clear and textures rich; composition emphasizes the central subject; overall duration approximately 5 seconds with appropriate information density.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Avoid thematic and scale shifts: Do not introduce macro-level scenes or incorrect cellular/tissue features unrelated to the task, and do not diminish the dynamic and contrastive manifestations of pathological cells.

任务: 3D high-resolution animation showcasing a single red blood cell navigating dynamic blood flow, with the camera focusing on the glycation process of glucose molecules binding to hemoglobin, meticulously presenting molecular binding details and subtle changes in cellular texture and color. The visuals are concise and vivid, emphasizing microscopic physiological mechanisms.
具体要求: Create an engaging microscopic world video that centers on the complex journey of red blood cells traveling through the bloodstream, exploring the remarkable process of glycation. Begin by immersing the viewer in a vibrant and dynamic blood flow environment, highlighting the perfectly rounded, elastic red blood cells moving gracefully within their surrounding microscopic world. Focus on a single red blood cell and further magnify to reveal the molecular process of glucose particles attaching to intracellular hemoglobin, exquisitely depicting the formation of glycated hemoglobin. Illustrate the cellular transformation during glucose attachment, indicating changes in texture and color, and potentially portraying the subtle crystalline forms of glucose binding. Seamlessly transition from the bloodstream to the alveoli, demonstrating hemoglobin’s journey in oxygen binding, then return to the spleen for cellular degradation, detailing the role of macrophages in the phagocytosis of senescent red blood cells. The video should employ vivid 3D animation to clearly and deeply present this complex physiological process with stunning visual effects, making it an ideal educational tool for understanding the A1C test and its significance in long-term blood glucose monitoring. Utilize high-resolution textures and realistic motion dynamics to make microscopic interactions tangible, ensuring the audience gains new insights into the hidden cellular world and its impact on conditions such as diabetes and prediabetes.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Accuracy in cellular and molecular scale and morphological representation: erythrocytes exhibit a biconcave disc shape and are anucleate; their diameter is proportionally consistent with capillaries, and the relative sizes of hemoglobin and glucose molecules are appropriately scaled; the thickness and texture of the cell membrane conform to recognized biological characteristics.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Scientific Expression of the Mechanism of Glycated Hemoglobin Formation: Non-enzymatic glycation of hemoglobin by glucose accumulates over time and is influenced by blood glucose levels; the temporal sequence from rapid initial interaction to formation of a more stable product is logically presented; the visualization does not exaggerate changes as instantaneous large alterations in color/texture or crystallization.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Microcirculatory blood flow and cellular/molecular movement conform to physical and physiological principles: laminar flow and shear gradients are appropriately represented; erythrocytes exhibit accurate elastic deformation and axial migration within narrow capillaries; coupling of small molecule diffusion and convection, as well as Brownian motion characteristics, align with concentration gradients.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Correct representation of oxygenation/deoxygenation and alveolar gas exchange process: oxygen reversibly binds to ferrous hemoglobin in pulmonary capillaries exhibiting cooperative effects; color changes reflect only the oxygenation state rather than glycation level; interaction with glucose does not constitute competitive binding.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Accurate description of erythrocyte aging and splenic clearance pathways as well as the significance of HbA1c: lifespan and turnover timescales are reasonable; the sequence of splenic cord retention, macrophage phagocytosis, and heme degradation/iron recycling is correctly represented; the temporal window and stability expression of HbA1c as an indicator of long-term glycemic exposure are appropriately conveyed.

rubrics_visual.d1: High image clarity with discernible microstructural details, adequate texture resolution, clean edges, and clearly defined structural hierarchy.
rubrics_visual.d2: The 3D modeling and material exhibit physically realistic appearance, with harmonious proportions and scale relationships, as well as appropriate representation of translucency and subsurface scattering.
rubrics_visual.d3: The animation is coherent and credible with respect to microscale dynamics; blood flow and molecular trajectories are smooth, camera movement is stable, and scene transitions are natural.
rubrics_visual.d4: Lighting and color are managed in a naturally unified manner; volumetric lighting and soft shadows remain stable; depth of field and focal guidance are clear; key mechanisms are emphasized through color and luminance.
rubrics_visual.d5: Low levels of artifacts and adverse visual phenomena, minimizing flicker, noise, aliasing, banding, and excessive motion blur.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Comprehensively depict the multi-stage physiological process chain specified by the task: transit of a single erythrocyte in dynamic blood flow → molecular-level glycation formation → alveolar oxygenation → clearance by spleen and macrophages
rubrics_instruction.d2: The lens and subject are properly focused and encompass the core elements: continuous focus on a single red blood cell, providing molecular-scale close-ups, clearly presenting the glucose-hemoglobin binding and the textural/color changes at the cellular level, while also including necessary imagery of the relevant environment and participating cells/organs.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Educational and Scientific Expression Standards: Accurately elucidates the glycosylation mechanism and its correlation with long-term A1C monitoring; presents a clear narrative with rigorous information; illustrations and terminology are precise and free from misleading elements.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Technical and performance specifications comply with instructions: high-resolution 3D textures, realistic motion and fluid dynamics, natural viewpoint and close-up transitions, clean and vivid visuals with smooth scene changes, emphasizing microscopic mechanisms.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Severe violations of the designated deduction criteria: omission of critical phases or core components, deviation of focus from a single subject, absence of molecular binding details, scientifically incorrect mechanisms or conflicts with established settings.

任务: Under the microscope, Candida albicans rapidly proliferates on the surface of vaginal epithelial cells, forming an initial biofilm with clearly visible cellular structures and biofilm textures. Beneficial bacteria such as lactobacilli are dispersed around, while immune cells aggregate at the site of infection attempting to eliminate the Candida. Mild erythema and swelling appear in the local epithelial tissue, reflecting the microscopic dynamics and ecological balance.
具体要求: Create a captivating microscopic world video that explores in detail the dynamic environment of vaginal yeast infection caused by Candida albicans. In this engaging scene, depict the micro-interactions within the vaginal ecosystem, highlighting the balance between Candida albicans and beneficial bacteria such as lactobacilli. Illustrate the overproliferation of yeast cells and their invasion into the mucosal epithelium as environmental conditions change. Capture the intense response of immune cells to the invasion, showcasing their efforts to clear the infection while combating the resilient Candida that kills immune cells and forms an impervious biofilm. Emphasize the fine textures of this microscopic world, from the complexity of Candida albicans’ cellular structures to the inflamed and damaged epithelial tissue, as well as the vivid portrayal of immune cells engaged in fierce struggle. Include the visual complexity of biofilm formation and seamlessly integrate vivid manifestations of candidiasis symptoms such as erythema, swelling, and thick white discharge as the yeast spreads within the vaginal mucosa. This video should not only educate viewers on biological aspects but also serve as a visual journey through a common female health issue, combining scientific insight with visual elegance.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Accurate representation of the morphological and scale relationships between vaginal epithelium and microbiota: epithelial stratification and cell polarity, rod-shaped and clustered distribution of lactobacilli, yeast and hyphal forms of Candida albicans, as well as their relative size, spacing, and density conforming to biological parameters.
rubrics_scientific.d2: The causal sequence of pathogen-host interaction and morphological transition is correct: Candida albicans adhesion → proliferation → morphological transition (from budding to pseudohyphae/hyphae) → surface invasion and epithelial damage, with the process being coherent and consistent with established physiological and pathological knowledge.
rubrics_scientific.d3: The structure and dynamics of biofilm formation are scientifically presented: from initial adhesion and microcolony development to the hierarchical and spatial organization during maturation, including the distribution and texture of the extracellular matrix, as well as the representation of mass transfer diffusion gradients and channel structures, all of which are reasonable and reflect an enhanced tolerance to clearance.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Immune responses and effector mechanisms conform to physiological principles: steps and time scales of immune cell chemotaxis, aggregation, adhesion, migration, phagocytosis/degranulation/extracellular trap release are appropriate; interactions with fungal antagonism and evasion (e.g., hyphal damage, biofilm shielding) are consistent and not exaggerated.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Dynamic consistency driven by microecology and environmental factors: the role of lactic acid bacteria in maintaining an acidic microenvironment; the viscosity and composition of mucus and secretions; the impact of nutrient and pH fluctuations on community balance and Candida overgrowth; and the spatiotemporal correspondence between inflammatory signs (redness, swelling, thick white discharge) and tissue damage.

rubrics_visual.d1: High image clarity and resolution of fine details: cellular structures, biomembrane textures, and tissue boundaries are distinctly visible, without over-sharpening or blurring.
rubrics_visual.d2: Natural lighting and depth of field effects: Characteristic shallow depth of field in microscopy imaging, stable subsurface scattering and volumetric light representation; clear gradation of light and shadow without highlight bleeding or flickering.
rubrics_visual.d3: Animation Smoothness and Temporal Coherence: The transitions among proliferation, migration and aggregation, permeation and invasion, and deformation processes are seamless, with a stable frame rate, free of jitter, stutter, and motion blur.
rubrics_visual.d4: 3D Modeling Accuracy and Material Realism: Geometric proportions of cells, microbial communities, and tissues are harmonious; surface roughness, translucency, and moist adhesiveness are convincingly rendered; textures exhibit no stretching or repetition.
rubrics_visual.d5: Color and Contrast Natural and Unified: The background tissue coloration, inflammatory redness, and bacterial hue are distinctly differentiated without oversaturation; white balance is accurate; the overall tonal layers are rich, with no loss of detail in shadows or highlights.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Fully realize the core microscopic scenarios and task objectives, focusing on the infection ecology and host-microbe interactions at the microscopic level, thereby forming a coherent and self-consistent narrative loop.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Accurately depict the key participating elements and their spatial relationships, including pathogenic microorganisms, commensal microbiota, immune cells, host epithelium/tissues, and characteristic pathological features, while maintaining the readability of structural and textural characteristics.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Clearly articulate the dynamic process and stage evolution from balance to imbalance, encompassing key aspects such as proliferation/invasion, biofilm/colony development, immune response, and microecological changes.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Scientific accuracy and effective popular science communication are maintained; morphology and processes conform to biological principles; narrative is coherent with appropriate information density, avoiding exaggeration or misleading statements.
rubrics_instruction.d5: The perspective and technical specifications comply with microscopic presentation requirements; the microscope's viewing angle and scale are consistent. The duration and pacing support a complete narrative, with stable framing and focus, and clear image quality.

任务: From a microscopic perspective, during an asthma attack, the bronchial wall smooth muscle contracts rapidly, and the release of chemical mediators such as histamine triggers bronchospasm. The bronchial wall swells, mucus secretion increases, and the airway gradually narrows. Inflammatory cells dynamically move within the inflamed tissue, meticulously displaying cellular structures and a richly colored microscopic scene, highlighting the microprocess of airway obstruction.
具体要求: Create a stunning microscopic world video vividly illustrating the complex process of airflow obstruction in the bronchi during asthma, a chronic inflammatory disease. Visualize the diaphragm contracting and relaxing to facilitate airflow through the trachea, bronchi, and bronchioles, reaching the alveoli where fundamental gas exchange occurs. Focus on the diffusion process of carbon dioxide exiting the blood and oxygen entering it in this awe-inspiring animation. Emphasize the responsiveness of bronchial smooth muscle under autonomic nervous system control, demonstrating how sympathetic and parasympathetic stimulation leads to bronchial dilation or constriction. When asthma patients encounter triggers, highlight their hyperreactive airways by capturing the release of chemical mediators such as histamine and leukotrienes during an asthma attack. Depict the ensuing bronchospasm characterized by bronchial muscle contraction, swelling of the bronchial wall, and increased mucus secretion from goblet cells, resulting in airway obstruction and impaired gas exchange. Visualize symptoms of bronchospasm such as coughing, wheezing, and chest tightness, while also demonstrating the effects of asthma medications. Emphasize the texture of inflamed tissue and the dynamic movement of cellular structures, creating a richly colored and patterned scene that vividly portrays the microscopic struggle within the airway.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The anatomical hierarchy and scale relationship between bronchi and alveoli are accurately represented; the bronchial wall exhibits a layered structure with circularly arranged smooth muscle, and the spatial relationship among the epithelium, glands, and blood vessels is appropriately depicted.
rubrics_scientific.d2: The airflow and gas exchange processes adhere to the principles of physics and physiology, with clearly defined regions of convection and diffusion. The effects of partial pressure gradients, membrane thickness, and surface area on oxygen influx and carbon dioxide efflux are appropriately represented.
rubrics_scientific.d3: The mechanisms by which bronchial smooth muscle responds to autonomic nerves and chemical mediators are accurately described, with sympathetic stimulation causing bronchodilation, vagal/parasympathetic stimulation causing bronchoconstriction, and a clear causal chain delineated for mediators such as histamine and leukotrienes inducing bronchoconstriction, mucus hypersecretion, and mucosal edema.
rubrics_scientific.d4: The temporal sequence and multiscale consistency are reasonable; the onset and resolution of acute bronchospasm, mucus secretion, and edema occur in a sequential and rate-matched manner; the site of drug intervention and the rhythm of onset and offset effects are scientifically credible.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Key physiological locations and mechanisms must not be reversed or confused (e.g., placing gas exchange in the conducting zone, misrepresenting sympathetic/parasympathetic effects, or incorrectly depicting smooth muscle orientation or contraction patterns).

rubrics_visual.d3: Lighting and color rendering are natural and realistic; volumetric lighting and scattering, subsurface scattering of translucent tissues, and depth of field control are well-coordinated; color saturation and contrast are harmonized to emphasize focal points.
rubrics_visual.d4: Modeling and material properties are credible; the geometric accuracy, roughness, and viscoelastic responses of the tissue, cells, and fluids are appropriate, with scale perception and detail density consistent.
rubrics_visual.d5: Visual artifacts are effectively controlled; defects such as aliasing, noise, color banding, compression blocks, interlacing, and flickering are minimized.

rubrics_instruction.d22: Accurately reflects key biological structures and mechanisms: bronchial smooth muscle reactivity and spasm, chemical mediator release, inflammatory cell dynamics, mucus secretion and wall edema, sympathetic/parasympathetic regulation, and gas diffusion pathways.

任务: High-definition microscopic close-up of a hepatic lobule with rich colors, depicting hepatocytes dynamically metabolizing glucose and storing glycogen, active Kupffer cells phagocytosing toxins in the bloodstream, with clear visualization of cellular structures and microscopic textures. The environment is minimalistic, with dynamic particle flow highlighting the liver's metabolic and detoxification functions, suitable for a 5-second immersive microscopic world video.
具体要求: Create a visually stunning microscopic world video that immerses viewers in the complex anatomy of the hepatic lobule, showcasing its fundamental roles in metabolic processes. The scene centers on a vivid close-up of the hepatic lobule, emphasizing its internal dynamic activities. Observe hepatocytes actively metabolizing carbohydrates, lipids, and proteins, capturing the seamless influx of glucose from the digestive tract into the lobule. Highlight hepatocytes storing excess glucose as glycogen and releasing glucose back into the bloodstream. Provide an in-depth depiction of Kupffer cells efficiently phagocytosing and neutralizing toxins and bacteria in the blood flow. Meticulously capture the complex processes of hepatocyte secretion of bile and synthesis of blood coagulation factors. Illustrate the gradual and irreversible progression of liver cirrhosis, demonstrating how scar tissue nodules form, obstruct blood flow, and lead to the characteristic fibrotic appearance of the liver due to chronic hepatic failure. This artistic representation should present microscopic textures and cellular structures with astonishing clarity and precision, immersing the audience in the complexity of liver function and pathology, while conveying the importance of maintaining liver health and raising awareness of liver-related diseases.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Accuracy of hepatic lobule microanatomical structure and spatial relationships: Hepatocyte cords and sinusoidal spaces radially arranged around the central vein; clear localization and connectivity of the portal triad; Kupffer cells attached to the sinusoidal endothelium; bile canaliculi situated between adjacent hepatocytes.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Scientific accuracy of fluid and substance flow direction and compartmentalization: Portal vein/hepatic artery blood flows from the portal triad toward the central vein; bile flows along the bile canaliculi toward the portal triad without mixing with the blood flow pathways; glucose enters the lobule via the portal vein, is processed as needed, and is returned to the bloodstream; Kupffer cells phagocytize toxins and bacteria within the sinusoids.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Accurate expression of the mechanisms involved in metabolic and secretory processes: the steps, destinations, and regulation of hepatic glycogen synthesis and breakdown, lipid metabolism, protein synthesis and coagulation factor secretion, as well as bile formation and secretion, conforming to physiological principles and the functional specialization of organelles.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Scale relationships, morphological details, and kinematic consistency are appropriate: the relative sizes and proportions of hepatocytes, sinusoidal spaces, bile canaliculi, and Kupffer cells are accurate; the size and modes of motion (diffusion, Brownian motion, and microcirculatory laminar flow) of molecular-level particles and cellular components conform to physical properties.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Pathological progression of liver fibrosis/cirrhosis and its impact on hemodynamics: Accurate depiction of features such as fibrous septa formation and bridging, regenerative nodule remodeling, sinusoidal capillarization, and portal venous reflux obstruction, reflecting the causal relationship between structural remodeling and functional impairment.
