任务: Comparison of cellular-level tissue structures between damaged cartilage of the right hip joint and the metallic surface of the prosthesis under high-resolution microscopic view, dynamically depicting slight contact between the cartilage and the prosthesis, highlighting the textural and cellular detail differences between the damaged cartilage and the smooth prosthetic surface, with a clean background focusing on microscopic structures.
具体要求: Create an engaging microscopic world video focusing on the intricate details of minimally invasive anterior approach total hip replacement (THR) surgery performed on the right hip. The video should accurately depict the incision process, exposure of the hip joint, and precise placement of the acetabular prosthetic components, followed by meticulous removal of the diseased femoral head and implantation of the femoral head prosthesis. Emphasize the anatomical and physiological complexity of the hip, highlighting the smooth textures and intricate details of the articular cartilage, bone structures, and surrounding tissues. Showcase professional surgical instruments, including fluoroscopy devices and customized surgical tables, ensuring the audience appreciates the advantages and potential risks of this advanced surgical technique. Highlight the contrast between the damaged arthritic joint surface and the smooth new prosthetic components, providing an immersive visual experience that illustrates the complex interplay between technology and human skill in restoring mobility and alleviating severe pain.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Accuracy of microscale and structural proportions: The chondrocyte morphology and lacunar spaces of articular cartilage, collagen fiber orientation and matrix density, as well as the micro-roughness/processing texture of the metal implant surface, are quantitatively and relatively consistent with scientific rationale, and correspond anatomically to the right hip joint.
rubrics_scientific.d2: The contact mechanics and lubrication mechanisms conform to physiological and materials science principles: cartilage exhibits viscoelastic deformation and gradual recovery under slight contact; synovial fluid-mediated boundary/film lubrication and friction behaviors are appropriate; the metal load-bearing surface does not display a joint articulation interface covered by viable cells.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Scientific accuracy of key anatomical planes and anterior approach total hip arthroplasty procedure: incision and approach layers, sequence of acetabular exposure and component placement, extraction of the diseased femoral head, and femoral preparation and implantation with correct alignment and directional relationships; consistent right-side positioning and surrounding tissue relationships.
rubrics_scientific.d4: The prosthetic components and material properties are accurately presented: the structural relationships and fitting among the acetabular cup, liner, femoral head, and stem are scientifically valid; the bearing surface smoothness corresponds appropriately to the porous/coated implant interface; the metal-to-bone/soft tissue interface behavior aligns with biomaterial and interfacial biomechanics principles.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Physiological characteristics of cartilage and bone tissue are accurately represented: articular cartilage is avascular and aneural, exhibiting appropriate laminar structure and cell density; cortical and cancellous bone display pore architecture and hemorrhage patterns within physiological limits without exaggeration; bone marrow texture and hemorrhage magnitude are consistent with normal physiological parameters.

任务: From a microscopic perspective, pancreatic beta cells release insulin molecules, which bind to receptors on the cell membrane, illustrating the microscopic mechanisms of reduced insulin production and insulin resistance. The cell membrane and molecular structures are depicted in fine detail, set against a realistic cellular environment. The video is high-resolution, dynamically smooth, and suitable for a 5-second short clip.
具体要求: Create a captivating microscopic world video focusing on the complex physiology of type 2 diabetes in humans. Begin by showcasing the intricate details of nutrient absorption and normal insulin function. Within this microscopic scene, visualize the pancreatic cellular environment and demonstrate insulin production, emphasizing issues such as decreased insulin synthesis and increased insulin resistance that lead to hyperglycemia. Zoom into blood vessels to illustrate the impact of hyperglycemia, highlighting acute complications such as diabetic ketoacidosis, hyperosmolar hyperglycemic state, seizures caused by hypoglycemia, and diabetic coma. Gradually present chronic complications, explaining how microscopic damage from atherosclerosis results in neuropathy, retinal injury, and renal failure. Throughout this thrilling yet educational microscopic journey, emphasize the textures and details of the cell membrane, insulin molecules, glucose particles, and deteriorating vascular walls. Conclude with an in-depth presentation of various therapeutic approaches, depicting at the cellular level the roles of blood glucose monitoring technologies, healthy dietary fibers, exercise biomechanics, and pharmacological agents. Fill the scenes with high-resolution imagery and smooth animations to enrich audience understanding while providing visually engaging content, all meticulously crafted for optimal patient education and professional content marketing.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Accurate depiction of key cellular and molecular structures (cell membrane bilayer, receptor–ligand interactions, secretory granules, vascular wall layers), with consistent geometric morphology and scale proportions across different contexts.
rubrics_scientific.d2: The process logic of insulin production, secretion, receptor signaling transduction, glucose transport, and metabolism conforms to physiological principles, clearly distinguishing the mechanisms and consequences of reduced insulin secretion from those of insulin resistance.
rubrics_scientific.d3: The causal chain from nutrient absorption to blood glucose fluctuations and subsequent complications is accurately depicted, with the temporal sequence, target organs, and microstructural damage mechanisms of acute and chronic complications appropriately corresponding and clearly distinguished.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Molecular and cellular-level dynamics adhere to physicochemical principles (diffusion, convection, binding/dissociation, vesicle budding and fusion, membrane electrophysiological properties), with short-term compression preserving relative rates and temporal sequences.
rubrics_scientific.d5: The scientific expression of the micro-mechanisms underlying therapeutic and monitoring interventions (pharmacological targets and effects, the impact of exercise and dietary fiber on insulin sensitivity and glucose metabolism, principles of blood glucose monitoring), avoiding exaggeration of immediate efficacy.

任务: Microscopic animation of cervical cells focusing on the dynamic transition from healthy cells to early cancerous cells, highlighting nuclear morphology, chromatin density changes, and cell membrane contours, with clearly visible cytoplasmic flow. The background represents the cellular microenvironment, featuring vivid contrasting colors that dynamically showcase cellular structural changes and pathological characteristics.
具体要求: Create a stunning microscopic world animation video that focuses on the complex cellular-level processes of cervical cancer staging, inviting viewers to deeply explore the unknown realms of medical science. The central scene features a cluster of cervical cells vividly demonstrating their transition through various stages of cancer development (from stage 0 to stages IVAB). The animation should begin with healthy cervical cells, illustrating their structure and arrangement, then progressively depict the malignant transformation and progression at each cancer stage. During the transition, emphasize stage-specific cellular changes such as alterations in nuclear size and shape, increased chromatin density, and abnormal mitotic figures. The scene's dynamics should include the invasion of cancer cells into surrounding tissues, with particular attention to invasion pathways, changes in intercellular communication, and disruption of normal cellular functions. Visible textures in the video should include the fine granular chromatin within nuclei, undulating membrane contours during cell division, and the flowing cytoplasmic environment. The video should be rich in vivid colors representing healthy and pathological cellular states, creating a sharp contrast between normal and cancerous conditions, providing a visually striking and educational exploration for patient education and content marketing on cervical cancer staging.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Accurate correspondence between epithelial tissue stratification and stages of carcinogenesis: the healthy state is characterized by orderly stratification and cellular polarity with an intact continuous basement membrane; the in situ stage demonstrates full-thickness atypical hyperplasia with an intact basement membrane; the invasive stage is marked by basement membrane disruption, with clearly defined invasion pathways and spatial relationships toward the stroma, vasculature, and lymphatic channels.
rubrics_scientific.d2: The nucleo-cytoplasmic ratio and chromatin morphological progression are consistent: nuclear enlargement, irregular nuclear shape, nuclear membrane indentations, prominent nucleoli, and chromatin changes from fine granular to coarse granular/clumped aggregation and marginalization occur in a staged, progressive manner.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Aberrant mitosis and cell cycle timing accuracy: spindle positioning and chromosome segregation trajectories conform to cellular biology principles; the frequency and spatial distribution of abnormalities such as tripolar/multipolar divisions and lagging chromosomes are plausible; mitosis is not completed instantaneously, nor are there violations of chromosome number conservation.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Scale and temporal proportionality: The size hierarchy of cells, nuclei, nucleosomes/chromatin, organelles, basement membrane, and extracellular matrix (ECM) corresponds appropriately to the magnification used in microscopy; membrane fluctuation amplitudes, intracellular particle diffusion, and cell migration speeds occur within reasonable microscopic temporal scales.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Alterations in intercellular communication and adhesion are consistent with invasive biology: decreased tight junctions/adhesive junctions and E-cadherin-mediated adhesion, loss of polarity, enhanced EMT-associated morphology and motility, integrated with dynamic patterns of integrin-mediated ECM adhesion and local matrix degradation corresponding to stage progression.

rubrics_visual.d1: High image clarity with fine detail representation, stable edges of microstructures, precise focus control in key areas, and consistent detail retention during magnification and zooming processes.
rubrics_visual.d2: High fidelity in 3D modeling and material realism; microscale geometry and scale relationships are accurate; consistent representation of translucency and viscoelastic properties; adequate texture resolution without stretching.
rubrics_visual.d3: The animation is smooth with natural transitions; structural changes and shape deformations are coherent; camera movements and temporal rhythm are stable, exhibiting no stuttering or motion blur.
rubrics_visual.d4: Color rendition and contrast relationships are naturally unified, with stable white balance; color mapping of state differences is clear and well-layered, avoiding oversaturation or gray haze.
rubrics_visual.d5: Lighting and depth of field are realistically rendered, with consistent micro-illumination; soft shadows and bokeh exhibit clean transitions; volumetric effects and highlights are free from overexposure.

rubrics_instruction.d1: The full presentation of the staged progression from healthy cervical cells to carcinoma, encompassing stages 0 through IVAB, centered around a cluster of cervical cells, with a clear and coherent sequential transition.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Accurately and dynamically highlight key cytological features: alterations in nuclear size and morphology, chromatin density and fine granular texture, atypical mitotic figures, undulating cell membrane contours, and cytoplasmic streaming.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Vividly demonstrate the interaction between invasion and the microenvironment: invasion pathways, alterations in intercellular communication, and functional disruption of surrounding tissues, with healthy and pathological states distinguished by vivid contrasting colors.

任务: Inside the cell nucleus, folate molecules are converted into their metabolically active forms and precisely bind to the DNA double helix and RNA strands, demonstrating their critical role in new cell synthesis. The scene is a highly realistic 3D medical animation, meticulously depicting the DNA double helix, RNA strands, and cellular membrane structures, highlighting the precise molecular interactions and dynamics. The overall imagery is clear and vivid, suitable for a 5-second microscopic world video.
具体要求: Create a stunning and complex microscopic world video centered on folate molecules, vividly capturing their essence. The scene unfolds within the human body, with a particular focus on the vivid and dynamic activities occurring at the cellular level during pregnancy. Depict folate molecules actively participating in new cell synthesis, intricately interacting with DNA and RNA strands, emphasizing their key role in genetic material formation and cellular function. Highlight scenes where folate promotes the rapid growth of critical components such as brain cells and blood cells, underscoring its importance in preventing neural tube defects during fetal development. The video should seamlessly portray molecular interactions and transformations, illustrating the conversion of folate into its metabolically active form, with special attention to folate’s integration in the cell proliferation process. Details and textures should include highly detailed 3D renderings of cellular structures, such as the DNA double helix, fine RNA strands, and the target cell membrane, vividly showcasing their textures and vitality under high-resolution microscopic imaging. The visual aesthetics should combine the realism of medical animation with artistic visual effects to engage and educate viewers about the biochemical grandeur of folate in the body, ensuring every element—from atomic to molecular scale—is presented with precision and clarity to enrich understanding and visualization of this essential nutrient’s function and impact.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Accurate presentation of folate activation and one-carbon metabolism mechanisms: demonstrating its reduction to a metabolically active form and function as a one-carbon donor in enzyme-catalyzed reactions involved in nucleotide synthesis and the methylation cycle, rather than acting directly on genetic material.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Depiction of folic acid or its active form as directly and stably bound to or intercalated within the DNA double helix/RNA strand, thereby exerting its function.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Subcellular localization and structural/scale relationships are reasonable: the geometric features and chirality of the DNA double helix within the nucleus are consistent; the relative size and density of RNA strands correspond appropriately; the chromatin environment is accurately represented; pure nuclear images do not include irrelevant structures such as the cell membrane; the size ratio between molecules and the double helix is credible.
rubrics_scientific.d4: The temporal sequence and causal chain of biochemical processes are clear and conserved: activation → one-carbon transfer → nucleotide synthesis/methylation → DNA replication or transcription follow a rational order; inter-compartmental steps involve spatial transitions and do not violate material and energy constraints.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Molecular motion and interactions conform to physicochemical principles: predominantly governed by Brownian motion and diffusion, with reversible binding–dissociation constrained by charge and hydrogen bond directionality, avoiding ballistic trajectories, supra-scale attraction, or the substitution of real forces with optical effects.

rubrics_visual.d1: The image exhibits high clarity with richly detailed layers, where microstructural textures are distinctly resolved at high resolution, without noticeable blurring or defocusing.
rubrics_visual.d2: The 3D modeling demonstrates high accuracy and scale consistency, with the geometric morphology and material textures of biomolecules, nucleic acid chains, and cellular structures rendered realistically and in well-defined layers.
rubrics_visual.d3: The animation exhibits smoothness and strong physical coherence in interactions; the molecular transformation and binding processes proceed with appropriate rhythm and continuous trajectories, while camera movements remain stable without abrupt jumps.
rubrics_visual.d4: Lighting and depth of field are handled naturally; volumetric lighting and occlusion relationships are accurate; color saturation and contrast align with medical photorealism style; the main elements exhibit prominent visual hierarchy.
rubrics_visual.d5: The image exhibits noise, aliasing, flickering, clipping errors, shadow jitter, excessive motion blur, or compression artifacts.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Comprehensively presenting the closed-loop process in which the specified molecule is transformed into its metabolically active form, precisely binds to DNA/RNA, and promotes the synthesis of new cells.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Accurately depict and emphasize the key structures and elements specified by the task (such as the DNA double helix, RNA strand, and cell membrane), with the primary molecules and their interactions clearly distinguishable and precise.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Perspective and style consistent with the setting: intracellular/intranuclear microscopic viewpoint, highly realistic 3D medical animation combined with high-resolution microscopy textures; images are clear, vivid, and information-rich.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Duration and dynamic narration meet requirements: total duration approximately 5 seconds; molecular transformations and interactions smoothly connected without missing key steps or abrupt pacing.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Presence of scientific errors, contextual deviations, or misleading representations (e.g., departing from in vivo conditions, diminishing relevance to the specified physiological stage, or improper description of mechanisms)

任务: From a microscopic perspective, healthy prostate cells and cancerous prostate cells are displayed side by side against the vascular network background at the base of the bladder, highlighting the contrast in nuclear structure and morphology. In the scene, the nuclei of the cancerous cells are enlarged and undergo irregular division, finely depicting the smooth textures and microscopic details of the cells and blood vessels. The overall image is clear, with a duration of approximately 5 seconds.
具体要求: Create an engaging microscopic world video showcasing the complex structures of healthy and cancerous prostate tissue, emphasizing their walnut-like size and intricate features. Focus on a dynamic scene where the prostate is located at the bladder base and encases the urethra, displaying vivid details as it interacts with surrounding tissues. Highlight the contrast between healthy and cancerous cells, illustrating the development and impact of prostate cancer at the microscopic level. Capture the meticulous process of radical prostatectomy, where each precise incision reveals the detailed anatomy of the prostate, adjacent tissues, and seminal vesicles. Depict the smooth textures and fine details of glands, cellular structures, and vascular networks to deepen the audience's understanding of the surgical procedure and reflect the precision and complexity inherent in this common yet critical operation.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Accuracy of anatomical location and structural relationships: The prostate is located at the base of the bladder and encircles the urethra; the spatial relationships among the prostate, seminal vesicles, and surrounding vascular network conform to human anatomy.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Comparison of cell and nuclear morphology to the authentic state: The morphology and size of healthy prostate epithelial cells are relatively uniform, while cancerous cells exhibit features consistent with pathological principles, such as increased nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio, nuclear pleomorphism, and irregular mitotic figures.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Scale and Temporal Consistency: The proportional sizes of cells, blood vessels, tissues, and organs correspond coherently with changes in magnification levels, and dynamic variations within short-duration sequences do not violate physiological time scales.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Scientific Presentation of Microcirculation and Vascular Structure: The layering of the vessel wall and endothelial continuity, blood flow direction and velocity, as well as suspended particles and viscosity characteristics conform to microhemodynamic principles.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Surgical anatomical planes and resection pathway accuracy: During radical prostatectomy, the dissection planes and relative positional relationships among the prostate, bladder neck, and seminal vesicles are precise; the tissue layers exposed by the incisions correspond accurately to the true anatomical structures.

rubrics_visual.d1: High image clarity and superior microscopic detail resolution, with sharp edges of key structures and consistently clear, blur-free texture representation.
rubrics_visual.d2: High accuracy in 3D modeling and realistic material representation, with precise depiction of microscopic surface normals and roughness, as well as clearly defined texture layers.
rubrics_visual.d3: The animation exhibits smooth motion, with coherent micro-deformations and fragmentation processes, maintaining a stable rhythm without frame skipping or jitter.
rubrics_visual.d4: Lighting and shadow effects as well as depth of field are handled naturally; volumetric lighting and shadow transitions are smooth; subsurface scattering and specular reflections are appropriately applied to enhance spatial perception.
rubrics_visual.d5: Color management with harmonious contrast, moderate saturation, and consistent color temperature; high color separation of key elements to prevent overexposure and color casting.

rubrics_instruction.d11: Present healthy and pathological cells/tissues side by side within the specified anatomical context, emphasizing the contrast in nuclear structure and morphology, and including abnormal dynamic features of pathological nuclei (e.g., swelling and irregular mitosis).
rubrics_instruction.d22: Accurately represents the spatial relationships and functional context of the organ and adjacent structures (e.g., located at the base of a superior organ, enveloping luminal structures, accompanied by vascular networks), with consistent coherence between macroscopic and microscopic scales.
rubrics_instruction.d33: Delicately rendering the smooth textures and microscopic details of cells, glands, and blood vessels; the overall image is clear and stable with consistent lighting and material properties, effectively highlighting the main elements without overpowering them.
rubrics_instruction.d44: Present an educational dynamic narrative contrasting healthy and pathological states, appropriately reflecting the impact of disease progression and key surgical procedure elements; progressively reveal the anatomical details of the target organ, surrounding tissues, and associated accessory structures, demonstrating the precision and complexity of the operation.
rubrics_instruction.d55: Compliance with technical specifications: duration approximately 5 seconds, consistent microscopic/close-up perspective, camera focus on the designated subject, clear shot transition and rhythm; avoidance of irrelevant elements, off-topic content, or contradictions in scale and positional relationships.

任务: Under the microscope, a single fluid-filled immature follicle within the female ovary, with FSH and LH hormone molecules transmitting signals through the capillary network; excessive LH causes irregular follicular maturation, locally displaying elevated insulin and testosterone molecules, illustrating the microscopic changes of follicles under hormonal imbalance in PCOS, with high-definition scientific details in a single-scene microscopic medical animation.
具体要求: Create a captivating microscopic world video that delves into the complex environment of the ovary—the core of the female reproductive system—focusing on the critical microscopic structures essential for understanding polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS). In this scene, observe the intricate interactions of biological processes: small, fluid-filled follicles enclosing immature oocytes, awaiting signals from pituitary hormones such as FSH and LH. As the animation progresses, detail the process of these hormones entering the bloodstream and reaching the ovary, presenting a stunning visual sequence that depicts the maturation of hundreds of oocytes and follicular enlargement, along with estrogen secretion. Capture the climax of ovulation with the release of mature oocytes, illustrating the dynamic rupture of follicles. Enhance the narrative tension by explaining PCOS-related phenomena: excessive LH leading to irregular follicular maturation, resulting in cyst retention, while elevated insulin levels promote increased testosterone production. Vividly render the follicular surface textures, the complex capillary network transmitting hormones, and the distinct, detailed presence of insulin and testosterone molecules, highlighting a high-definition depiction of this critical yet often misunderstood syndrome. Emphasize the consequences of PCOS, such as hormonal imbalance, infertility, and associated health risks. This detailed portrayal should reflect the latest scientific accuracy and engage viewers to visit the channel for more compelling educational 3D medical animations at https://www.youtube.com/user/nucleushealthvideos.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The organizational hierarchy and vascular distribution are consistent with histological architecture, with accurate morphology and relative size proportions of cells, follicles, and capillaries.
rubrics_scientific.d2: The temporal sequence and quantitative scale of the reproductive endocrine processes are appropriate: pituitary hormone release → vascular transport → receptor binding → follicular recruitment/dominance selection → ovulation or atresia, with the causal order and the number of ovulations per cycle consistent with species-specific physiology.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Accurate hormonal mechanisms and target sites: precise localization of FSH/LH receptors matching corresponding cell types; clear delineation of steroidogenesis and feedback regulatory pathways; scientifically characterized interactions between insulin and androgens affecting follicular development.
rubrics_scientific.d4: The biophysical behavior of molecules and fluids is reliable: capillary blood flow, molecular diffusion and convection, transmembrane/barrier permeability, binding–dissociation kinetics, and concentration gradient profiles conform to physico-chemical principles.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Scientific consistency of PCOS-specific alterations: accurate characterization of LH/FSH ratio imbalance, follicular arrest at the small antral stage with retention as cystic structures, insulin resistance-driven hyperandrogenism, and consequent anovulation and associated risks.

rubrics_visual.d1: Image clarity and fine presentation of microscopic details are ensured, with sharp structural edges and well-controlled noise and compression artifacts.
rubrics_visual.d2: Color and contrast faithfully reproduce natural appearance with clear tonal hierarchy; highlights and shadows retain detail, avoiding oversaturation and color shifts.
rubrics_visual.d3: The animation exhibits smoothness and continuity of motion, with particle and fluid dynamics consistent with physical intuition, free of frame skipping, ghosting, or jittering.
rubrics_visual.d4: High accuracy in 3D modeling with realistic material representation; detailed microstructures, vasculature, and molecular surface textures; consistent scale and proportion.
rubrics_visual.d5: Lighting and depth of field are handled naturally; volumetric lighting and translucent/subsurface scattering are rendered realistically, avoiding hard shadows and exposure imbalance.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Achievement of Core Scenario and Theme: Focus on the ovarian/follicular microenvironment from a microscopic perspective, centered on follicular changes caused by hormonal imbalances associated with PCOS.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Key biological elements and interactions appropriately represented: demonstrating the liquid follicular environment and immature oocytes, FSH/LH signaling within the capillary network, local insulin and testosterone molecules, and their effects on follicular maturation or retention.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Dynamic Process and Temporal Continuity: Demonstrates the continuous sequence from hormone entry into the bloodstream to ovarian arrival, follicular growth and estrogen secretion, maturation and ovulation (or irregular maturation and cystic retention in PCOS), with timing and duration meeting the task requirements (if applicable).
rubrics_instruction.d4: Viewpoint and Technical Specification Compliance: Microscopic medical animation style focused on a single scene, featuring high-definition details and scientifically accurate textures/annotations; stable camera focus on the specified microscopic target without introducing unrelated macroscopic contexts.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Deduction for Deviations and Conflicts: Points will be deducted for content that conflicts with the instructions, including frequent switching between multiple scenarios, dominance of non-microscopic perspectives, omission of key elements, scientific inaccuracies, or clearly inconsistent settings in quantity or scale.

任务: Under a high-definition microscopic perspective, early-stage lung cancer cells embed into the airway wall, with dynamic extension of the cell membrane interacting with surrounding bodily fluid flow. The cellular morphology and texture are detailed and clear, with vivid colors highlighting the contrast between cancer cells and their environment. The fluid dynamics are realistic and scientifically accurate, presented as a 5-second microscopic animation.
具体要求: Create a stunning and scientifically accurate microscopic world video focusing on the complex journey of cancer cells within the pulmonary environment. The visual masterpiece centers on the detailed visualization of cancer cells at various stages of lung cancer progression, vividly symbolized by bright colors for each stage. It begins with the occult phase, where cancer cells disperse in bodily fluids without visible tumors, progresses to stage 0 where cancer cells start embedding into the airway wall, and further advances through stages IA, IB, IIA, and IIB, with invasive cancer cells penetrating deeper into respiratory tissues. The scenes dynamically transition from a microscopic perspective, showcasing the elusive characteristics of these early cancer cells and their subtle interactions with the aqueous environment, gradually emphasizing their aggressive infiltration as the disease progresses. Detailed textures and visual clarity accelerate audience comprehension, highlighting the unique morphological features of cancer cells at different stages. The video employs high-definition, realistic animation to depict the complexity of fluid dynamics and cellular diversity, evoking a profound understanding of the cellular microenvironment to enhance patient education and awareness. The video should integrate delicate fluid and cellular textures, capturing the astonishing clarity of the microscopic world with unparalleled precision and depth, enabling viewers to grasp the narrative of these relentless entities.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The cell morphology and size ratio correspond to the microscopic scale of lung tissue, demonstrating scientifically relevant morphological differences associated with developmental stages.
rubrics_scientific.d2: The spatial relationship between the airway wall layers and cancer cells is accurately identified, and the invasion depth corresponds to the staging definitions from stage 0 to stage I.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Micro-scale hydrodynamics and cell–fluid/extracellular matrix interactions conform to physical and physiological principles, exhibiting laminar flow, low Reynolds number, and adhesion–deformation coupling.
rubrics_scientific.d4: The timing and progression of stages are presented with logical coherence; inter-stage changes conform to biological time scales and are expressed through juxtaposition or camera transitions to highlight phase differences.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Accurate representation of key elements and scale relationships within the pulmonary microenvironment, including the epithelium, mucus/surfactant layer, basement membrane and extracellular matrix, as well as the relative positioning and density of immune cells.

rubrics_visual.d1: The image exhibits high clarity and detailed texture representation, with sharp edges of microstructures and well-defined layering.
rubrics_visual.d2: Animation and motion are smooth; the physical realism of cell-fluid interactions is credible; camera movements and scene transitions are coherent and stable.
rubrics_visual.d3: High accuracy in 3D modeling and realistic material rendering, with appropriate representation of microscopic material properties such as transparency, viscosity, and glossiness.
rubrics_visual.d4: Color saturation and contrast are natural and realistic, with clear separation between the subject and the environmental colors, avoiding color bleeding and excessive saturation.
rubrics_visual.d5: Visual artifacts are prominent (such as aliasing, color banding, compression noise, or flickering), or motion jitter and stuttering adversely affect image stability.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Comprehensively present the multistage progression and dynamic transitions of early lung cancer within the pulmonary microenvironment, with a focus on the airway wall and fluidic milieu.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Accurately represents key biophysical interactions and structural details: cancer cell embedding within the airway wall, dynamic extension of the cell membrane, and interactions with fluid; clear cell morphology and texture.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Compliance with Technical Specifications and Visual Performance: High-definition microscopic perspective, approximately 5 seconds in duration, realistic fluid dynamics, and vivid colors effectively distinguishing cancer cells from the surrounding environment.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Scientific Accuracy and Alignment with Educational Objectives: Stage definitions, anatomical localization, and behaviors conform to scientific consensus; inter-stage differences are clearly articulated, and information is communicated effectively.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Violation of core directives or absence of key elements (e.g., failure to demonstrate multi-stage progression, lack of microscopic perspective or airway wall/fluid environment, significant deviation in duration, distortion in fluid/cellular representation)

任务: High-definition dynamic presentation of cervical smear cells under the microscope, featuring slight undulations of the semi-transparent cellular membrane, slow movement of the nucleus, subtle intercellular contacts, highlighting vivid contrasting colors and delicate textures to showcase a scientifically accurate and vivid microscopic world visual effect.
具体要求: Create an engaging microscopic world video that delves into the complex process of cervical smear examination, focusing on the vibrant cellular scenes captured under the microscope. This breathtaking visual narrative should depict the precise procedure of collecting cells from the cervix, emphasizing the gentle sweeping motion of the brush or spatula across the cervical surface to obtain the sample. Highlight striking details and textures that reveal the unique patterns and structures of cervical cells, their vivid hues, semi-transparent membranes, and the dynamic interactions between cells observed under laboratory microscopy. Viewers should be captivated by the subtle details of fine fibers, the nuances of the cell nuclei, and the vivid contrasting colors that bring this microscopic world to life. Emphasize an extraordinary and scientifically accurate color palette to illuminate the scene, aiming to inspire awe and appreciation for this critical medical procedure.

rubrics_scientific.d11: The scale relationships among cell morphology, nucleus-to-cytoplasm ratio, and cell-to-cell contact/overlap conform to the biological characteristics of cervical cytology specimens.
rubrics_scientific.d22: The temporal scales and trajectories of plasma membrane fluctuations, slow nuclear migration, and granule/cytoplasmic flow adhere to biophysical principles and the constraints imposed by the microenvironment.
rubrics_scientific.d33: The performance of microscale, magnification, and resolution limit is appropriate, with levels of detail, depth of field, and field of view consistent with those of real optical microscopy imaging.
rubrics_scientific.d44: The coloration and optical imaging conform to cytological staining and transmitted light principles, with consistent and appropriate nuclear-cytoplasmic staining intensity relationships, contrast, and illumination direction, without excessive exaggeration.
rubrics_scientific.d55: The depiction of the cervical cell sampling and slide preparation process is accurate, with the sequence and techniques of sampling instrument contact, smear transfer onto the slide, fixation, and microscopic examination being scientifically standardized.

rubrics_visual.d1: High image clarity and distinguishability of microscopic details, with sharp delineation of fine textures, fibers, and cellular structure boundaries.

任务: Under a high-definition microscopic perspective, display serrated clusters of cancer cells in lung tissue actively penetrating the smooth, elastic membranes of healthy cells, highlighting the stark contrast in morphology and texture between the two. Capture the moment of cancer cell invasion into the alveolar structures, using vivid colors to differentiate cell types, and meticulously present the surface details and microstructures of the cells.
具体要求: Produce a stunning microscopic world video that deeply explores the cellular landscape of lung tissue affected by cancer. In this scene, depict the dynamic and compelling interactions between rogue cancer cells and healthy lung tissue, capturing the relentless spread as malignant cells form clusters and invade surrounding alveolar structures. Emphasize the pronounced textural contrast, showcasing the aggressive morphology of cancer cells with serrated and irregular surfaces in contrast to the smooth and elastic membranes of healthy lung cells. Highlight the proliferation process with vivid detail illustrating cell division and mutation, demonstrating the characteristic rapid growth pattern of lung cancer. Incorporate visual elements conveying therapeutic intervention, showing microscopic agents or drug components interacting with cancer cells, leading to their degradation and apoptosis. Capture the healing process, including regeneration of healthy tissue and the ongoing surveillance and defensive response of the immune system. Present the microscopic world in high definition to ensure viewers can appreciate the subtle nuances of complex structures and cellular behaviors, using vibrant colors to distinguish cell types, treatment effects, and the gradual restoration of healthy lung tissue. This video should serve both as an inspiring educational tool and a captivating visual narrative of the microscopic battle within the human body, emphasizing the importance of medical intervention and the fight against lung cancer.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The morphology, size, and scale proportions of cells and tissues correspond to the microanatomy of lung tissue; the polymorphism of cancer cells and the difference in smoothness and elasticity compared to healthy epithelial cells are realistically represented without exaggeration.
rubrics_scientific.d2: The tumor invasion process is presented according to biological mechanisms, including alterations in cell adhesion, degradation of the basement membrane and extracellular matrix, migration and transmigration, and local tissue remodeling; interfacial mechanics and membrane deformation conform to physical principles at the cellular scale.
rubrics_scientific.d3: The cancer cells primarily invade the alveolar structures by directly puncturing or penetrating the healthy cell plasma membrane, disregarding the basement membrane and stromal barriers.
rubrics_scientific.d4: The temporal sequence of cell proliferation and apoptosis corresponds accurately with phenotypic characteristics; for instance, the mitotic phases occur sequentially, apoptosis is manifested by membrane blebbing and nuclear fragmentation rather than instantaneous rupture, and the proliferation rate is consistent with the tissue microenvironment.
rubrics_scientific.d5: The treatment and immune interaction mechanisms possess biological interpretability, with clear correspondence between drug targets and signaling pathways that elicit rational cellular responses; the processes of immune cell recognition, infiltration, and effector functions (cytotoxicity or phagocytosis) as well as their spatial localization are credible.

rubrics_visual.d1: Image clarity and fine representation of microscopic details, with clearly defined structural layers free from blurring and distortion
rubrics_visual.d2: Color saturation and contrast are natural; color coding distinctly differentiates elements; overall tone is uniform without color casts or banding.
rubrics_visual.d3: Animation timing and motion smoothness; micro-interactions and deformation continuity remain stable without stuttering, flickering, or motion artifacts.
rubrics_visual.d4: The 3D modeling demonstrates high accuracy and realistic material texture, with well-represented surface roughness, elasticity, and translucency, and exhibits pronounced morphological contrast.
rubrics_visual.d5: Lighting and depth of field are handled naturally; volumetric lighting, subsurface scattering, and specular highlights are well-balanced; the main subject exhibits clear layering without overexposure or underexposure.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Fully depict the microscopic scene of lung tissue in which serrated clusters of cancer cells penetrate the smooth elastic membranes of healthy cells and invade the alveolar structures, highlighting the contrast in morphology and texture between the two.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Coherently demonstrate the key biological process cascade from invasion to outcome: invasion and diffusion, proliferation (cell division and mutation), therapeutic intervention and drug interactions, cancer cell degradation and apoptosis, healthy tissue regeneration, immune surveillance and defense.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Present cellular surface and microstructural details from a high-resolution microscopic perspective, employing vivid and consistent color coding to differentiate cell types, therapeutic effects, and stages of tissue recovery.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Shot and Duration Arrangement for Service Cognition: Maintains a stable microscopic perspective with clear focus; critical segments have sufficient duration to facilitate recognition; pacing is coherent, and transitions aid comprehension without disrupting continuity.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Content Avoiding Conflict with Instructions and Omissions: Do not replace microscopic features with macroscopic perspectives; do not attenuate or omit alveolar structures and textural contrasts; do not misrepresent the morphology of cancer cells as regular and smooth; do not omit treatment- and immunity-related aspects.

任务: Under high magnification microscopy, clusters of ovarian cancer cells invade adjacent normal ovarian cells, with dynamic cell membrane fluidity and slight movement of surface protein granules. The DNA replication structures within the nuclei are clearly visible, and the early stages of cell division are dynamically presented. The background consists of extracellular fluid with subtle agitation of particles, simulating the effect of potential therapeutic agents. Color contrast highlights the abnormalities of the cancer cells, with the overall focus on the microscopic interactions between cancerous and healthy cells.
具体要求: Create a stunning microscopic world video showcasing the cellular and molecular landscape of ovarian tissue affected by cancer. The central subject of this scene is a cluster of ovarian cancer cells displayed under high magnification. These cells exhibit uncontrolled growth, demonstrating their invasive characteristics as they interact with surrounding normal ovarian tissue. Complex animations capture the active process of cell division, with exceptional clarity highlighting key components such as nuclei, mitochondria, and various microtubules. The video dynamically depicts the invasion of cancer cells into healthy cells, illustrating the aggressive progression of the disease. In terms of detail and texture, emphasis is placed on the cell surface features, including membrane fluidity, granularity of surface proteins, and internal organelle structures. Vivid colors and rich contrast emphasize the distinctions between healthy and cancerous cells, with particular attention to structural changes in DNA during replication. Background details show particle agitation within the interstitial fluid, simulating the extracellular environment’s response, including potential therapeutic agents aimed at targeting cancer cells. This video should serve as an educational tool providing microscopic insights into ovarian cancer pathology, underscoring the importance of treatment and lifestyle modifications in disease management.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The morphology, structural details, and relative scale of cells and organelles are accurately depicted, with reasonable representation of the differences between ovarian cancer cells and normal ovarian cells in nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio, cytoskeletal density, and cell membrane characteristics.
rubrics_scientific.d2: The temporal sequence and key morphological markers of DNA replication and early mitotic events are consistent with established cell biological principles, and the dynamics of intranuclear replication structures, chromatin condensation, and spindle apparatus association are appropriately represented.
rubrics_scientific.d3: The mechanisms underlying cancer cell invasion into adjacent normal cells and interactions with the microenvironment are scientifically sound, accurately encompassing processes such as adhesion alterations, pseudopodia formation, and matrix remodeling, with precise directionality and spatial relationships.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Cell membrane fluidity, the movement of surface protein particles, and the dynamics of particles in the extracellular fluid adhere to physicochemical laws, reflecting characteristics of lateral diffusion and Brownian motion, with velocity and trajectories consistent with viscosity and scale.
rubrics_scientific.d5: The mechanism of action of the potential therapeutic agent is consistent with the cellular response, with target localization, endocytosis, or signaling pathway alterations presenting within a reasonable temporal and intensity range corresponding to changes in the cytoskeleton and cell division processes.

rubrics_visual.d1: The image exhibits high clarity and discernibility of microstructures, with organelles and subcellular structural details presenting clean edges and accurate textural features.
rubrics_visual.d2: Color representation and contrast are natural and harmonious, with clear color differentiation between healthy and cancerous cells, and an overall consistent microscopic style without color bias.
rubrics_visual.d3: The animation is smooth and stable, coordinated with the biological motion rhythm; the cell membrane fluidity, as well as the movement of granules and internal structures, is continuous and free of stuttering.
rubrics_visual.d4: The 3D modeling and material rendering are realistic, with clear surface textures and internal layering of the cell, exhibiting good consistency in scale and morphology.
rubrics_visual.d5: Visual artifacts such as significant noise, banding, compression blocks, aliasing, motion tearing, or excessive sharpening/saturation that noticeably degrade the microscopic texture.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Comprehensively depict the core scene from the perspective of high-magnification microscopy, focusing on the interaction and invasion process between ovarian cancer cell clusters and adjacent normal ovarian cells.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Clearly articulate the key cellular structures and dynamic processes specified in the instructions (such as the nucleus and DNA replication structures, early stages of cell division, microtubules and mitochondria, cell membrane fluidity, and the movement of surface protein particles).
rubrics_instruction.d3: Visual presentation and viewpoint technical specifications compliance: color and contrast effectively highlight healthy versus cancerous differences; lens and focus remain stable on the core subject; magnification and resolution correspond appropriately to task requirements.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Accurate reconstruction of background environment and external factors: extracellular fluid particulates exhibit slight agitation, and the interaction with potential therapeutic agents is appropriately visualized in a discernible manner without overshadowing the primary subject.
rubrics_instruction.d5: The overall duration and pacing facilitate information comprehension; the narrative is coherent, balancing scientific rigor with educational value, thereby supporting use in science communication and teaching.

任务: From a microscopic perspective, the electrocautery loop precisely excises the prostate cell layer, with cellular structures and ultrastructural glandular textures clearly visible. The excised tissue fragments float in sterile fluid, the electrocautery loop coil flickers faintly, and subtle luminescence flows between cells. The environment is minimalistic, emphasizing the cutting action and microscopic cellular architecture.
具体要求: Create a breathtaking, immersive video exploring the microscopic world of transurethral resection of the prostate (TURP), highlighting the complexity and precision involved in this critical surgical procedure. Focus on the walnut-sized prostate gland located at the base of the bladder, demonstrating the surgical instruments delicately navigating through the urethra. Depict the meticulous insertion of a cystoscope equipped with a light source through the penile urethral meatus, followed by the infusion of sterile solution to enhance visibility. Capture the expert guidance of the resection loop powered by microcurrents, precisely excising thin layers of prostate tissue. The excised tissue fragments should be shown floating toward the bladder, accompanied by the gentle flow of sterile fluid guided by the latest cystoscopic technology, ensuring clean extraction through a dedicated channel. Emphasize the ultrastructural texture of the prostate cellular architecture and the shimmering coil of the electrocautery loop, as well as the sterile solution carrying tissue fragments flowing into the bladder. Pause to appreciate the bright and intricate details of the prostate, urethra, and bladder as seen through the cystoscope, and highlight the postoperative placement of a catheter to maintain urinary flow. Portray a tranquil postoperative environment, microscopically showing an intravenous line administering antibiotics for prophylactic infection control. Finally, demonstrate the elegant flow of fluid infused through the catheter, clearing clots and residual debris, capturing the rapid recovery process experienced by most patients within two days after surgery.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The anatomical relationship of the transurethral pathway and the instrument position is accurate, with the prostate located at the bladder base. The relative positions and movement trajectories of the cystoscope, resection loop, and irrigation/return channels conform to clinical endoscopic operation standards.
rubrics_scientific.d2: The microarchitecture and scale of the prostatic glandular tissue relative to the surrounding tissues are appropriate; the proportions of glandular lumens, epithelial cells, and stromal texture correspond to histological norms in terms of size ratios, and maintain consistent cross-scale relationships with the dimensions of the excision ring.
rubrics_scientific.d3: The physical and physiological manifestations of electrocautery energy interaction with tissue are accurate, demonstrating coagulation/vaporization boundaries, hemostatic responses, and thermal diffusion ranges that are consistent and proportional. Optical and electrical effects in the liquid medium conform to electrosurgical principles without exaggeration, exhibiting no large electric arcs or bioluminescence phenomena.
rubrics_scientific.d4: The rheology of the sterile perfusate and the transport of tissue fragments conform to the dynamics of endoscopic perfusion, with appropriate flow direction, flow rate, and pressure gradient; tissue fragments float toward the bladder and are recovered via a dedicated channel, and the postoperative continuous irrigation path and efficacy through the urinary catheter are reliable.
rubrics_scientific.d5: The surgical sequence and physiological causal chain are scientifically rigorous: the timing and coordination of endoscope insertion and perfusion, layered resection and hemostasis, debris retrieval, catheter placement and postoperative irrigation, as well as intravenous antibiotic administration and early recovery are consistent and appropriately responsive.

rubrics_visual.d1: High image clarity with strong discernibility of microscopic details; the main subject (cutting action and cellular/tissue structures) is prominent, with clear edges and well-defined layers.
rubrics_visual.d2: The color and lighting effects are natural and harmonious, with appropriate control of color saturation and contrast, and stable color accuracy; the direction and intensity of illumination are reasonable, with finely controlled depth of field and subtle shadows/highlights without glare.
rubrics_visual.d3: The animation and camera movements are smooth, with coherent motion trajectories of cutting actions, slow flow of sterile fluids, and tissue fragment drift that maintain a physically plausible scale, free of stuttering or frame drops.
rubrics_visual.d4: The 3D modeling and material details are realistic, with accurate geometric proportions. The instrument and tissue surface textures, normal/displacement maps, and subsurface scattering effects are finely rendered, with textures free of stretching and repetition artifacts.
rubrics_visual.d5: Artifacts, noise, aliasing, compression blocks, banding, ghosting, and overexposure/oversaturation defects are prominent.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Fully implement the core micro-scale scenarios and dissection actions described in the task, emphasizing the textural details and ultrastructural patterns at the target tissue and cellular levels.
