任务: From a microscopic perspective, within the slowly flowing blood surrounding the venous valves of the lower leg, red blood cells and fibrin gradually aggregate to form an initial thrombus. The imagery is clear and detailed, emphasizing the thrombus structure, blood components, and the microanatomical details of the venous valves, illustrating the dynamic process of thrombus formation.
具体要求: Produce a stunning and insightful video that delves into the microscopic world of deep vein thrombosis (DVT), with a focus on the complexity of thrombus formation within the veins of the lower leg. The scene begins with a tranquil view of slowly flowing blood inside the vein, capturing the progressive accumulation of red blood cells and various blood components around the venous valves. This aggregation leads to thrombus formation, which significantly impedes blood circulation by causing venous obstruction. As the action unfolds, depict the moment when the thrombus detaches from the valve leaflets, becoming an embolus. The video then transitions to a dramatic sequence showing the embolus traveling through the circulatory system, passing through the heart, and ultimately lodging in the pulmonary vasculature, laying the foundation for life-threatening pulmonary embolism. Throughout the animation, emphasize the vivid and intricate details and textures of the clot components, focusing on blood cell aggregation, the fibrin network, as well as the fine structures of the venous valves and pulmonary vessels, creating a rich visual experience that highlights the anatomical and physiological complexity of this critical medical condition.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The microanatomical morphology and dimensional proportions of the venous valves and the deep veins of the calf are accurate, with clear hierarchical relationships among the valve leaflets, valve sinuses, and the vascular wall layers.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Local hemodynamics exhibit characteristics consistent with low venous shear stress and flow stasis, with flow direction and velocity gradients appropriately aligned and corresponding to the location of the sinus reflux region.
rubrics_scientific.d3: The thrombus formation mechanism and composition exhibit characteristics consistent with venous red thrombi, predominantly composed of red blood cells and fibrin networks, initiating and proliferating around the valve.
rubrics_scientific.d4: The process of embolus detachment, migration, and lodgment in pulmonary embolism conforms to anatomical pathways and size constraints, traveling through the deep veins, inferior vena cava, right heart, and pulmonary artery, and causing obstruction at a correspondingly matched vessel diameter.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Temporal consistency with physicochemical scales is well maintained; the coagulation reaction time course, fibrin polymerization and blood cell aggregation rates, as well as blood flow velocity, are appropriately coordinated with the cardiac cycle.

rubrics_visual.d1: Image clarity and fine depiction of microscopic details, with sharp structural boundaries, rich texture information, and well-controlled noise and compression artifacts.

rubrics_visual.d3: Animation exhibits excellent smoothness and physical plausibility, with continuous and seamless microscale fluid and particle motion; aggregation, separation, and deformation transitions occur naturally without jitter.
rubrics_visual.d4: Lighting, shadows, and volumetric scattering are rendered naturally; depth of field is properly controlled; translucent media and subsurface scattering exhibit clear layering; no overexposure or hard shadows are present.
rubrics_visual.d5: Color and contrast are consistent and distinctive, with clear differentiation of key component colors; saturation and brightness are controlled to prevent distortion and glare.

rubrics_instruction.d2: Inclusion and Identifiability of Key Structures and Elements: Accurate restoration and clear distinguishability of core components such as venous valves and venous lumen, red blood cells and fibrin networks (including platelets and other blood constituents), as well as the heart and pulmonary vasculature, with appropriate spatial and scale relationships.

rubrics_instruction.d5: Technical Specifications and Presentation Alignment: Adherence to the duration, resolution, and pacing requirements specified in the instructions; text annotations or narration consistently correspond with the visual content and enhance comprehension, without redundant or distracting information.

任务: From a microscopic perspective, the detailed features of the granular calcification on the stenotic aortic valve leaflets are clearly visible, with the blood vessel rhythmically pulsating slightly. A surgical catheter slowly passes through the leaflets, demonstrating the microscopic dynamics of valvular pathology and interventional surgery, with a clean background that highlights the leaflet texture and the medical minimally invasive scene.
具体要求: Create an engaging microscopic world video that deeply explores the complex procedure of transcatheter aortic valve implantation (TAVI), focusing primarily on the heart and aortic valve. The scene begins with a detailed depiction of the stenotic aortic valve affected by calcification, showcasing clear imagery of the calcified leaflets surrounded by rhythmically pulsating blood vessels, emphasizing the significance of this medical procedure. As the footage progresses, focus on the meticulous step-by-step advancement of the transfemoral catheter-based aortic valve implantation, highlighting how the procedure is carefully performed to replace the dysfunctional, calcified, and stenotic aortic valve, all within the dynamic internal environment of the heart. Ensure that every detail and texture, from the granular calcification on the leaflets to the smooth movement of the surgical instruments through the vessels, is captured with extremely high clarity, revealing the rich textures and complex details of these biological structures to create an awe-inspiring visual experience. The microscopic perspective allows the audience to appreciate the transformative nature of this life-saving procedure, immersing them deeply in the anatomy and advanced medical technology involved in this minimally invasive surgery.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The anatomical morphology and positional relationships of the heart, aortic valve, and adjacent structures are accurate, including the number of valve leaflets and their attachment points, the location of the coronary ostia relative to the aortic sinuses, and the continuity between the aortic root and ascending aorta.
rubrics_scientific.d2: The timing of leaflet opening and closing during the cardiac cycle, the direction and velocity profiles of blood flow, as well as the amplitude and frequency of vascular wall pulsations, conform to hemodynamic and physiological principles; the jet stream and pressure gradient resulting from stenosis are appropriately represented.
rubrics_scientific.d3: The lesion phenotype and tissue characteristics are consistent, with granular or laminar textures of leaflet calcification, thickening and sclerosis causing restricted mobility and reduced orifice area, and morphological alterations of the annulus presenting coherently.
rubrics_scientific.d4: The interventional approach and procedural steps are scientifically standardized, including transfemoral access, guidewire/catheter traversal across the valve, sequence of device positioning and deployment, as well as the mechanical interaction and tissue deformation between valve annulus anchoring and native valve elements, all demonstrating rationality.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Scientific Negative Criteria: Presence of critical errors such as inversion of anatomical locations, instrument pathways traversing incorrect chambers, reversal of blood flow direction, severe distortion of size proportions between instruments and tissues, malpositioning deviating from the valvular annulus, or obstruction of coronary ostia.

任务: From a microscopic perspective, red blood cells and blood components slowly accumulate at the venous valves within the calf veins, gradually forming a spherical and slightly irregular thrombus. The intravascular environment details are clearly depicted, highlighting the contrasting textures and colors between the thrombus and the vascular tissue, showcasing the dynamic microscopic process of thrombus formation.
具体要求: Create a captivating microscopic world video illustrating the complex phenomenon of venous thrombosis, beginning with a detailed depiction of thrombus formation in the calf veins. Capture the slow and almost mesmerizing movement of red blood cells as they flow through the veins, showing how the velocity changes as an increasing number of cells and other blood components adhere to the fragile venous valves. Vividly portray these elements gradually aggregating into a thrombus, emphasizing the contrasting textures and colors between the blood cells and the surrounding vascular tissue, and highlighting the spherical yet slightly irregular surface of the thrombus. This visual journey continues to depict the detachment of the thrombus, transforming into an embolus, and commencing its perilous journey toward the heart and lungs. Use blood flow animation to demonstrate this movement with dramatic realism, allowing viewers to observe the embolus navigating through lifelike vascular interiors. The animation concludes with the embolus lodging in the pulmonary tissue, strikingly illustrating its potential to cause life-threatening pulmonary embolism. Emphasize the complex interactions between blood components and lung tissue, underscoring the severe impact on blood flow and oxygen exchange.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Accuracy of Venous Anatomy and Scale Relationships: The morphology, location, and relative dimensions of the calf veins' lumen, tunica adventitia, bicuspid venous valves, and valvular sinuses correspond to physiological proportions; the sizes and magnitudes of red blood cells, platelets, and fibrin are appropriately matched.
rubrics_scientific.d2: The mechanism and composition of thrombosis are correctly presented: under low shear conditions, red blood cells are primarily trapped within a fibrin network accompanied by platelet involvement; the thrombus originates at the valvular sinus or leaflet margin and extends into the lumen; the thrombus is globular with a slightly irregular surface consistent with the local flow field.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Hemodynamics and flow patterns consistent with physiology: venous return is slow and influenced by valves as well as muscular and respiratory pumps; stasis and vortices form in the sinus of the valve, promoting aggregation; emboli detach and travel through the inferior vena cava—right heart—pulmonary artery in a forward direction with appropriate directional and velocity gradients.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Temporal Sequence and Causal Chain Science: Stasis/endothelial injury/hypercoagulability trigger the coagulation cascade → initial adhesion → growth and stabilization → partial detachment forming emboli → obstruction of pulmonary circulation perfusion leading to ventilation-perfusion mismatch and decreased oxygenation; temporal compression does not disrupt the sequential logic.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Exclusion criteria for critical scientific errors: anatomical or pathological location misidentification (e.g., placing the embolus within the lung parenchyma or alveoli instead of the pulmonary artery lumen), confusion of arterial and venous flow patterns and directions, depiction of venous thrombi as high-shear white thrombus characteristics, etc.

rubrics_visual.d2: Animation coherence and physical plausibility are maintained, with smooth temporal rhythms in microfluidic and particle aggregation, adhesion, formation, detachment, and migration processes; velocity and inertial variations occur naturally without jitter.
rubrics_visual.d3: The 3D modeling and material rendering are realistic, with structurally proportionate and well-formed shapes. Irregular surface details, surface roughness, and adhesive interfaces are accurately represented, demonstrating good consistency of biological material properties.
rubrics_visual.d4: Lighting and depth of field are naturally controlled and stable; translucency, subsurface scattering, and volumetric scattering are rendered convincingly; hue and saturation are consistent with contrast emphasizing the subject.
rubrics_visual.d5: Rendering and compositing defects are effectively controlled, preventing noise, aliasing, banding, texture crawling, inter-frame flickering, clipping, incorrect motion blur, and compression artifacts.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Complete presentation of the sequential process from component aggregation at the calf vein valves → formation of spherical, slightly irregular thrombi → detachment of thrombi forming emboli → emboli traveling via the bloodstream to the lungs and lodging, resulting in pulmonary embolism.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Using a microscopic endoscopic perspective, the venous environment and valve structures are clearly reconstructed, with the main features consistently distinguishable and the imagery coherent.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Accurately reflect the key elements and relationships: erythrocytes and other blood components, venous valves, vascular walls, and the texture and color contrast of the thrombus; the thrombus appearance is globular and slightly irregular.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Dynamic process performance is appropriate: erythrocytes exhibit slow movement with velocity changes and adhesion at the valve sites, gradually aggregating into clusters; thrombi are driven by blood flow to advance along anatomically plausible paths, with motion exhibiting realism.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Temporal and Shot Conventions: Narrative segments fully encompass key phases; pacing and dwell time facilitate detailed observation while avoiding irrelevant shots and perspective shifts that cause information loss.

任务: Microscopic structure of the nephron under the microscope, with blood cells flowing through the glomerular capillary network and clearly defined cell membrane texture. The focus is on the dialysis membrane surface, demonstrating the effective filtration of waste and fluid in the blood through membrane micropores. The pulsation of the fluid and the vivid color contrast highlight the dynamic scene of the kidney's microscopic filtration process.
具体要求: Create an engaging microscopic world video that delves into the anatomy and physiology of the kidney, with a special emphasis on the extraordinary processes involved in hemodialysis and peritoneal dialysis. The central focus of this video is the kidney's microscopic structure, particularly the nephron, which is the fundamental unit responsible for filtering waste and excess fluid. In this captivating scene, observe the complex and dynamic flow of blood cells through the intricate renal vascular network. Witness the detailed interaction of the dialysis machine during hemodialysis, demonstrating how harmful toxins and fluid accumulation are effectively removed from the blood while essential nutrients are replenished. During the peritoneal dialysis segment, transition to the delicate peritoneum to seamlessly illustrate waste and electrolyte exchange, providing insight into a cleansing mechanism that does not require external blood extraction. Pay special attention to capturing the distinct texture and color contrast of cell membranes, the subtle pulsation of fluid transport, and the vibrant activity within microscopic channels. These visual complexities showcase the intricacy of renal function and the transformative impact of dialysis therapy in maintaining bodily homeostasis.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The glomerulus and nephron microstructures are accurately presented, with filtration barrier layers and selective permeability characteristics consistent with scientific principles.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Reasonable scale and proportion: the sizes of erythrocytes, capillaries, membrane pore diameters, and common solute dimensions are appropriately matched; the time series reflects differences in filtration, diffusion, and convection rates.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Microcirculation and hemodynamics conform to physiological principles: laminar flow predominance, appropriate red blood cell deformation and distribution, and correct representation of glomerular ultrafiltration pressure and dialysis transmembrane pressure.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Accurate depiction of dialysis mechanisms: Hemodialysis demonstrates countercurrent flow between blood and dialysate, diffusion gradients, and ultrafiltration driven by transmembrane pressure; peritoneal dialysis illustrates solute exchange across a semipermeable membrane and net water transport driven by osmotic gradients.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Avoidance of critical scientific errors: do not depict cells or macromolecules crossing filtration membranes or peritoneal membranes; do not allow direct mixing of blood and dialysate; do not invert the positional relationship of nephron structures.

rubrics_visual.d2: Animation Smoothness and Microscopic Dynamics Consistency: The flow of particles/cells, liquid pulsation, and material exchange are continuous and smooth, with natural transitions in velocity and deformation, exhibiting no jittering or tearing between frames.
rubrics_visual.d3: Color Rendering and Contrast Control: The color layering of key elements is distinct, with natural saturation and contrast levels that highlight the focal points of the process while maintaining the authenticity and readability of the organizational colors.
rubrics_visual.d4: 3D Modeling Accuracy and Material Realism: Microscopic structural proportions and topology are clearly defined, with material parameters (transparency, subsurface scattering, roughness, anisotropy) exhibiting credible performance.
rubrics_visual.d5: Lighting and Shadow Effects with Depth of Field Guidance: Microscale illumination exhibits appropriate directionality and softness; highlights, refraction, and volumetric scattering are naturally controlled; depth of field effectively emphasizes the narrative focal point while preserving spatial layering.

任务: Dynamic close-up of the pyloric sphincter muscle fibers and connective tissue under high-definition microscopy, illustrating the microscopic structural changes caused by muscle layer contraction leading to channel narrowing in pyloric stenosis. Vivid colors and rich textures highlight the anatomical features of the pyloric sphincter, suitable for a 5-second short dynamic presentation.
具体要求: Create an engaging microscopic world video that delves into the complex structure of the pyloric sphincter, stunningly showcasing close-up views of the hypertrophied muscle layer responsible for pyloric stenosis. Set the scene with dynamic visuals to demonstrate how this narrowing affects the digestive process in infants, emphasizing the progressive motility and functional changes of the gastric outlet before and after the condition. Focus on the details of the pyloromyotomy procedure, illustrating the surgeon’s precise incisions and manipulations that enlarge the pyloric channel. Textures should be rendered in vivid resolution to reveal muscle fibers and connective tissue, capturing the transformation as the pyloric passage opens and displaying anatomically accurate, finely tuned changes. Utilize high-definition microscopy to captivate the audience with seamless integration of vivid color and texture, reflecting the unique biological architecture of the sphincter. This exploration should not only educate but also fascinate viewers with its precision and depth, providing insights into the complexity of infantile pyloric stenosis and the transformative impact of pyloromyotomy.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The anatomical layers and tissue composition of the pyloric sphincter are accurately presented: the muscle layer hypertrophy predominantly composed of circular smooth muscle is clearly demonstrated, with a rational orientation and thickness of muscle fibers; the spatial positioning and boundaries of the mucosa, submucosa, and connective tissue are well-defined.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Causality and temporal sequence between contraction–relaxation and luminal diameter changes conform to physiological and pathological mechanisms: smooth muscle contraction leads to luminal narrowing; visual manifestations of peristalsis before and after stenosis correspond with changes in gastric emptying; short-term displays do not disrupt the causal order.
rubrics_scientific.d3: The pyloromyotomy procedure and outcome comply with medical standards: a longitudinal incision is performed through the serosa and hypertrophied muscular layer without breaching the mucosa; following the incision, appropriate mucosal bulging and luminal dilation are observed; the instrument application site and incision direction are precise.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Scale and Proportion Science: The relative proportions of muscle layer thickness, muscle fiber diameter, connective tissue porosity, and lumen diameter are accurate; the microscopic magnification is consistent with the size range of the infant pylorus; texture enhancement does not alter the true scale relationships.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Exaggerated or erroneous imaging and effects causing scientific misinterpretation: excessive color mapping, texture sharpening, or unreasonable distortion leading to misjudgment of tissue architecture, boundaries, or material properties.

任务: From a microscopic perspective, demonstrate the intricate structure of healthy peripheral nerve fibers, highlighting the layered myelin sheath and the grooves on the axonal surface. Dynamically illustrate the rapid conduction of nerve signals along the axon, and compare the microscopic manifestations of single nerve fiber damage in mononeuropathy with multiple nerve fiber damage in polyneuropathy. The synaptic cleft exhibits subtle color changes in response to pulsatile neurotransmitter release, with altered texture and coloration in damaged areas. The overall coloration is realistic and the texture rich, reflecting the microscopic mechanisms of efficient and impaired nerve signal transmission.
具体要求: Produce a stunning microscopic world video focusing on the complex anatomy and physiology of peripheral nerves, with an in-depth exploration of the complexities of mononeuropathy and polyneuropathy. The scenes should vividly depict the delicate interplay of nerve fibers, emphasizing single nerve damage in mononeuropathy and multiple nerve damage in polyneuropathy. Use dynamic animation sequences of nerve signal propagation along axons to captivate the audience and illustrate the destruction caused by nerve injury. Each frame should be richly detailed with full textures, emphasizing the unique layered structure of the myelin sheath and demonstrating its impact on efficient nerve signal transmission. The surface of each axon should shimmer with the microscopic appearance of grooves, while the synaptic cleft displays varying colors corresponding to pulsatile neurotransmitter activity, effectively drawing attention to potential health issues and therapeutic approaches. The microscopic complexity should be so finely detailed and texturally rich that viewers can almost perceive the tactile properties of these microstructures, gaining a profound understanding of neuropathic conditions and their treatments.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The hierarchical organization of peripheral nerve fibers (axon, myelin lamellae, Schwann cells, and nodes of Ranvier) is accurately presented, with morphology and size proportions consistent with anatomical scale relationships, and the expression of ultrastructural layers appropriately demonstrated.
rubrics_scientific.d2: The description of saltatory conduction of neural signals along myelinated axons and the pattern of membrane potential changes are accurate; the conduction direction and timing are appropriate, and the conduction velocity is consistent with the relationship to fiber diameter and myelin sheath thickness.
rubrics_scientific.d3: The distribution of lesions and pathological mechanisms in mononeuropathy versus polyneuropathy demonstrate distinct patterns, reflecting differences between focal and diffuse involvement, as well as between demyelination and axonal degeneration, which affect conduction efficiency and synchrony.
rubrics_scientific.d4: The structural scale of the synaptic cleft aligns with the dynamics of neurotransmitters consistent with physiological processes; the pulse release, diffusion, and clearance/reuptake occur coherently, and the signal modulation driven by concentration changes is expressed appropriately.
rubrics_scientific.d5: The consistency between scale and physicochemical principles is maintained: molecular and ionic movements follow diffusion and electric field-driven dynamics, with temporal and spatial scales properly matched; color and texture mappings correspond to membrane potential, neurotransmitter concentration, and myelin integrity, without exhibiting exaggerated effects that violate established principles.

rubrics_visual.d2: The 3D modeling and material realism are high, with consistent geometric scale and reasonable proportions; the micro-surface roughness and subsurface scattering are well-executed, and the normal/displacement maps are detailed and free of stretching.
rubrics_visual.d3: Color and contrast are naturally unified; dynamic color variations convey clear semantic meaning without excess; saturation is properly controlled; tonal gradations are complete with no color cast or overexposure.
rubrics_visual.d4: Animation is smooth with clear temporal and logical sequencing; propagation direction and velocity transitions are seamless; particles, wavefronts, and energy flows are coherent; camera movements and focal shifts are stable, without stuttering or motion blur.

Contact shadows are precise, depth of field transitions are smooth, and volumetric scattering and glow are applied appropriately to enhance spatial layering.

任务: From a microscopic perspective, fetal abdominal organs slowly herniate through a diaphragmatic defect, compressing the developing lungs. The diaphragm and organs dynamically interact, showcasing tissue textures and fluid dynamics with realistic and detailed imagery, scientifically accurate.
具体要求: Create a stunning and highly detailed microscopic world video focusing on the central theme of congenital diaphragmatic hernia. This complex animation should depict the subtle process of fetal abdominal organs passing through the diaphragmatic defect during gestation. Emphasize the intricate dynamics involved, portraying this complex physiological process with maximum realism and scientific precision. Highlight the smooth motion and interaction between the diaphragm and the invading organs, illustrating the contrast between normal diaphragmatic function and the altered state caused by the hernia. Accurately capture fluid movement, thoroughly describe tissue textures and the structural integrity of the involved organs, including the lungs. The animation should demonstrate the compression and challenges imposed on the developing lungs by the hernia, adding tension and emphasizing the severity of the condition. Clearly depict the key surgical steps involved in repairing the congenital diaphragmatic hernia, showcasing precision and meticulousness in handling fragile tissues. Ensure the visuals are richly detailed with realistic textures and dynamic motion, bringing this medical phenomenon to life and creating an engaging narrative that is both educational and accurately represents the critical medical concepts involved.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The anatomical structures and spatial relationships of the fetal diaphragm, lungs, and abdominal organs, as well as their size proportions, conform to biological standards.
rubrics_scientific.d2: The process of organ herniation through the diaphragmatic defect and subsequent pulmonary compression, the comparison between normal and abnormal diaphragmatic motion, as well as the dynamical presentation of pulmonary hypoplasia, conform to fetal physiology and biomechanical principles.
rubrics_scientific.d3: The biomechanical behavior and interfacial interaction of the tissue are scientifically consistent with actual tissue properties in terms of tissue compliance and viscoelasticity, sliding contact and tensile recoil, as well as wettability and low friction performance.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Fetal fluid environment and fluid dynamics accuracy: The lungs are filled with liquid exhibiting microflow rather than ventilation; the direction, velocity, and pressure variations of fluid in the thoracic and abdominal cavities as well as blood flow conform to fetal circulation and fluid mechanics principles.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Scientific sequence and steps of congenital diaphragmatic hernia surgical repair: reduction of herniated contents, assessment and closure of defect margins (suture or patch reconstruction), and appropriate instrument-tissue interaction with controlled force application.

rubrics_visual.d2: The dynamics and interactions between soft tissues and fluids are naturally coherent, with stable contact, sliding, deformation, and recovery, exhibiting no non-physical interpenetration or volume loss.
rubrics_visual.d3: High-quality 3D modeling and material hierarchy, clean structural proportions and topology, stable details in subdivision and displacement/normal maps, with material properties consistent with volumetric perception.
rubrics_visual.d4: Light and color are managed naturally and realistically, with accurate volumetric lighting, subsurface scattering, smooth shadow transitions, and precise depth of field control; hue, saturation, and contrast are well balanced.
rubrics_visual.d5: The animation and camera movement exhibit high smoothness, with seamless frame-to-frame transitions free of jitter and flicker; the pacing is clear, and the shifts in focus and composition remain stable, effectively highlighting key actions.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Comprehensively present the core pathological process of congenital diaphragmatic hernia: gradual herniation of fetal abdominal organs through a diaphragmatic defect with subsequent compression of the developing lungs, emphasizing the seamless interaction between the diaphragm and the herniated organs, and contrasting normal versus abnormal states; the narrative consistently remains focused on the topic.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Ensure scientific accuracy and anatomical consistency: organ location, scale, and developmental stage are appropriately matched; lung compression is realistically depicted; physiological mechanisms are expressed rigorously; avoid exaggerated or misleading representations.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Key elements and details accurately presented: clearly demonstrate tissue texture, surface details, fluid dynamics, and tissue-fluid coupling, reflecting realistic materials and a high level of detail.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Technical and Perspective Compliance with Instructions: The microscopic perspective remains stable and consistent, focusing on the defect area; camera angles and pacing facilitate observation of dynamic changes; duration, resolution, and lighting meet the visualization requirements specified by the task.

任务: Under an ultra-high-definition microscopic perspective, electrical signals rapidly propagate as bright light flows along the myocardial fiber network. Catheter ablation precisely targets abnormal conduction pathways, instantaneously altering the flow of electrical signals, highlighting the dynamic changes in myocardial cellular structures, fiber textures, and ablation sites.
具体要求: Create a stunning microscopic world video that thoroughly explores the primary themes of the cardiac conduction system at an extraordinary cellular level, demonstrating its critical role in regulating heart rate and rhythm through complex electrical signaling. In this captivating scene, emphasize the dynamic process of catheter ablation, which strategically targets and neutralizes problematic pathways within the conduction system to precisely correct arrhythmias. Capture every subtle detail and texture of this phenomenon, focusing on the transmission of electrical signals within cardiac cells and highlighting the intricate interactions and structural beauty of myocardial tissue. Explore the finely interwoven textures of cardiac cells, including the delicate fiber networks that form conduction pathways and facilitate smooth, rhythmic contractions. Stress the precision of catheter ablation techniques as they skillfully navigate the complex anatomical maze of the heart to restore harmonious cardiac function and eliminate arrhythmias. The video should combine high-definition visual effects to immerse viewers in the molecular artistry of cardiac physiology, ensuring it serves as a valuable resource for patient education and provides deep insight into advanced cardiac care methods.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Anatomical accuracy and spatial relationships of the cardiac conduction system, including the sinoatrial node, atrioventricular node, His bundle/branch bundles/Purkinje network, and the relative orientation of myocardial fibers; catheter contact and path along the endocardial surface are appropriate and consistent.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Correct propagation pattern of electrical activity: depolarization wavefront advances according to physiological sequence with anisotropic conduction; physiological atrioventricular nodal delay is evident; repolarization and refractory periods appropriately limit conduction; corresponding conduction delays or blocks occur before and after ablation, and rhythm changes are consistent with scientific mechanisms.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Spatial and temporal consistency: Relative size proportions among cells, fibers, gap junctions, and lesions are appropriate; propagation velocity corresponds to frame rate, with electrical activity preceding contraction and exhibiting a reasonable delay in electro-mechanical coupling; lesion extent corresponds appropriately to the applied energy.
rubrics_scientific.d4: The ultrastructure and interactions of cells are scientifically demonstrated: myocardial striations and intercalated disks/gap junction-mediated electrical coupling are appropriately presented; the fiber network is continuous with clearly defined anisotropy; the texture and boundaries of the ablation lesions correspond to histological characteristics without involving untargeted distal structures.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Deductions for Major Scientific Errors: depicting electrical signals as freely propagating through blood, fat, or fibrotic isolation tissue while neglecting atrioventricular nodal delay and anisotropy; or representing ablation as an instantaneous remote-beam incision with the original abnormal pathway remaining unaffected and unobstructed.

rubrics_visual.d1: Image clarity and fine presentation of micro-scale details are achieved, with sharp edges of critical microstructures and effective control of noise and compression artifacts.
rubrics_visual.d2: Animation smoothness and dynamical consistency; continuous and stable microscopic signal/energy flow; coordinated timing of interaction and organizational responses; absence of jitter and frame skipping.
rubrics_visual.d3: Lighting and depth of field are naturally controlled; volumetric lighting and subsurface scattering are harmoniously integrated; specular highlights and shadow layers are distinctly defined; spatial depth perception is clearly conveyed.
rubrics_visual.d4: The 3D modeling and material representation are realistic, with microstructure topology properly organized without interpenetration or mesh defects; translucent and anisotropic material properties are rendered convincingly.
rubrics_visual.d5: Color representation and information encoding are clear, with consistent hue and contrast, avoiding overexposure and color cast; color usage effectively highlights key information and processes.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Full implementation of the core theme and scene setting: Present the rapid conduction of electrical signals within myocardial cells along the fiber network from an ultra-high-definition microscopic perspective, focusing on catheter ablation precisely targeting abnormal pathways and instantaneously altering conduction flow as the main storyline.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Key elements and details are thoroughly presented: highlighting the myocardial cell structure and fibrous texture, the conduction pathways, and the dynamic changes at ablation sites, reflecting the role of electrical activity in regulating heart rate and rhythm.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Clear dynamic process and causal comparison: presenting time-series changes in electrical signal propagation patterns, rhythm recovery, and tissue responses before and after ablation, establishing a definite correspondence between processes and outcomes.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Compliance with audiovisual and technical specifications: ultra-high-definition image quality with microscopic/cellular-level perspectives, stable shots, and clear optical flow visual encoding; duration and pacing aligned with information density and viewer comprehension requirements.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Educational and Medical Consistency Compliance: The content and visualization align with the principles of cardiac conduction and catheter ablation, featuring clear narration and standardized terminology and labeling, fulfilling the requirements for patient education and science popularization.

任务: Under a 3D microscopic perspective, finely separate dense fibrous adhesions on the surface of the small intestine using laparoscopic instruments, highlighting the contrast between the tough texture of the fibrous bands and the smooth undulating surface of the intestine. Showcase the dynamic moment of adhesion separation in a microscopic medical scenario, with high-definition detail focusing on medical intervention intricacies, immersive microscopic particle dynamics, and realistic lighting effects.
具体要求: Create a stunning 3D microscopic world video that intricately captures intra-abdominal adhesions, emphasizing the interaction between fibrous bands and the small intestine. Present an immersive scene where these adhesions develop complexly, leading to potential complications such as obstruction, twisting, and segmental displacement of the small intestine. Highlight the precise and delicate maneuvers of laparoscopic instruments carefully dissecting these adhesions, demonstrating meticulous yet impactful interventions. Integrate realistic high-resolution details to depict the fibrous nature of the adhesions and the smooth, undulating surface of the intestinal tissue. Emphasize the stark contrast between the dense, resilient adhesions and the flexible, dynamic small intestine, creating a vivid visual experience that underscores the complexity of these medical scenarios.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Accurate anatomical relationships and spatial positioning: The attachments and orientations of the small intestine serosa with the mesentery, peritoneal surfaces, and fibrous adhesions conform to anatomical standards; the relative positions of instruments and tissues are appropriate and do not breach the intestinal lumen.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Authenticity of tissue texture and mechanical properties: Adhesion fibrous bands exhibit dense tensile strength and limited extensibility; the small intestine surface is smooth, moist, flexible, and resilient; tissue deformation and recovery during separation are consistent with biomechanical behavior.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Tissue-instrument interaction and immediate physiological responses are appropriate: coordinated traction and countertraction, rupture patterns consistent with blunt/sharp separation, degree and distribution of bleeding, serosal minor injury accompanied by exudation/microhemorrhage, and thermal effects and smoke levels associated with energy device usage conform to physiological limits.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Scale and time proportionality consistency: adhesion thickness corresponds with fiber bundle diameter, intestinal wall layer thickness, instrument tip size corresponds with operational speed, and tissue motion timescale matches droplet/particle movement within realistic microscopic ranges.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Accurate histogenetic and histological characterization of adhesions: collagen fiber orientation and cross-linking, degree of vascularization and neural distribution, continuity of bridging between opposing surfaces, and color and texture related to inflammation/fibrosis reflecting pathological understanding.

rubrics_visual.d1: High image clarity and superior microscopic detail resolution, with sharp and stable textures, microstructures, and edge contours.
rubrics_visual.d2: The distinction between material properties and texture is authentic, with a clear contrast between the rugged fibrous structure and the smooth undulating surface of the flexible tissue. Roughness, specular highlights, and subsurface scattering are consistent and credible.
rubrics_visual.d3: The interaction animation between instruments and tissue is coherent and physically plausible, with natural timing of contact, force application, deformation, and separation. Details of tearing, sliding, and rebound are continuous, without interleaving or unnecessary jitter. The response of particles and fluids is coordinated.
rubrics_visual.d4: Lighting and depth of field are realistically controlled to focus on key operations; local illumination, shadow transitions, specular highlights, and volumetric scattering are harmonized; depth of field guides attention without excessive blurring.
rubrics_visual.d5: Color and exposure are stable and natural, white balance is accurate, contrast and saturation are moderate, and the image is clean without noticeable noise, artifacts, flickering, or compression blocks.

rubrics_instruction.d11: Fully implement 3D microscopic/laparoscopic medical scenarios and task objectives, demonstrating the interaction between fibrous adhesions and the small intestine, and completing the precise separation process.
rubrics_instruction.d22: All key elements are complete and relationships are clearly defined: the dense and resilient fibrous band contrasts sharply with the smooth undulating surface of the small intestine and interacts closely with the precise movements of the laparoscopic instruments.
rubrics_instruction.d33: Reflect the dynamic process and causal context of adhesion separation, including visual cues of adhesion development and potential concomitant effects (obstruction, distortion, displacement).
rubrics_instruction.d44: Compliance with technical and performance standards: high-definition detail, realistic lighting and shadows, immersive micro-particle dynamics; camera focus on close-range medical intervention, with duration and pacing in accordance with task specifications.
rubrics_instruction.d55: Avoidance of critical bias: Utilizing macroscopic or non-laparoscopic perspectives, omitting key subjects, or introducing prominent elements and narratives irrelevant to the medical context.

任务: From a microscopic perspective, depict the binding of nicotine and carbon monoxide molecules to the surface of red blood cells, meticulously showcasing the complex surface architecture of red blood cells, the molecular details of toxic molecules, and lipid deposition on the damaged arterial intima. Illustrate the slow blood flow, initial platelet aggregation near the plaques, and clearly visible arterial wall degeneration, presented with scientific accuracy and artistic expression.
具体要求: Create a visually captivating and scientifically detailed microscopic world video demonstrating the interactions of cigarette smoke chemicals within the human circulatory system. The central theme focuses on the processes by which toxic chemicals such as nicotine, carbon monoxide, and radioactive particles mix with red blood cells and interact with the arterial intima. The animation should precisely depict how these harmful substances infiltrate the bloodstream, highlighting plaque formation at sites of atherosclerotic damage and platelet aggregation leading to potential thrombosis. Emphasize the molecular structures and compositions of the toxic chemicals, vividly and realistically illustrating the integration of nicotine and toxic gases within the biological environment. The complex surface details of red blood cells, the hemodynamics of blood flow, and the progressive narrowing of arteries should be prominently featured. The focus lies on arterial wall degeneration, lipid deposition formation, and the consequent cardiovascular disease risks. This overall visual experience should combine scientific accuracy with artistic creativity to profoundly portray the health risks associated with smoking.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Accurate representation of multi-scale structures and morphologies: clear depiction of the biconcave discoid shape of red blood cells, the disc-shaped form of platelets, the layered structure of the arterial wall (endothelium/intima/media), and the localization of plaques within the intima; relative size differences among molecules, cells, and tissues are preserved across orders of magnitude, allowing for appropriate visualization magnification without inverting the hierarchical scale levels.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Toxic molecular chemical characteristics and primary binding/distribution sites are accurate: the molecular conformation and fundamental physicochemical properties (lipophilicity/charge/solubility) are presented logically; interactions conform to established affinities and localization patterns (e.g., carbon monoxide exhibits high-affinity binding to hemoglobin; nicotine is predominantly carried in plasma and distributed within lipid membranes rather than nonspecifically adsorbed on erythrocyte surfaces; the distribution and endothelial interactions of radioactive or ultrafine particles in the bloodstream are consistent with evidential data).
rubrics_scientific.d3: Hemodynamics and microdynamics conform to physiological principles: the main arterial trunk predominantly exhibits laminar flow, with increased velocity at the stenotic segment and possible flow separation or recirculation downstream; molecular diffusion and Brownian motion are present; red blood cell deformability and the margination effect cause platelets to migrate toward and accumulate near the vessel wall.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Correct mechanism of atherosclerosis and thrombosis: endothelial dysfunction → lipoprotein infiltration and oxidation → monocyte recruitment → macrophage phagocytosis forming foam cells → formation of lipid core and fibrous cap; initial platelet adhesion/aggregation depends on exposed vWF/collagen and high shear stress environment; plaque formation is a chronic cumulative process rather than an instantaneous event.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Penalty for Major Scientific Errors: Depicting non-specific interactions as high-affinity specific binding, reversing critical causal chains, or severely violating conservation laws and scales (e.g., representing molecules as approaching cellular dimensions, depicting intact endothelium being instantaneously penetrated by molecules, or incorrectly illustrating toxic gases as stably adsorbed on the exterior membrane of red blood cells).

rubrics_visual.d1: High image clarity with well-resolved microscopic details; clear texture at the cell/molecular/tissue interfaces; consistent scale and proportion.
rubrics_visual.d2: The animation motion is coherent and smooth, with temporality and trajectories of fluid and particles (diffusion, aggregation, adhesion) being scientifically plausible, exhibiting no jittering or stuttering.
rubrics_visual.d3: Light and shadow effects, volumetric/subsurface scattering, and depth of field are naturally integrated; color and contrast emphasize the subject without distortion.
rubrics_visual.d4: The 3D modeling and material rendering are realistic, featuring high geometric accuracy and clear differentiation of surface roughness as well as transparency, translucency, and reflectivity characteristics.
rubrics_visual.d5: Visible artifacts such as noise, aliasing, compression artifacts, texture sticking, flickering, and excessive motion blur are prominent.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Comprehensively present the core causal pathway and central theme from the entry of harmful chemicals into the bloodstream to their impact on vascular lesions.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Accurately reflects the key elements and structures specified in the task and their interactions: molecular characteristics of key molecules (such as nicotine, carbon monoxide, radioactive particles), intricate surface details of red blood cells, arterial wall degeneration and lipid deposition, initial aggregation of platelets near plaques, and slow blood flow.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Adherence to microscopic perspective and shot composition requirements: clear expression at the microscopic scale, appropriate use of close-ups on key regions alongside the overall context, adequate depiction of temporal progression illustrating process evolution and gradual arterial constriction, with stable shots and good visual readability.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Integration of Scientific Accuracy and Artistic Expression: Structural proportions, procedural sequences, and interaction mechanisms conform to scientific consensus; molecular structures and component identifications are clearly defined; artistic interpretation does not compromise scientific rigor.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Penalty for Defects Conflicting with Instructions: omission of core elements or critical steps, misleading scenario design, incorrect representation of fluid dynamics or biological interactions, failure to meet specified duration or other technical requirements.

任务: From a microscopic perspective, focus on the cervical spinal canal stenosis region of the human body, dynamically displaying the spinal cord compression and the surrounding microstructures, highlighting the delicate textures and vivid colors of the vertebrae, spinal cord, and lamina caused by age-related wear. In the scene, medical surgical instruments approach the compressed area with surreal clarity, demonstrating subtle differences in tissue wear and the precise interaction with the instruments, suitable for a 5-second medical microscopic world simulation.
具体要求: Create a stunning microscopic world video showcasing detailed human cervical spine anatomy, illustrating both its normal structure and the complex changes resulting from age-related degeneration. This animation should immediately capture the viewer's attention by presenting spinal canal stenosis, followed by an immersive posterior cervical laminectomy and fusion surgical procedure aimed at relieving spinal cord pressure and stabilizing the spine. The scene should transition from static anatomical visualization to dynamic surgical interaction, emphasizing the precision and meticulous care involved in the surgery, enhanced by vivid colors and textures to increase the realism of biological structures and surgical instruments. Emphasize the details of the spinal cord, vertebrae, and lamina, rendering each texture with surreal clarity to allow viewers to perceive subtle differences in tissue wear and the seamless integration of medical instruments during the procedure. The video should be rich in medical educational content, suitable for hospitals to use high-quality animation for patient education and content marketing, effectively conveying the scientific and procedural aspects of cervical surgery. Highlight the tags #CervicalLaminectomy #NeckSurgery #PosteriorCervicalFusion to maximize audience engagement and awareness.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Accurate depiction of key cervical spine anatomical structures and spatial relationships, with the lamina, vertebral body, pedicle, intervertebral disc, vertebral canal, dural sac, spinal cord, and nerve roots correctly layered and positioned in accordance with biological and medical imaging evidence.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Scale and proportion are scientifically accurate; the thickness of the tissue organization, texture granularity, and instrument dimensions correspond to true microscopic scales; the degree of spinal canal stenosis and the ratio of spinal cord compression are reasonable.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Accurate expression of age-related degenerative changes and stenotic phenotypes, with features such as osteophyte formation, ligamentum flavum hypertrophy, intervertebral disc dehydration, and height reduction consistent with the corresponding anatomical locations and tissue properties.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Instrument-tissue interactions conform to biomechanical and materials science principles, with contact mechanics during cutting/traction/drilling consistent with the scientific magnitude and distribution of tissue deformation, incision edge morphology, thermal effects, and hemorrhagic responses.
rubrics_scientific.d5: The dynamic process and temporal sequence conform to physiological and procedural causality. The order, scope, and outcomes—from static anatomy to decompression and fusion steps—are consistent with the physiological objectives of spinal cord decompression and spinal stability.

rubrics_visual.d1: Surreal clarity with finely rendered microscopic textures; key structural edges are sharp, and tissue abrasion and surface microtextures are distinguishable without excessive sharpening artifacts.
rubrics_visual.d2: The 3D modeling and material realism are high, with accurate geometric proportions and clearly differentiated layers of soft and hard tissues; the soft tissues exhibit subsurface scattering and translucency characteristics, while metallic instruments demonstrate consistent reflection and roughness.
rubrics_visual.d3: Animation and interactions are smooth and coherent, with seamless camera movements and scene transitions; contact, sliding, and deformation feedback between instruments and tissues are precise, without penetration artifacts or jitter.
rubrics_visual.d4: The integration of lighting, depth of field, and focal control is natural and harmonious; the direction and intensity of macro lighting are appropriate; shadow gradations are well-defined; and the depth of field emphasizes the subject without obscuring key information.
rubrics_visual.d5: Color and contrast possess medical readability; white balance is accurate; saturation is appropriate; structural layering and key regions are effectively distinguished through color and luminance.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Focusing microscopically on the critical context of cervical spinal canal stenosis and spinal cord compression, this evaluation meticulously presents age-related degenerative textures and vivid coloration of the spinal cord, vertebrae, and laminae.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Smooth transition from static anatomical visualization to the dynamic surgical procedure of posterior cervical laminectomy and fusion, emphasizing precise instrumentation handling and the decompression objective of the compressed region.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Completely includes and clearly identifies the key anatomical structures and surrounding microstructures, demonstrating the precise interaction and seamless integration between surgical instruments and tissues.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Compliance with technical specifications and presentation requirements: approximately 5 seconds in duration, continuous microscopic perspective, hyper-realistic clarity, stable focus, and appropriate camera sequencing.

任务: High-definition 3D animation focusing on a single layer of human skin, showcasing the lamellar texture of the stratum corneum and the fibrous collagen network of the papillary dermis. From a microscopic perspective, simulate the immediate dynamic response of cellular and tissue structures during a slight puncture by a biopsy needle, scientifically accurate and visually focused, suitable for a 5-second microscopic world video.
具体要求: Create an engaging microscopic world video that meticulously captures the human skin layer as the primary subject, revealing the subtle interactions between cells and tissues under magnification. The scene should detail three different skin biopsy techniques, demonstrating how each method precisely extracts tissue samples for pathological examination and diagnosis. The video must emphasize highly detailed textures, such as the lamellar surface of the stratum corneum, the fibrous collagen network of the papillary dermis, and the dynamic movement of cellular structures responding during the biopsy process, all presented in high-definition 3D animation to bring the hidden world of skin anatomy to life.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The laminar texture of the stratum corneum, the undulations at the epidermal-dermal junction, and the spatial orientation and density distribution of the dermal papillary collagen fiber network are consistent with skin anatomy, with accurate hierarchical positional relationships.
rubrics_scientific.d2: The proportional relationships among cell size, intercellular spaces, collagen fiber diameter and bundle orientation, as well as needle dimensions and puncture depth, are appropriate, and the time scale and tissue deformation rate fall within biologically relevant magnitudes.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Accurate correspondence among the sampling depth of the three skin biopsy methods, instrument–tissue interaction, and the geometric morphology of the specimen, with the extraction trajectory consistent with the target layer.
rubrics_scientific.d4: The instantaneous dynamic responses of cells and extracellular matrix during puncture and cutting processes conform to the principles of tissue viscoelasticity and microcirculatory physiology, with the timing of deformation amplitude, recoil, and exudative diffusion appropriately coordinated.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Confusion of anatomical hierarchies, mismatch between sample geometry and operational trajectories, and scale incoherence or contradictions with physical and physiological laws.

rubrics_visual.d1: At the microscopic level, the image exhibits high clarity and detail fidelity, with the main subject’s edges naturally sharp. rubrics_visual.d2: The animation timing and dynamics are coherent, with natural and continuous deformation, compression, and rebound occurring in the interaction between tissues and instruments. Cellular and fiber micromotions are smooth without stuttering, and transitions between keyframes and simulations are seamless without abrupt changes.
