rubrics_visual.d5: Prominent visual artifacts (aliasing, flickering, moiré patterns, noise, banding, compression blocks, shadow light leakage/halos, texture stretching or UV distortion)

rubrics_instruction.d1: Fully implement the core scenarios with a primary focus on the main objects, presenting the skin at a microscopic level as the main feature, all unified within a high-definition 3D animation style.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Accurately represents key elements and dynamic processes: stratum corneum lamellar texture, papillary dermal collagen fiber network, and the immediate cellular and tissue responses to biopsy puncture; scientifically rigorous.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Clearly demonstrate three skin biopsy techniques, differentiate the methods and sampling procedures, and present the precise steps and outcomes of tissue specimen extraction.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Compliance with technical specifications such as duration and perspective: approximately 5 seconds, microscopic or magnifying glass view, stable image focus, and coordination between pacing and information density.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Scene Setting and Environmental Consistency: The skin anatomical background is realistic and credible, with lighting, materials, and texture rendering supporting both educational and professional presentations.

任务: Dynamically display the microscopic structure of damaged alveoli in chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) under high-resolution microscopy, highlighting the detailed textures of alveolar wall destruction, airway narrowing, fibrotic tissue, and mucus accumulation. Focus on the authentic dynamic changes of a single diseased alveolus in a medical microscopic world style, with a duration of 5 seconds.
具体要求: Create an engaging microscopic world video focusing on the complex cellular architecture of the human lung, with particular emphasis on depicting healthy and damaged tissues caused by chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD). Present a dynamic scene that allows viewers to observe the stark contrast between normal healthy alveoli and those affected by emphysema and chronic bronchitis. In this animation, emphasize cellular and structural alterations, showcasing inflamed and narrowed airways as well as progressive destruction of alveolar sacs over time. Ensure meticulous attention to detail and texture, highlighting delicate fibrotic tissue, the intricate branching of bronchial tubes, and mucus accumulation, using high-resolution visualization techniques to authentically represent the microscopic environment. The video should feature seamless transitions illustrating various stages of COPD progression and integrate sequences demonstrating potential therapeutic interventions such as drug diffusion and surgical repair, providing an educational and visually impactful narrative explaining this critical respiratory disease.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Accurate anatomical hierarchy and spatial relationships of terminal airways and alveoli; the branching topology, orientation of openings, and relative positions from bronchi/bronchioles to alveolar ducts and alveolar sacs are appropriate; narrowing is localized in small airways and produces ventilation distribution effects consistent with anatomical principles.
rubrics_scientific.d2: The morphology and size proportions of alveoli and alveolar septa conform to human histology; the comparison of volume, wall thickness, septal integrity, and capillary density between healthy and emphysematous alveoli is accurate.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Accurate representation of the pathophysiological processes of COPD: the temporal sequence and causal relationships among chronic inflammation, mucus hypersecretion with narrowing of small airways, decreased elastic recoil, and alveolar wall destruction are coherent; dynamic changes adhere to the principles of tissue mechanics and gas/liquid flow dynamics; during demonstration of therapeutic interventions, the direction and magnitude of structural changes induced by drug diffusion and receptor interaction or mechanical support are consistent with established mechanisms.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Scientific representation of organizational components and microtexture: the distribution of fibrotic bundles, elastic fibers, and collagen orientation, as well as the localization of interstitial thickening, is appropriate; the viscoelasticity of mucus and its morphology within the airway lumen, interfacial tension, and adhesion/slippage behaviors are consistent with rheological properties.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Avoidance of critical scientific errors: do not depict ciliated epithelium or free-floating red blood cells within the alveolar lumen; do not represent mucus extensively filling the alveolar lumen; do not confuse blood flow or bubble movement within small blood vessels with gas exchange processes.

rubrics_visual.d1: High image clarity and microscopic detail resolution, with clean edges and well-defined volumetric structures, without excessive sharpening or smearing.
rubrics_visual.d2: The 3D modeling and material textures are realistic, with consistent tissue morphology and scale; microscopic features such as translucency, moisture, and fibrous texture are convincingly represented.
rubrics_visual.d3: The animation is smooth with consistent timing; deformations, particle dynamics, and fluid motions are natural and continuous, and phase transitions occur seamlessly without stuttering or flickering.
rubrics_visual.d4: The integration of lighting, volumetric scattering, and depth of field is natural and coherent; the light source direction corresponds accurately with the shadows; volumetric rendering is stable without noise or color banding.
rubrics_visual.d5: Color and contrast relationships are accurate and consistent; saturation and white balance appear natural; key structural layers are clear and provide effective visual guidance.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Complete implementation of core scenarios and comparative requirements: using the specified microscopic subject as the narrative mainline, presenting a clear contrast between healthy and pathological tissues, with continuous focus on the real dynamic changes of a single focal lesion.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Coverage of Key Pathological and Structural Elements: Emphasize the pathological and histological features specified in the task requirements (such as tissue destruction, luminal stenosis, fibrosis, and mucus accumulation), and demonstrate the relevant cellular components and branching structures.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Reflecting dynamic evolution and stage progression: Demonstrates the trajectory and detailed processes of lesion changes from early to late stages, with coherent scene transitions and seamless continuity, forming a clear temporal advancement.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Compliance with Technical Specifications and Microscopic Visual Style: Adherence to the specified duration and viewpoint/composition requirements, maintaining high-resolution microscopic style consistent with the micro-scale, with stable imagery and discernible fine details.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Integration of Intervention Sequences with Educational Presentation: Therapeutic or intervention visualizations (e.g., drug diffusion, reparative methods) are integrated as required, with clear and scientifically accessible narration.

任务: Under the microscope, pancreatic cancer cells exhibit abnormal mitosis within the fibrotic stroma, with cell membranes displaying complex textures. The stroma and cancer cells dynamically interact, and the color variations in the pericellular fluid highlight the pathological features of the pancreatic cancer microenvironment.
具体要求: Create a visually stunning microscopic world video showcasing the complex cellular environment of the pancreas, with a focus on the development and progression of pancreatic cancer. The scene begins with a detailed examination of the normal pancreatic anatomy, emphasizing the structure and function of exocrine cells and the endocrine islets of Langerhans, which are rich in vascular networks and hormone secretion. As the video progresses, it gradually shifts focus to pathophysiological changes, revealing the formation of exocrine adenocarcinoma tumors within the fibrotic stroma. Witness the proliferation of cancer cells, illustrating cellular mutations and abnormal mitosis leading to adenocarcinoma clusters. Key details should include the dynamic interactions between cellular and stromal components, the complex texture of tumor cell membranes, and color changes in cellular fluid dynamics. Enrich the visual narrative by incorporating microscopic views of surgical interventions—Whipple procedure, total pancreatectomy, and distal pancreatectomy—highlighting the cellular and tissue responses to these treatments. Conclude with depictions of targeted effects at the microscopic level from external beam radiotherapy, brachytherapy, and chemotherapy, focusing on treatment-induced cellular damage and apoptosis pathways. This video aims to provide an insightful and educational journey exploring the microscopic biology and treatment of pancreatic cancer.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Accurate representation of the structural and scale relationships between normal and pathological pancreatic tissues: the relative size, density, and spatial positioning of exocrine acini, ducts, islets of Langerhans, blood vessels, and fibrotic stroma conform to biological realities.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Tumorigenesis and cellular dynamics exhibit pathophysiological consistency: the accumulation of mutations, abnormal mitosis, clonal expansion, and invasive progression occur in a coherent temporal sequence and morphological pattern.
rubrics_scientific.d3: The dynamic interactions among cells, stroma, and the microenvironment are scientifically sound: Accurate representation of cell adhesion, matrix remodeling and mechanics, nutrient and oxygen gradients, diffusion of signaling molecules, as well as the feedback relationships involving vascular and immune components.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Microscale physical and chemical processes conform to governing laws: the texture and resolution of cell membranes and subcellular structures are matched; fluid and molecular movements follow laminar flow, Brownian motion, and diffusion principles; color mapping corresponds to authentic physiological variables without causing misinterpretation.
rubrics_scientific.d5: The micro-level effects and mechanisms of diagnostic and therapeutic interventions are accurately corresponded: the surgical-induced tissue resection margins, ischemia, and repair responses; radiotherapy-induced DNA damage and activation of apoptotic pathways; selective manifestations of chemotherapy on the cell cycle and targeted effects.

rubrics_visual.d1: High image clarity with strong discernibility of microstructural details; complex texture of the cell membrane, clear visualization of the nucleus and nucleolus, well-defined fibrotic stromal fiber bundles, and distinct microvascular network layers.
rubrics_visual.d2: Excellent coherence between animation and dynamics; abnormal mitotic phases transition smoothly; cell–mesenchyme interactions exhibit causal consistency; fluid color diffusion and deformation rhythms are well-coordinated; camera movement remains stable.
rubrics_visual.d3: Color and contrast are controlled scientifically and naturally; tissue layering and pathological features are distinctly differentiated through hue, brightness, and saturation. The overall color scheme is consistent and avoids oversaturation that could impair recognition.
rubrics_visual.d4: Realistic 3D modeling and material/volume rendering; reasonable cell membrane roughness and subsurface scattering; credible anisotropic fibers and volumetric scattering in the stroma; clearly defined hierarchical structure of blood vessels and islet formations with consistent scale.
rubrics_visual.d5: Visual artifacts and stability issues are prominent, including compression blocking, flickering, color banding, moiré patterns, aliasing, inter-frame drift, or texture swimming, all of which reduce immersion.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Complete presentation of the narrative sequence and transitional stages from normal microscopic architecture to pathological progression
rubrics_instruction.d2: Coverage of key specified micro-pathological elements and interactions in the task (e.g., tumor-stroma dynamics, abnormal mitosis, complex cell membrane textures, microenvironmental fluid and color variations)
rubrics_instruction.d3: Accurately demonstrate specified surgical and chemo-radiotherapeutic interventions and their cellular-level effects (targeting, destruction, and apoptosis pathways) at the microscopic scale.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Perspective and Technical Compliance (Consistent microscope perspective and scale; duration and pacing meet requirements; clear and distinguishable imagery; camera angles and element composition effectively support narration and comprehension)
rubrics_instruction.d5: Deviation and omission penalties (including the presence of irrelevant macroscopic content, neglect of core stages or key elements, and incorrect depiction of critical mechanisms resulting in inconsistencies between scientific accuracy and narrative coherence)

任务: Human bronchioles and alveoli from a microscopic perspective, demonstrating the effects of asthma triggers (such as allergens) including bronchiolar smooth muscle contraction, airway narrowing, and mucus accumulation. The alveoli and capillary network are clearly visible, dynamically illustrating impaired gas exchange. The scene ends with an instantaneous display of airway dilation and symptom relief under the action of a bronchodilator. High-definition detail, scientifically accurate, suitable for a 5-second microscopic world video.
具体要求: Create a captivating microscopic world video showcasing the complex and vital structures of the human respiratory system, focusing on the bronchioles and alveoli. Capture the dynamic process of air inhalation, illustrating how oxygen smoothly transitions from the alveoli into the surrounding capillaries while carbon dioxide moves in the opposite direction, symbolizing the respiratory cycle. Emphasize bronchiolar dilation under ideal conditions and vividly depict their contraction in response to asthma triggers such as allergens and cold air. Provide close-up views of inflamed bronchi, demonstrating excessive mucus production and smooth muscle contraction during asthma attacks, highlighting the difficulty of airflow. Highlight the textures of delicate, membranous alveolar sacs and the intricate capillary network, showcasing gas interaction and exchange with remarkable clarity and precision. Include a scene illustrating the effect of bronchodilator medication, where constricted airways visibly relax and expand, emphasizing symptom relief. Detailed textures of smooth muscle contraction, inflamed epithelial cells, and mucus accumulation should be meticulously rendered to create an engaging educational tool. Incorporate rich keywords such as asthma, respiration, airway, inflammation, and bronchodilation to attract audiences interested in health education, patient awareness, and medical professionals.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The morphology and spatial relationships of the terminal bronchioles, alveoli, alveolar septa, and capillary network are accurate, and the distribution of smooth muscle encircling and the epithelial layering conform to anatomical standards.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Physiological processes are accurately presented: asthma triggers induce smooth muscle contraction leading to airway narrowing; increased mucus production and inflammatory responses result in ventilation limitation; gas exchange across the alveolar-capillary membrane follows partial pressure gradients; bronchial dilators cause smooth muscle relaxation consistent with improved ventilation.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Scale and proportion appropriateness: The lumen diameter of terminal bronchioles, alveolar size, and capillary diameter are proportionally consistent with the scale of erythrocytes; the degree of contraction and dilation remains within physiological limits; alveolar wall thickness and permeability characteristics are appropriately manifested.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Dynamic and Physical Consistency: Airflow and mucus movement conform to fluid mechanics and viscoelastic properties; gas diffusion pathways and rates correspond with tissue structure; changes in ventilation-perfusion relationships coherently impact exchange efficiency.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Scientific Error Penalty: The occurrence of fictitious structures or hierarchical confusion (e.g., cilia within alveolar spaces, extensive smooth muscle or blood cells crossing intact barriers), or exaggerated effects that violate fundamental physical or physiological principles.

rubrics_visual.d1: The image exhibits high clarity with finely detailed microstructures; tissue textures and edges are well-defined, and the layered information is rich, neither blurred nor contaminated.
rubrics_visual.d2: Animation coherence and believable kinetic performance; contraction and relaxation rhythms are natural; fluid and particle motions are smooth, without frame skipping or motion blur; clear transitions in drug effects.
rubrics_visual.d3: Anatomical modeling and material texture are realistic, with structurally proportional and topologically coherent relationships; microscopic material properties such as membranous translucency, viscosity, and the glossiness of blood are accurately depicted.
rubrics_visual.d4: The rendering of light, shadow, and volumetric effects is accurate; subsurface scattering and volumetric scattering appear natural; shadows are soft and directionally consistent; depth of field effectively emphasizes micrometer-scale details.
rubrics_visual.d5: Color and contrast are well-controlled, with balanced saturation and luminance distribution; key elements exhibit clear and distinguishable colors without harshness, and the overall color tone is uniform and free of color casts.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Complete presentation of the core narrative chain in the microscopic respiratory scene: bronchioles and alveoli as the main subjects → triggers inducing smooth muscle contraction/airway narrowing/mucus accumulation → impaired gas exchange → conclusion with an instantaneous depiction of bronchodilator relief.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Key structures and elements are accurately visible and scientifically validated: bronchiolar smooth muscle, inflamed epithelium, excessive mucus, alveolar sacs with capillary networks, and signals indicating the direction and obstruction of oxygen/carbon dioxide exchange.
rubrics_instruction.d3: The dynamic process is clear, coherent, and encompasses key points of the respiratory cycle: inhalation → diffusion → exhalation, with a distinct comparison between unobstructed and obstructed phases. The rhythm and transitions are suitably adapted to a 5-second short video format.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Perspective and image technical specifications compliance: consistent microscopic/microstructural perspective, high-definition detail and clear texture, stable composition with lighting and color that support comprehension, while avoiding irrelevant shots and noise.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Major Deduction Criteria: Presence of anatomical or physiological errors, omission of concluding segments addressing drug action and symptom relief, significant deviation in duration, departure from microscopic perspective, or absence of the core subject.

任务: Under a 3D endoscopic perspective, Helicobacter pylori exhibits helical motility within the gastric mucosal inner layer. The bacterial surface texture is clearly defined, with endoscopic lighting illuminating both the bacteria and the surrounding flowing gastric fluid. The organic texture of the gastric mucosa and its microscopic environment are meticulously depicted, capturing the dynamic interactions between the bacteria and the gastric fluid.
具体要求: Produce a stunning microscopic world video focusing on Helicobacter pylori within the gastric mucosal inner layer. The visuals portray a detailed 3D endoscopic examination, presenting a dynamic and educational journey through the upper gastrointestinal tract. Accurately capture the interplay between medical technology and the microscopic world to aid in the diagnosis and treatment of gastric ulcers. Pay close attention to the texture and details of Helicobacter pylori, showcasing its helical shape and motility, illuminated by the endoscopic light. The bacteria are enveloped within the authentic gastric fluid environment, with the organic texture of the gastric mucosa clearly visible, capturing the pulsations and rhythmic contractions of the gastric wall. Subsequent scenes emphasize the complex process of treating hemorrhagic gastric ulcers, detailing how endoscopic instruments cauterize bleeding sites. Highlight the transformation of ulcerated tissue, vividly and realistically portraying the precise application of medical intervention, providing a comprehensive appreciation of the meticulous care involved in endoscopic surgery.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The helical morphology and flagellar arrangement of Helicobacter pylori, as well as its helical propulsion mechanism within the mucus layer, are accurately depicted; the size ratio and spatial positional relationship between the bacterium and the gastric mucosal layers are appropriately represented.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Microscopic anatomy of the gastric mucosa is presented scientifically: the architecture of the epithelium and gastric pits, the thickness and continuity of the mucus layer, and the hierarchical and positional relationships of the basal layer and capillaries are accurately depicted.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Reasonable depiction of the fluidic and chemical microenvironment of gastric juice and mucus: viscoelasticity and locally shear-dominated flow characteristics, their effects on bacterial trajectories and diffusion, as well as pH gradient-driven directional behavior consistent with physiological principles.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Pathological features of hemorrhagic gastric ulcer and mechanisms of endoscopic hemostasis accurately described: depiction of bleeding source, tissue coagulation and degeneration induced by thermal/electrocoagulation, temporal sequence of blood flow reduction and corresponding tissue responses consistent with medical facts.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Deduction for Scientific Inaccuracy: Distortion across scales (e.g., bacteria enlarged to the scale of tissues), non-physiological movements or incorrect anatomical spatial relationships, and the presence of implausible super-resolution details or optical effects at the given imaging scale.

rubrics_visual.d1: High image clarity with well-defined microscopic textures and edge details; noise and blur are controlled, ensuring the key subjects remain discernible during motion.
rubrics_visual.d2: The light and color rendering align with the visual characteristics of endoscopic imaging; illumination direction and intensity are appropriate; volumetric lighting and specular highlights appear natural; white balance and contrast remain stable; depth of field transitions are smooth and natural.
rubrics_visual.d3: Animation is smooth, with coherent motion of microspheres and particles; the timing and scale of fluid pulsation, tissue pulsation, and interactions with microspheres are synchronized, without any jitter or stuttering.
rubrics_visual.d4: 3D modeling and material physical plausibility are accurate, with precise geometric forms; microscopic surface roughness and wetness are effectively conveyed; refraction and layering relationships of transparent and translucent media are clearly defined.
rubrics_visual.d5: The image exhibits obvious artifacts and post-processing defects, such as compression blocks, color banding, moiré patterns, excessive sharpening or over-applied motion blur, overexposure, or crushed blacks.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Using a 3D endoscope with a first-person perspective, maintaining stable focus on the designated target area and microscopic structures within the specified luminal tissue, thereby reflecting the spatial relationships and observational trajectory inherent to endoscopic examination.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Accurate reproduction of core microscopic dynamics and interactions: the characteristic motion patterns of the subject, light and shadow effects under endoscopic illumination, fluid interactions with surrounding bodily fluids and extracellular matrix, as well as tissue pulsations and rhythmic variations.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Coherent presentation of medical procedures and intervention key points: highlighting critical steps from examination to treatment (lesion localization, instrument manipulation and energy application, tissue state transitions and therapeutic feedback), emphasizing educational value and diagnostic and therapeutic objectives.
rubrics_instruction.d4: High fidelity in detail and material reproduction: the surface texture and morphology of microscopic subjects are clearly distinguishable; the organic textures of surrounding tissues and environments are credible; lighting and scale perception conform to endoscopic imaging characteristics.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Meets the specified production requirements such as task-designated duration, camera movement, and editing rhythm; exhibits smooth narrative flow and clear information presentation while avoiding the inclusion of irrelevant elements or deviations from the scene setting.

任务: Under the perspective of a microscopic endoscope, the delicate surface of the colonic mucosa is clearly visible, with the epithelial cell layer and vascular network distinctly displayed. The scene focuses on the precise moment a polyp is meticulously excised, highlighting the healthy microscopic structure of the colon and medical details.
具体要求: Create a visually stunning video that deeply explores the complex microscopic world of gastrointestinal health, with a focus on the dynamically moving endoscope during colonoscopy as the central subject navigating the vivid and intricate colonic environment. The scene should depict the endoscope skillfully maneuvering through the colon, capturing vivid images of the mucosal inner layer, showcasing healthy tissue and potential abnormalities such as polyps, while emphasizing the precision of performing biopsies to differentiate benign from malignant tissues. Highlight detailed colonic textures, including the vascular network and the layered surface of the epithelial tissue, with a particular emphasis on the clear visualization of a polyp being meticulously removed during polypectomy. The imagery should capture the transition process from normal to abnormal and back to a healthy colonic ecosystem.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The hierarchical structure, surface texture, and thickness ratio of the colonic mucosa and epithelial tissue are accurately represented, with the spatial scale and localization relationships between healthy and abnormal regions being appropriate.
rubrics_scientific.d2: The submucosal vascular network and microcirculatory dynamics conform to physiological principles, with capillary caliber and branching density, blood flow direction and velocity, as well as coupling of pulsation and peristalsis, all presenting in a consistent and appropriate manner.
rubrics_scientific.d3: The biopsy sampling and tissue processing procedures are performed with scientific accuracy; the sampling site and depth are appropriate, and the mechanical and hemorrhagic responses of the surrounding tissues are authentic. The criteria for distinguishing benign from malignant lesions are consistent with the observable imaging information.
rubrics_scientific.d4: The morphological characteristics of the polyp and the resection procedure are scientifically accurate; pedunculated or sessile features are clearly identified, and the time sequence of margin evaluation, mucosal contraction, and coagulation/hemostatic responses is appropriate.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Use of visual renderings that exceed the resolution of endoscopic imaging or are inconsistent with the physical optics and physiological scale (e.g., depicting cellular-level details as visible under white light, or exaggerating color contrast or size proportions), resulting in misleading representations.

rubrics_visual.d1: High image clarity and microscopic detail resolution; sharp and stable tissue texture and layer boundaries; noise and defocus effectively controlled.
rubrics_visual.d2: The representation of lighting and color aligns with microscopic optical properties; white balance and contrast are natural; specular highlights and subsurface scattering in moist tissues are authentic; depth of field and volumetric scattering are harmonized without overexposure.
rubrics_visual.d3: Good physical consistency between 3D modeling and materials, with reasonable microscopic surface undulations and hierarchical structures; accurate normal/displacement mapping and roughness details; credible representation of translucency and volumetric details.
rubrics_visual.d4: Camera movements and interactive animations are coherent and stable, with natural pacing in translation and rotation; frame rates remain consistent without stuttering, and motion blur and micro-vibrations are properly controlled.
rubrics_visual.d5: Minimization of Technical Flaws and Visual Artifacts: obvious defects such as aliasing, moiré patterns, compression blocking, banding, flickering lighting, reflective overexposure, object interpenetration, and shadow fragmentation.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Complete implementation of the core scenario: dynamic navigation under a microscopic endoscopic view within the colon, focusing on the precise moment of polypectomy and demonstrating accurate biopsy maneuvers.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Accurate depiction of critical medical elements: delicate mucosal texture, epithelial layer architecture and vascular network, and clear differentiation between healthy tissue and abnormalities (e.g., polyps)
rubrics_instruction.d3: Process and Narrative Coherence: Demonstrates the transition from normal condition → detection of abnormality → biopsy diagnosis → polypectomy → restoration of health, with clear sequencing and pacing of visual shots.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Perspective and Technical Specifications Compliance: consistent endoscopic close-up perspective, stable focus with discernible details, appropriate illumination; duration and pacing cover all critical phases.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Points deduction for content deviating from instructions: inclusion of external or incorrect organ scenes, departure from endoscopic perspective, omission of critical steps in biopsy or polypectomy, neglect of mucosal microscopic details.

任务: Under a high-definition microscopic perspective, the hepatitis virus (type A or B) with a spiked capsid dynamically attaches to and penetrates the membrane of a healthy hepatocyte. The delicate surface texture of the hepatocyte and the soft hepatic microenvironment serve as the background, highlighting the moment of interaction between the virus and the hepatocyte membrane. The video meticulously depicts the scientific details of membrane deformation and viral invasion, with scientifically accurate imagery suitable for a short 5-second presentation.
具体要求: Produce a stunning microscopic world video that vividly captures the invasion of the hepatitis virus and the subsequent hepatic response. The main focus is the dynamic interaction between hepatitis A or B virus and hepatocytes, emphasizing how the virus stealthily enters healthy liver cells, presented with high resolution and scientific accuracy. Within this captivating scene, demonstrate the self-replication of the hepatitis virus inside hepatocytes, triggering the body's immune response. Highlight hepatocytes involved in the inflammatory response and show immune cells aggressively attempting to eliminate the invading virus, initiating a cascade of viral replication and cellular inflammation. Emphasize the delicate texture of liver tissue, the virus's spiked capsid, and the rich, flexible structure of the liver prior to fibrosis. Capture hepatocyte swelling, rupture, and death leading to scar tissue formation and cirrhosis, with a clear degradation of liver structural integrity over time. Carefully depict microscopic battle scenes such as viral attachment to hepatocytes, blood filtration within the hepatic lobules, and complex cellular interactions to engage, educate, and captivate a scientifically inclined audience.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The virus type, morphology, size, and capsid characteristics are consistent with the selected hepatitis virus (HBV possesses an envelope and surface projections, while HAV is a non-enveloped small RNA virus), and the entire description remains internally coherent.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Accurate presentation of the mechanism of virus-hepatocyte membrane interaction: receptor-mediated adsorption followed by endocytosis or membrane fusion induces localized membrane deformation, without mechanical penetration-type invasion.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Viral replication and intracellular localization correspond to virus type: HBV undergoes nuclear phase and reverse transcription, whereas HAV performs RNA replication in the cytoplasm; assembly and release pathways are scientifically consistent.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Accuracy of micro-scale and structural relationships: coordination of the relative sizes and spatial positions among the virus, receptor, lipid bilayer thickness, hepatocyte microvilli, sinusoidal space, and erythrocytes.
rubrics_scientific.d5: The temporal sequence and mechanisms of immune and pathological processes are coherent: clearance mediated by Kupffer cells and NK/CTLs; hepatocyte swelling, apoptosis/necrosis, inflammation propagation, and fibrosis (hepatic stellate cell-mediated collagen deposition) are presented with temporal compression or clear transitional phases, without occurrence of instantaneous liver cirrhosis.

rubrics_visual.d1: High image clarity with sharply defined microstructural edges and distinguishable texture details, exhibiting no significant noise, compression artifacts, or excessive sharpening.
rubrics_visual.d2: The animation is smooth and stable, with continuous motion trajectories and deformation. Interactive transitions are natural, and within a short duration, the rhythm is clear and information density is balanced.
rubrics_visual.d3: The lighting and depth of field effects are consistent with the microscopic scale; volumetric lighting and subsurface scattering exhibit natural coherence without flickering, noise, or exposure drift.
rubrics_visual.d4: Accurate 3D modeling and material representation with clear scale hierarchy; micro-surface roughness, normal and displacement details, and reflection responses are credible.
rubrics_visual.d5: Color grading is natural and consistent, with moderate saturation and contrast; tonal separation and focal guidance are clear, balancing scientific clarity with aesthetic appeal.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Comprehensively demonstrate the main sequential processes from pathogen attachment and penetration to intracellular replication, immune response, and tissue alterations, emphasizing critical interaction events.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Ensure scientific accuracy and biological plausibility, with pathogen morphology, membrane deformation mechanisms, immune cell behavior, and tissue responses conforming to mainstream scientific consensus.
rubrics_instruction.d3: In accordance with high-definition microscopic perspectives and technical specifications, the image is clear and stable, with discernible details, and the texture and microenvironmental background support information comprehension.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Coverage of Key Elements and Scenario Settings: Clear presentation of critical structural features of the pathogen, target cell membrane deformation and invasion pathways, as well as the composition of relevant cells and microenvironment.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Complies with the specified short duration and rhythmic arrangement, delivering the main information within approximately 5 seconds, with concise shot segments and no overload of irrelevant information.

任务: Under the microscopic perspective, seamlessly connect a healthy vascular graft with an occluded coronary artery at the fine suture points, clearly revealing the delicate textures of the vascular fibrous layer and graft surface. Oxygenated blood flows through the newly established pathway, highlighting the dynamic moment of microstructural detail, color, and restoration of blood flow.
具体要求: Create a stunning and scientifically accurate microscopic world video focusing on the intricate details of the coronary artery bypass grafting (CABG) procedure. The primary theme should be the subtle interaction of skillfully grafting a healthy artery or vein onto an occluded coronary artery, demonstrating the meticulous work of bypassing the blockage to restore optimal myocardial perfusion. Capture the scene from a microscopic viewpoint, showcasing the surgeon’s precise suturing techniques and the seamless integration between the graft and native artery. Depict the complex dynamics of the cardiopulmonary bypass machine and the cardiopulmonary interactions when the heart function is temporarily supported by the bypass, enabling the cardiac surgeon to operate methodically under optimal conditions. Delve into the textures and colors of the vessels, emphasizing their fibrous layers, the graft’s glossy surface, and the vitality of oxygenated blood flowing through the newly formed conduit. Include visual effects of mechanical stabilizers used during off-pump surgery, highlighting their role in providing a stable environment for precise grafting. Illuminate the intricate textures of the postoperative cardiac surface, illustrating the orderly closure of the sternum and soft tissues, as well as the placement of drainage tubes critical for postoperative recovery. Detailed depictions of sutures, graft vessels, and the transition of blood flow should be vividly reproduced to provide an immersive educational experience for audiences interested in cardiovascular surgery and the marvels of medical science.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Accuracy of the anatomical and dimensional relationship between the coronary artery and the graft, including the vascular wall layers, diameter matching, and the geometric configuration and orientation of the anastomosis, evaluated at the microscopic scale.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Hemodynamic and oxygenation states conform to physiological principles, with flow direction and velocity distribution being appropriate; primarily laminar flow accompanied by localized disturbances; the coloration and pathways of oxygenated and deoxygenated blood are consistent.
rubrics_scientific.d3: The temporal sequence and causal relationships during the surgical procedure are scientifically coherent, with consistent coordination between the intervention devices and physiological states (e.g., initiation and cessation of extracorporeal circulation corresponding to changes in cardiac activity, local stabilizing devices reducing tissue displacement, and the sequential progression of blood shunting and reperfusion restoration).
rubrics_scientific.d4: Instrument-tissue interaction and material properties are realistically represented; suture needle mechanics correspond appropriately with tissue deformation recovery, bleeding, and hemostatic responses; surface texture and reflectivity closely mimic reality without obscuring structural boundaries.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Severe scientific errors will be penalized, including reversal of blood flow direction or pressure gradient, misalignment or luminal narrowing of anastomoses, disappearance or displacement of vascular wall layers, and instrument movements that violate the laws of physics.

rubrics_visual.d1: Image clarity and detailed performance are high, with discernible microstructures and clean edges.
rubrics_visual.d2: The animation exhibits smoothness and strong physical consistency, with continuous and stable interactions between the microfluidics and the tissue.
rubrics_visual.d3: Accurate color reproduction with balanced saturation and contrast, and well-preserved subtle tonal gradations.
rubrics_visual.d4: High fidelity in 3D modeling accuracy and material texture realism, with appropriately layered textures and scale-consistent mapping.
rubrics_visual.d5: Lighting effects and depth of field are naturally controlled, with accurate sense of scale and smooth transitions between highlights and shadows.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Complete implementation of the main task: Under microscopic view, precisely demonstrate the anastomosis between the healthy graft and the recipient vessel, with the core process of bypassing the obstruction and restoring blood flow clearly visible, emphasizing the critical moments of suturing and reperfusion.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Microscopic Perspective and Restoration of Microstructural Details: The entire presentation predominantly employs a microscopic viewpoint with stable imaging. Vascular stratification at the micro-scale, surface texture and coloration of the graft, and details of oxygenated blood flow are clearly depicted. The pacing and duration support the identification of instantaneous dynamics and structural details.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Comprehensive coverage of key elements and devices: Demonstrates surgical suturing actions seamlessly connected to the anastomotic site, reflecting the dynamic transition of blood flow from occlusion to patency, and includes visualization of intraoperative and postoperative critical elements relevant to the task (such as extracorporeal circulation or mechanical stabilizers, postoperative closure, and drainage).
rubrics_instruction.d4: Scientific Accuracy and Effective Educational Communication: Anatomical relationships, instrument functions, blood flow direction, and oxygenation characteristics conform to established medical facts; visual presentation and expression facilitate comprehension of surgical principles and procedures, maintaining rigor without excessive artistic exaggeration.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Major deviation deduction: Occurrence of perspectives and content contrary to the requirements (e.g., predominantly macro-level perspective), omission of core processes or key elements, presence of significant scientific errors, or a high proportion of content irrelevant to the topic, all adversely affecting task completion and scenario reconstruction.

任务: Under the microscope, surgical instruments precisely excise abnormal breast cancer cells within pink and white fibrous breast tissue, highlighting the color contrast between healthy and cancerous cells. The scene locally reveals a complex network of blood vessels and lymph nodes, delicately showcasing the minute adjustments of surgical tools and changes in cellular structures. The imagery is scientifically accurate, detailed, and vivid.
具体要求: Produce a stunning microscopic world video that immerses the audience in the complex field of oncology, focusing on the microscopic breast cancer cells within the tissue matrix as the central theme. The scenes transition through various surgical procedures, including lumpectomy, simple mastectomy, modified radical mastectomy, and radical mastectomy, demonstrating at the cellular level how these surgeries interact with and impact abnormal cellular structures. Carefully capture the movements and precision of surgical instruments as they navigate through pink and white fibrous breast tissue, meticulously removing cancerous cells and surrounding tissue. The video should exhibit extraordinary detail, emphasizing the color differentiation between healthy and infected cells, the subtle adjustments of surgical tools, and the intricate vascular and lymphatic networks involved in these transformative medical procedures. By combining rich textures and vivid colors with microscopic-level precision and accuracy, this video will serve as an educational masterpiece for medical professionals and patients alike, presenting a detailed and awe-inspiring perspective on microscopic surgical treatment of breast cancer.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Accurate representation of microscale dimensions and proportions: the size scales and relative ratios of cells, extracellular matrix fibers, microvasculature, and lymphatic vessels conform to biomeasurements, with dynamic behaviors such as motility, diffusion, and Brownian motion appropriately matched to imaging magnification.
rubrics_scientific.d2: The organizational hierarchy and spatial relationships are scientifically rational: the boundaries, continuity, and heterogeneity between epithelium and stroma, adipose and fibrous matrix, as well as the basement membrane and the tumor invasion front, correspond to pathological characteristics, and the tumor–normal tissue interface is presented credibly.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Accuracy of microvascular and lymphatic network structure and hemodynamics: vessel wall thickness and compliance, branching and anastomosis patterns, rheological and shear properties of erythrocyte and lymph flow, as well as physiologically consistent processes of hemorrhage/exudation and hemostasis/closure following injury.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Realistic surgical instrument–tissue interaction: tissue deformation, rupture, and thermal effects caused by cutting, traction, dissection, and energy application (e.g., electrocautery) correspond to biomechanical and heat conduction mechanisms in both spatial extent and temporal sequence, with microscopic cutting margins consistent with the surgical manipulation.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Visual encoding and causal narrative to avoid misrepresentation: color contrast and brightness enhancement follow the principles of microscopy imaging and pathological staining, without substituting biological differences with arbitrary hues; the correspondence between macroscopic surgical types and microscopic effects avoids mismatches and exaggeration.

rubrics_visual.d1: High image clarity and resolution of microscopic details, with clear hierarchical structures of cells and tissue textures, stable edges, and absence of noise and aliasing artifacts.
rubrics_visual.d2: Accurate representation of color differentiation and contrast, with clear and natural distinctions between healthy and abnormal structural colors; saturation and contrast are balanced to avoid color cast and color bleeding.
rubrics_visual.d3: Animation fluidity and precise rendering of micro-movements; coherent and stable motion of instruments, cellular deformation, and tissue response; proper control of frame rate and motion blur; smooth and seamless camera and scene transitions.
rubrics_visual.d4: High modeling and material realism, consistent geometric scale, clearly defined hierarchical structures of cells, stroma, blood vessels, and lymphatics, with credible representation of material properties such as translucency and subsurface scattering.
rubrics_visual.d5: Lighting and depth of field are managed naturally; volumetric scattering and shadow softness are appropriate; key areas maintain stable focus; light direction and intensity are consistent; overall tonal hierarchy and contrast are prominent without overexposure.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Focusing on the micromorphology and abnormal cells specified by the instructions as the central theme, the core scenarios and precise manipulations under the microscopic perspective are fully accomplished.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Encompasses multi-stage processes and scenario transitions as specified in the instructions, demonstrating at the cellular level the interactions and impacts of different processes on abnormal structures.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Key elements and contrast relationships clearly presented: organization of texture and color, contrast between healthy and pathological cells, associated vascular and lymphatic networks, instrument fine adjustments and microscopic structural changes
rubrics_instruction.d4: Technical Specifications and Scientific Accuracy Compliance: Microscale and perspective are correctly represented; the visuals are detailed and rigorous; duration and pacing align with the instructional and popular science requirements.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Avoidance of Instruction Conflicts and Content Bias: Presence of macro or irrelevant shots, missing or ambiguous key elements, inaccuracies in medical descriptions, and excessive stylization that undermines scientific integrity.

任务: Under a high-definition microscopic perspective, at the site of a transparent and complex atherosclerotic plaque within the coronary artery, a balloon catheter is slowly inflated, contrast agent flows, and a metallic mesh stent simultaneously expands and anchors to the arterial wall. Blood flow is restored, with clear details of the arterial wall and plaque structure. The environment is brightly illuminated, highlighting the critical moments of the microscopic interventional procedure.
具体要求: Produce a stunning and scientifically accurate microscopic world video that deeply explores the complex process of coronary angioplasty, showcasing the core subject—the atherosclerotic plaque within the coronary artery. The video should capture the scene of the guidewire skillfully navigating through the artery, followed by the insertion of a small and flexible catheter, demonstrating the precise maneuvers as the catheter advances toward the plaque accumulation. Emphasize the detailed texture of the arterial wall, depicting its complex layers, while illuminating the transparent and intricate structure of the plaque in high definition. Highlight the inflation of the balloon at the catheter tip effectively compressing the plaque and successfully restoring blood flow. Visualize the flow of a specialized contrast agent within the artery to vividly enhance the fluoroscopic monitoring of any occlusion. As the procedure progresses, show the deployment of the metallic mesh stent surrounding the balloon. Capture the moment of inflation, emphasizing the precise expansion of the stent and its anchoring to the arterial wall to ensure the vessel remains patent. Conclude the video by focusing on microvascular details as the catheter is carefully withdrawn, with the stent securely left in place. Throughout the video, apply clear and scientifically authentic narration providing comprehensive insights into each step of coronary angioplasty.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Accurate depiction of coronary artery wall layers and plaque morphology: clear delineation of the intima, media, and adventitia boundaries; plaque composition and distribution, degree of stenosis, and luminal deformation consistent with pathological characteristics.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Correct procedural steps and mechanical responses of devices: guidewire navigates intraluminally; catheter advancement is smooth; balloon uniformly inflates at nominal size to compress the plaque; metallic stent expands synchronously with full wall apposition and anchoring.
rubrics_scientific.d3: The hemodynamics and contrast agent kinetics conform to physiological and physical principles: flow velocity and pressure gradient changes in the stenotic region are appropriate; contrast agent filling, mixing, and washout trajectories are realistic; post-dilation blood flow restoration and vortex distribution are reasonable.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Scale and Temporal Scale Consistency: Accurate proportionality among vessel diameter, lesion length, and device dimensions; the temporal sequence of balloon inflation/deflation, stent deployment, and contrast agent passage corresponds to the coronary pulsation rhythm.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Imaging appearance consistent with material properties: metallic structures exhibit high radiopacity, contrast agents enhance intraluminal contrast, and soft tissue contrast is limited; the overall visual style remains consistent with the stated observation/imaging modality.

rubrics_visual.d1: High image clarity with discernible microstructural details; effective edge anti-aliasing with no noise, compression artifacts, or motion blur ghosting.
rubrics_visual.d2: Lighting and material rendering appear natural; the volumetric perception of transparent and translucent tissues is credible; specular highlights and reflections on metallic supports are well controlled; depth of field and exposure remain stable without flickering.
rubrics_visual.d3: Color and contrast are unified and harmonized; hues of blood and contrast agent are distinctly differentiated, with adequate preservation of luminance gradation, avoiding oversaturation and color bias.
rubrics_visual.d4: Animation smoothness and physical coherence; natural rhythm in guidewire and catheter advancement, balloon inflation and deflation, stent expansion, and blood flow restoration without jittering or stalling, with consistent fluid-structure interaction.
rubrics_visual.d5: High-quality 3D modeling and topology; hierarchical arterial wall layers and plaque morphology are coherent; stent mesh is orderly without interpenetration; deformation exhibits no tensile tearing or penetration defects.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Complete demonstration of the core procedural sequence of coronary artery angioplasty (guidewire navigation → catheter advancement → balloon positioning and gradual inflation → stent deployment and anchoring → restoration of blood flow and catheter withdrawal completion)
rubrics_instruction.d2: Accurately represents key elements and dynamic interactions (clear delineation of transparent complex plaques and arterial wall layers; enhanced contrast between occlusion and patency due to contrast agent flow; synchronous expansion of balloon and metallic mesh stent with effective plaque compression).
rubrics_instruction.d3: Meets the requirements of a microscopic perspective and image texture quality (high-definition details, bright environment, realistic textures, with instrument and anatomical structure proportions and positions accurately represented).
rubrics_instruction.d4: Meets duration and shot organization requirements (shots are continuous and stable, perspectives are coherent, key moments are fully displayed, and the chronological sequence of initiation—inflation—expansion—recovery is clearly presented).
rubrics_instruction.d5: Maintain scientific accuracy and educational clarity (the narration/subtitles provide clear and rigorous explanations of the purpose and mechanisms of each step, without misleading information or scientific errors).

任务: Under a high-definition 3D microscopic perspective, depict the abnormal proliferation of breast cancer cells within breast tissue, meticulously showcasing cellular structural abnormalities and their microscopic interactions with the surrounding stroma. Locally, display changes in the cellular layers corresponding to skin dimpling or nipple discharge. The presentation should be medically accurate, richly detailed, and suitable for a 5-second short video.
具体要求: Create a visually striking and information-rich microscopic world video focusing on the detailed anatomy and symptoms of breast cancer. The scene should present a complex and realistic 3D medical animation journey through breast tissue, highlighting various breast cancer symptoms such as changes in breast size or shape, nipple retraction, dimpling or puckering of the breast or nipple skin, and any abnormal nipple discharge. Emphasize extreme precision in rendering textures and fine details, including the cellular architecture of breast tissue, the complexity of skin dimpling, and subtle variations in secretions, delivering a high-definition, immersive experience designed to educate and engage viewers on these critical aspects of breast health.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The microscopic anatomical structures and hierarchical relationships between breast tissue and tumor are accurately depicted, including clear boundaries among ducts/acini, epithelium-basement membrane-stroma, and appropriate spatial relationships of blood vessels and lymphatics; the morphological abnormalities of cancer cells (nuclear atypia, pleomorphism, altered nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio) conform to pathological characteristics.
rubrics_scientific.d2: The process of tumor cell proliferation and invasion conforms to pathophysiological principles, characterized by abnormal mitosis, disruption of the basement membrane barrier, and infiltration into the stroma; the interaction mechanisms involving adhesion to the extracellular matrix and microenvironmental cells, matrix degradation, and fiber remodeling are appropriately regulated.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Scale and temporal consistency are accurate, with scientifically valid size proportions among cells, nuclei, duct lumens, basal layer, and collagen fibers; the dynamic rates and sequences within the 5-second short video do not contradict reality (e.g., secretion flow/diffusion, cell deformation and migration, as well as the velocity and amplitude of fiber deformation).
rubrics_scientific.d4: The causal relationship between macroscopic symptoms and microscopic pathology is clearly defined. Skin dimpling and nipple retraction result from mechanical effects of ductal or stromal fibrous traction and fibrosis-induced contraction. Abnormal nipple discharge originates along the ductal pathway, with its viscosity and granular characteristics reflecting the composition and source.
rubrics_scientific.d5: The presentation of physical chemistry and fluid dynamics is scientific, with molecular Brownian motion and diffusion, matrix viscoelastic damping, and intratissue microflow direction consistent with pressure gradients; optical and color mapping do not introduce non-biological halos or exaggerated effects to avoid misleading pathological interpretation.

任务: From a microscopic perspective, the sinoatrial node emits bright electrical impulses that propagate synchronously along the atrial cardiomyocytes, meticulously showcasing the ultrastructure of the myocardial cells and the flickering intercalated discs, highlighting the dynamic microscopic phenomenon of synchronized conduction waves flowing between cells.
具体要求: Create a stunning and scientifically accurate microscopic world video that deeply explores the cardiac conduction system driving the heartbeat within the human heart. The central theme of this awe-inspiring scene is the complex network of the cardiac conduction system, with emphasis on the sinoatrial node (SA node), atrioventricular node (AV node), and the branching pathways that generate and propagate electrical impulses essential for a healthy heartbeat. This scene captures the dynamic process of electrical impulses originating from the sinoatrial node in the right atrium, smoothly spreading through the atria and ventricles, resulting in coordinated contractions that deliver oxygen-rich blood to the body. Emphasize the captivating details and textures visible at the microscopic level, including the intricate ultrastructure of cardiomyocytes, the flickering intercalated discs connecting them, and the synchronous conduction waves propagating through the myocardial tissue. Explore the complex details of fibrillation and tachycardia, where irregular or excessively rapid impulses in certain regions disrupt the normal rhythm, causing the atria or ventricles to visually fibrillate in an engaging manner. Highlight with stunning clarity the life-saving medical interventions, showcasing the precise actions of catheter ablation or the rhythm correction by implantable devices such as pacemakers and defibrillators, as they restore normal cardiac rhythm by reestablishing synchronized flickering of cellular activity.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The cardiac conduction pathway and timing are accurately represented: the impulse originates from the sinoatrial node, travels through the atria to the atrioventricular node where a physiological delay occurs, then rapidly activates the ventricles via the His bundle, left and right bundle branches, and the Purkinje network, demonstrating consistency in both macroscopic and microscopic timing.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Accurate depiction of the microscopic structure and functional characteristics of cardiomyocytes and intercalated discs: correct orientation of sarcomeric striations, continuous positioning of intercalated discs between cells, clear roles of gap junctions mediating electrical coupling, and desmosomes along with fascia adherens providing mechanical coupling.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Reasonable scale and conduction dynamics: the proportions between cellular and tissue sizes are accurate; longitudinal conduction is faster than transverse conduction; atrioventricular node conduction is the slowest, Purkinje fiber conduction the fastest; wavefront velocity and critical delays are within comparable temporal scales.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Electrophysiological characteristics of abnormal rhythms are presented scientifically: fibrillation exhibits multiple spiral waves or multifocal desynchronized wavefronts, while tachycardia manifests continuous rapid conduction of reentrant or triggered activity, consistent with mechanical dyssynchrony.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Mechanism and efficacy correspondence of medical interventions: Catheter ablation delivers energy to target lesions or pathways, creating conduction block or rerouting; pacemakers restore timing by programmed stimulation; defibrillators terminate chaotic activity with high-energy shocks and reestablish sinus rhythm.

rubrics_visual.d1: Image clarity and microscopic detail presentation are refined; myocardial fiber texture and intercalated disc structures are distinctly identifiable, with clean, non-blurred edges.
rubrics_visual.d2: High animation fluidity with clear readability of synchronous conduction waves; electrical pulse brightness and diffusion rhythm are smooth, coherent, and free of abrupt transitions.
rubrics_visual.d3: Color reproduction and contrast control are natural, with clearly defined tissue tones and luminescent signal layers, avoiding oversaturation or overexposure.
rubrics_visual.d4: 3D modeling and material texture are realistic, with microstructural proportions well balanced, surface details consistent, and material responses credible.
rubrics_visual.d5: The rendering of light and depth of field appears natural, with appropriate subsurface scattering and translucency effects. The focal point emphasizes the subject without glare.

rubrics_instruction.d1: Fully implement the core conduction pathway and main scenario outlined in the task description: the initiation of impulses from the pacemaker source (e.g., sinoatrial node), transmission through relay nodes (e.g., atrioventricular node), and diffusion along branching pathways to the target chambers, thereby establishing a causal chain that coordinates contraction.
rubrics_instruction.d2: Accurately present key anatomical and microscopic structural elements (such as conduction nodes, bundle branches, myocardial cell microstructures, and intercellular junction interfaces/intercalated discs) with scientific precision.
rubrics_instruction.d3: Clearly demonstrate the spatiotemporal propagation and coupling of synchronous conduction waves across multiple scales from cells to tissues, emphasizing coherent visualization of changes in synchrony, directionality, and rhythmicity.
rubrics_instruction.d4: Comprehensive coverage of the specified arrhythmic events and intervention segments: demonstrating the disruption of propagation patterns caused by abnormal rhythms (e.g., fibrillation, tachycardia) and illustrating the process and outcome of medical interventions (e.g., catheter ablation, pacemaker/defibrillator) in restoring synchrony.
rubrics_instruction.d5: Meeting viewpoint and technical specification requirements: maintaining a microscopic perspective with high visibility; duration encompassing key phases including normal, abnormal, intervention, and recovery; consistent scale between shots and annotations; stable imagery; and smooth transitions.

任务: Under the microscope, within the porous microenvironment of the bone marrow, a single stem cell divides and differentiates into various blood cells, with clear and distinct cell membrane and nucleus structures, rich in color. In one corner of the frame, morphologically abnormal cancer cells are present, highlighting the complex architecture and cellular diversity of the bone marrow.
具体要求: Create an engaging video scene focusing on a detailed and scientifically accurate microscopic observation of bone marrow. Bone marrow is essential for hematopoiesis and serves as the primary site of pathology for hematologic malignancies such as leukemia, lymphoma, and multiple myeloma. Within this intricate environment, depict the dynamic process of blood cell formation, illustrating stem cell division and differentiation into various blood cell types, while also showing cancer cells attempting uncontrolled proliferation. Ensure these depictions feature colorful textures and rich hues, capturing the unique porosity of the bone marrow, the diverse forms and sizes of blood cells, and the abnormal morphology of cancer cells. Emphasize precise cellular structures, particularly highlighting the cell membrane, nucleus, and prominent mutations within cancer cells, aiming to provide a comprehensive yet captivating visual representation of the complex bone marrow microenvironment, offering profound insights into the cellular-level battles occurring in hematologic cancers.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The porous microenvironment of the bone marrow and the structure of the sinusoids–trabeculae align with histological characteristics, exhibiting appropriate proportions of extracellular matrix, stromal cells, and vascular spaces.
rubrics_scientific.d2: The mode of division and differentiation sequence of hematopoietic stem cells conform to the physiological principles of hematopoiesis, with a temporal scale consistent and coherent with lineage transitions.
rubrics_scientific.d3: The morphology, size scale, and nuclear status of various blood cell types are accurately presented, and the population composition and density distribution fall within the physiological range of bone marrow.
rubrics_scientific.d4: The morphological abnormalities, proliferative dynamics, and microenvironmental interactions of the cancer cells are consistent with hematologic tumor biology and do not overlap with normal differentiation trajectories.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Scale and Physical Consistency: The resolution of microscopic imaging corresponds to the level of detail, and cellular motion as well as local fluid dynamics comply with microscale physical principles.

rubrics_visual.d4: Color rendering is natural with distinct gradations; saturation and contrast are moderate. The colors and brightness levels of different cell types and abnormal cells are clearly differentiated, without obvious color bias or color bleeding.
rubrics_visual.d5: Visual distortions and artifacts are prominent, with noise, compression artifacts, aliasing, overexposure, or excessive special effects obscuring structural information; subtitles or watermarks intrude into critical regions of interest.

rubrics_instruction.d5: Compliance with technical specifications and presentation requirements for microscopic observation: appropriate microscopic perspective and magnification, stable image composition, color and texture richness that do not obscure scientific information, and duration and pacing that meet task demands.

任务: Microscopic perspective of the arterial intima, depicting a leukocyte penetrating the endothelium and differentiating into a macrophage, engulfing cholesterol to form foam cells. The cellular structures and textures are delicate, with lipid accumulation in the vessel wall, dynamic fluid flow, and vivid colors, illustrating the early microscopic changes of atherosclerosis.
具体要求: Create a breathtaking microscopic world video that deeply explores the complex process of atherosclerosis, vividly capturing the evolution of coronary artery occlusion caused by vascular blockage characteristic of atherosclerosis induced by chronic inflammation, leading to heart disease. The scene should meticulously depict damage to the arterial intima, focusing on a leukocyte penetrating the endothelium and differentiating into a macrophage responsible for engulfing and digesting cholesterol. This cellular transformation progresses into foam cells that accumulate within the vessel wall and promote plaque formation. Over several years, observe the intense buildup of plaques leading to rupture, releasing fragments into the bloodstream that cause occlusion in the left anterior descending artery (LAD), which supplies the left ventricle. The video culminates with an external view of the heart, highlighting the stark contrast of necrotic tissue on the ventricular wall. Emphasize the texture and detail of cellular structures, showcasing the layered arterial wall, lipid-rich foam cells, and fluid dynamics within the LAD occlusion. Enhance viewers’ understanding of these microscopic events with vivid colors and surreal textures, ensuring a stunning visual experience.

rubrics_scientific.d1: The hierarchical structure of the arterial wall and the proportion of cell morphology/size are accurately represented, with the relative thicknesses and spatial relationships of the endothelium, internal elastic lamina, media, and adventitia appropriately maintained.
rubrics_scientific.d2: The mechanism and sequence of inflammation-driven monocyte adhesion, transendothelial migration, differentiation into macrophages, and uptake of cholesterol to form foam cells are consistent with physiological processes.
rubrics_scientific.d3: The causal sequence between plaque progression and pathological outcomes is scientifically accurate: lipid deposition—fibrous cap formation—unstable plaque rupture—thrombus formation—coronary artery occlusion—myocardial necrosis in the corresponding perfusion territory.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Hemodynamic and fluid-structure interaction behaviors are consistent and reasonable (the distribution of shear stress, vortices, and changes in flow velocity and pressure gradient at the stenotic site correspond directionally and quantitatively with their effects on the endothelium and plaque).
rubrics_scientific.d5: Scale and temporal mapping consistency (proportional coordination from micrometer-level cellular and tissue scales to organ-level heart scale; temporal sequencing of chronic progression and acute events aligning with biological processes)

rubrics_visual.d3: Color saturation and contrast are natural, with uniform tone and distinct gradations; highlights and shadows are properly controlled to enhance element recognition.

rubrics_instruction.d22: Adherence to the perspective setting: Primarily adopts a microscopic viewpoint of the arterial intimal surface, clearly demonstrating the stratification of the arterial wall; the ending transitions to an external cardiac viewpoint corresponding with the narrative climax.
rubrics_instruction.d33: Completeness of key elements and details: leukocytes/macrophages/foam cells, cholesterol and lipid accumulation, plaque and debris in the bloodstream, fluid dynamics of obstruction within the LAD; cellular structure exhibiting fine texture, vivid coloration, and hyperrealistic textures to enhance readability.
rubrics_instruction.d44: Duration and Temporal Sequence Compliance: Encompasses a multi-year progression timeframe, with clearly defined stage advancement and smooth scene transitions, culminating in a distinct climax at the sites of coronary artery occlusion and myocardial necrosis.

任务: Microscopic close-up of the basal thumb joint cartilage: a dynamic display of an active yet fragile network of chondrocytes on the surface of degenerated cartilage, with synovial fluid reflecting rich colors. Miniature surgical instruments elegantly touch the cartilage surface, highlighting the delicate textures and color contrasts of cartilage degeneration. The scene is clean and focused, suitable for a 5-second dynamic presentation.
具体要求: Create a stunning microscopic world video that finely captures the essence of thumb osteoarthritis, focusing on the detailed dynamics of basal joint cartilage wear. The main content features close-ups of chondrocytes and fibrous tissue within the thumb joint cartilage, showcasing an active yet fragile chondrocyte network and the smooth, degenerated cartilage surface characteristic of this degenerative pathology. The scene unfolds within the joint cavity, where cartilage erosion due to osteoarthritis leads to bone-on-bone friction, causing inflammation and pain. The video captures critical moments of a surgeon performing a trapeziectomy accompanied by ligament reconstruction and tendon insertion. It highlights the surgeon’s precise and graceful maneuvers, such as excising the diseased trapezium bone and meticulously weaving and inserting tendons from adjacent muscles into the prepared space, forming a synthetic cushion that mimics cartilage to isolate painful bone contact. Details and textures are paramount, with the elastic flexibility of tendon fibers sharply contrasting the porous and fragile appearance of eroded cartilage. The shimmering synovial fluid should reflect light, creating an ethereal atmosphere that accentuates every intricate detail inside the joint. Visual presentation of any microscopic surgical tools interacting elegantly with biological structures is included. Emphasize rich color variations and vivid textural contrasts to simulate the tactile quality seen under the microscope, rendering a realistic depiction that immerses viewers in this complex, lifelike surgical intervention aimed at improving thumb osteoarthritis.

rubrics_scientific.d1: Consistency of Joint Anatomy and Scale: Accurate spatial relationships among osseous structures, articular cartilage, synovium, and ligaments/tendons within the thumb basal joint; proportional consistency in size and magnification between cells, collagen fibers, extracellular matrix, and micro-instruments.
rubrics_scientific.d2: Histological Accuracy of Chondrocytes and Matrix: The morphology of chondrocytes, their distribution within lacunae, and their relationship with the surrounding proteoglycan/collagen network conform to articular cartilage histology; degenerative phenotypes (such as decreased surface smoothness, fibrotic streaks, fissures and thinning, and frayed edges) are presented scientifically.
rubrics_scientific.d3: Pathophysiological and biomechanical dynamic plausibility: the transition from cartilage wear to bone-to-bone contact, synovial fluid viscoelastic lubrication and lubrication film rupture, the magnitude and time constants of local inflammatory exudation and tissue strain/rebound corresponding to microscale phenomena; particulate and fluid motion governed by low Reynolds number, viscosity-dominated physical laws.
rubrics_scientific.d4: Scientific Validity of Surgical Interaction and Reconstruction Mechanisms: The cutting, traction, and compressive deformations generated by instrument-tissue contact and their propagation pathways are consistent with tissue mechanics; the location, morphology, and fixation methods of resected pathological tissue and reconstructed/interposed materials reasonably restore or substitute the cushioning gap function, while avoiding anatomical conflicts with critical neurovascular structures and ligaments.
rubrics_scientific.d5: Penalty for Critical Scientific Errors: Negative points assigned for occurrences such as anatomical or scale confusion (interchanging structural nomenclature and positional attributes, erratic switching between cellular and tissue scales), neglecting imaging and optical principles (spontaneous cellular luminescence under label-free conditions, mismatches in reflection/refraction and media properties), or representing gradual cellular/matrix changes as abrupt instantaneous transitions.
